39. Agent @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Find logging pattern in policy package",
  "prompt": "I need to understand how logging is done in the Cilium policy package. Please search for:\n1. Where the `log` variable is defined that's used throughout pkg/policy/*.go files\n2. Look for any build initialization or init() functions that set up logging\n3. Check if there's a pattern like `log = logging.DefaultLogger.WithField()` or similar\n\nSearch in:\n- pkg/policy/*.go files for log variable definitions\n- pkg/logging/*.go for any exported loggers\n- Any init() functions that setup logging\n\nReturn the specific lines and files where logging is initialized.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "ac3990d41eb3673f6",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have all the information. Let me provide a comprehensive report.\n\n## Logging Setup in Cilium Policy Package\n\nBased on my analysis of the Cilium codebase, here's how logging is configured in the policy package:\n\n### 1. **Primary Log Variable Definition**\n\n**File:** `/workspace/pkg/policy/config.go` (Lines 16-20)\n\n```go\nvar (\n\tlog          = logging.DefaultLogger.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, \"policy\")\n\tmutex        lock.RWMutex // Protects enablePolicy\n\tenablePolicy string       // Whether policy enforcement is enabled.\n)\n```\n\nThis is the **only place** where the `log` variable used throughout all `pkg/policy/*.go` files is defined. It's a package-level variable that wraps the `DefaultLogger` with a subsystem field.\n\n### 2. **Base Logger Implementation**\n\n**File:** `/workspace/pkg/logging/logging.go` (Lines 50-52)\n\n```go\n// DefaultLogger is the base logrus logger. It is different from the logrus\n// default to avoid external dependencies from writing out unexpectedly\nvar DefaultLogger = initializeDefaultLogger()\n```\n\nThe `DefaultLogger` is initialized at module load time via the `initializeDefaultLogger()` function (lines 84-91):\n\n```go\nfunc initializeDefaultLogger() (logger *logrus.Logger) {\n\tlogger = logrus.New()\n\tlogger.SetFormatter(GetFormatter(DefaultLogFormatTimestamp))\n\tlogger.SetLevel(DefaultLogLevel)\n\treturn\n}\n```\n\n### 3. **Key Logging Constants**\n\n**File:** `/workspace/pkg/logging/logfields/logfields.go` (Line 11-12)\n\n```go\nconst (\n\t// LogSubsys is the field denoting the subsystem when logging\n\tLogSubsys = \"subsys\"\n\t// ... other fields ...\n)\n```\n\n### 4. **Usage Pattern in Policy Package**\n\nThe log variable is used throughout policy files in several patterns:\n\n**Simple logging (Line 777 in `/workspace/pkg/policy/l4.go`):**\n```go\nlog.WithError(err).Warningf(\"policy: Error getting %s TLS Context.\", direction)\n```\n\n**Conditional enhanced logging (Lines 555-562 in `/workspace/pkg/policy/l4.go`):**\n```go\nlogger := log\nif option.Config.Debug {\n\tlogger = log.WithFields(logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.Port:             port,\n\t\tlogfields.PortName:         l4.PortName,\n\t\tlogfields.Protocol:         proto,\n\t\tlogfields.TrafficDirection: direction,\n\t})\n}\n```\n\n### 5. **Init Functions**\n\n**Only found in tests:**\n- `/workspace/pkg/policy/l4_filter_test.go:137` - This initializes a regex LRU cache for testing, not logging-related:\n\n```go\nfunc init() {\n\tre.InitRegexCompileLRU(defaults.FQDNRegexCompileLRUSize)\n}\n```\n\nThere is **no logging-specific init() function** in the policy package files.\n\n### 6. **Complete Initialization Flow**\n\n1. `pkg/logging/logging.go` module is imported\n2. `DefaultLogger = initializeDefaultLogger()` runs at module init time (creates new logrus.Logger)\n3. `pkg/policy/config.go` is imported\n4. `log = logging.DefaultLogger.WithField(...)` creates the policy subsystem logger\n5. The `log` variable is then available to all files in the `policy` package (they share the same package scope)\n\n### Summary\n\n- **Log variable defined in:** `/workspace/pkg/policy/config.go:17`\n- **Pattern:** `log = logging.DefaultLogger.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, \"policy\")`\n- **Base logger setup:** `/workspace/pkg/logging/logging.go:52` via `initializeDefaultLogger()`\n- **No package-level init() functions** for logging in the policy package (only in tests)\n- **All 37 .go files** in `pkg/policy/` can access the shared `log` variable due to package-level scope",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "I need to understand how logging is done in the Cilium policy package. Please search for:\n1. Where the `log` variable is defined that's used throughout pkg/policy/*.go files\n2. Look for any build initialization or init() functions that set up logging\n3. Check if there's a pattern like `log = logging.DefaultLogger.WithField()` or similar\n\nSearch in:\n- pkg/policy/*.go files for log variable definitions\n- pkg/logging/*.go for any exported loggers\n- Any init() functions that setup logging\n\nReturn the specific lines and files where logging is initialized.",

"     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package logging\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"bufio\"\n     8\u2192\t\"bytes\"\n     9\u2192\t\"flag\"\n    10\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    11\u2192\t\"os\"\n    12\u2192\t\"regexp\"\n    13\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    14\u2192\t\"sync/atomic\"\n    15\u2192\t\"time\"\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n    18\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\"\n    21\u2192)\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192type LogFormat string\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192const (\n    26\u2192\tSyslog    = \"syslog\"\n    27\u2192\tLevelOpt  = \"level\"\n    28\u2192\tFormatOpt = \"format\"\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192\tLogFormatText          LogFormat = \"text\"\n    31\u2192\tLogFormatTextTimestamp LogFormat = \"text-ts\"\n    32\u2192\tLogFormatJSON          LogFormat = \"json\"\n    33\u2192\tLogFormatJSONTimestamp LogFormat = \"json-ts\"\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192\t// DefaultLogFormat is the string representation of the default logrus.Formatter\n    36\u2192\t// we want to use (possible values: text or json)\n    37\u2192\tDefaultLogFormat LogFormat = LogFormatText\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192\t// DefaultLogFormatTimestamp is the string representation of the default logrus.Formatter\n    40\u2192\t// including timestamps.\n    41\u2192\t// We don't use this for general runtime logs since kubernetes log capture handles those.\n    42\u2192\t// This is only used for applications such as CNI which is written to disk so we have no\n    43\u2192\t// way to correlate with other logs.\n    44\u2192\tDefaultLogFormatTimestamp LogFormat = LogFormatTextTimestamp\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192\t// DefaultLogLevel is the default log level we want to use for our logrus.Formatter\n    47\u2192\tDefaultLogLevel logrus.Level = logrus.InfoLevel\n    48\u2192)\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192// DefaultLogger is the base logrus logger. It is different from the logrus\n    51\u2192// default to avoid external dependencies from writing out unexpectedly\n    52\u2192var DefaultLogger = initializeDefaultLogger()\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192func initializeKLog() {\n    55\u2192\tlog := DefaultLogger.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, \"klog\")\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192\t//Create a new flag set and set error handler\n    58\u2192\tklogFlags := flag.NewFlagSet(\"cilium\", flag.ExitOnError)\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192\t// Make sure that klog logging variables are initialized so that we can\n    61\u2192\t// update them from this file.\n    62\u2192\tklog.InitFlags(klogFlags)\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192\t// Make sure klog does not log to stderr as we want it to control the output\n    65\u2192\t// of klog so we want klog to log the errors to each writer of each level.\n    66\u2192\tklogFlags.Set(\"logtostderr\", \"false\")\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192\t// We don't need all headers because logrus will already print them if\n    69\u2192\t// necessary.\n    70\u2192\tklogFlags.Set(\"skip_headers\", \"true\")\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192\tklog.SetOutputBySeverity(\"INFO\", log.WriterLevel(logrus.InfoLevel))\n    73\u2192\tklog.SetOutputBySeverity(\"WARNING\", log.WriterLevel(logrus.WarnLevel))\n    74\u2192\tklog.SetOutputBySeverity(\"ERROR\", log.WriterLevel(logrus.ErrorLevel))\n    75\u2192\tklog.SetOutputBySeverity(\"FATAL\", log.WriterLevel(logrus.FatalLevel))\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192\t// Do not repeat log messages on all severities in klog\n    78\u2192\tklogFlags.Set(\"one_output\", \"true\")\n    79\u2192}\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192// LogOptions maps configuration key-value pairs related to logging.\n    82\u2192type LogOptions map[string]string\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192// initializeDefaultLogger returns a logrus Logger with the default logging\n    85\u2192// settings.\n    86\u2192func initializeDefaultLogger() (logger *logrus.Logger) {\n    87\u2192\tlogger = logrus.New()\n    88\u2192\tlogger.SetFormatter(GetFormatter(DefaultLogFormatTimestamp))\n    89\u2192\tlogger.SetLevel(DefaultLogLevel)\n    90\u2192\treturn\n    91\u2192}\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192// GetLogLevel returns the log level specified in the provided LogOptions. If\n    94\u2192// it is not set in the options, it will return the default level.\n    95\u2192func (o LogOptions) GetLogLevel() (level logrus.Level) {\n    96\u2192\tlevelOpt, ok := o[LevelOpt]\n    97\u2192\tif !ok {\n    98\u2192\t\treturn DefaultLogLevel\n    99\u2192\t}\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192\tvar err error\n   102\u2192\tif level, err = logrus.ParseLevel(levelOpt); err != nil {\n   103\u2192\t\tlogrus.WithError(err).Warning(\"Ignoring user-configured log level\")\n   104\u2192\t\treturn DefaultLogLevel\n   105\u2192\t}\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192\treturn\n   108\u2192}\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192// GetLogFormat returns the log format specified in the provided LogOptions. If\n   111\u2192// it is not set in the options or is invalid, it will return the default format.\n   112\u2192func (o LogOptions) GetLogFormat() LogFormat {\n   113\u2192\tformatOpt, ok := o[FormatOpt]\n   114\u2192\tif !ok {\n   115\u2192\t\treturn DefaultLogFormatTimestamp\n   116\u2192\t}\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192\tformatOpt = strings.ToLower(formatOpt)\n   119\u2192\tre := regexp.MustCompile(`^(text|text-ts|json|json-ts)$`)\n   120\u2192\tif !re.MatchString(formatOpt) {\n   121\u2192\t\tlogrus.WithError(\n   122\u2192\t\t\tfmt.Errorf(\"incorrect log format configured '%s', expected 'text', 'text-ts', 'json' or 'json-ts'\", formatOpt),\n   123\u2192\t\t).Warning(\"Ignoring user-configured log format\")\n   124\u2192\t\treturn DefaultLogFormatTimestamp\n   125\u2192\t}\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192\treturn LogFormat(formatOpt)\n   128\u2192}\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192// SetLogLevel updates the DefaultLogger with a new logrus.Level\n   131\u2192func SetLogLevel(logLevel logrus.Level) {\n   132\u2192\tDefaultLogger.SetLevel(logLevel)\n   133\u2192}\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192// SetDefaultLogLevel updates the DefaultLogger with the DefaultLogLevel\n   136\u2192func SetDefaultLogLevel() {\n   137\u2192\tDefaultLogger.SetLevel(DefaultLogLevel)\n   138\u2192}\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192// SetLogLevelToDebug updates the DefaultLogger with the logrus.DebugLevel\n   141\u2192func SetLogLevelToDebug() {\n   142\u2192\tDefaultLogger.SetLevel(logrus.DebugLevel)\n   143\u2192}\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192// SetLogFormat updates the DefaultLogger with a new LogFormat\n   146\u2192func SetLogFormat(logFormat LogFormat) {\n   147\u2192\tDefaultLogger.SetFormatter(GetFormatter(logFormat))\n   148\u2192}\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192// SetDefaultLogFormat updates the DefaultLogger with the DefaultLogFormat\n   151\u2192func SetDefaultLogFormat() {\n   152\u2192\tDefaultLogger.SetFormatter(GetFormatter(DefaultLogFormatTimestamp))\n   153\u2192}\n   154\u2192\n   155\u2192// AddHooks adds additional logrus hook to default logger\n   156\u2192func AddHooks(hooks ...logrus.Hook) {\n   157\u2192\tfor _, hook := range hooks {\n   158\u2192\t\tDefaultLogger.AddHook(hook)\n   159\u2192\t}\n   160\u2192}\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192// SetupLogging sets up each logging service provided in loggers and configures\n   163\u2192// each logger with the provided logOpts.\n   164\u2192func SetupLogging(loggers []string, logOpts LogOptions, tag string, debug bool) error {\n   165\u2192\t// Bridge klog to logrus. Note that this will open multiple pipes and fork\n   166\u2192\t// background goroutines that are not cleaned up.\n   167\u2192\tinitializeKLog()\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192\tif debug {\n   170\u2192\t\tlogOpts[LevelOpt] = \"debug\"\n   171\u2192\t}\n   172\u2192\tinitializeSlog(logOpts, len(loggers) == 0)\n   173\u2192\n   174\u2192\t// Updating the default log format\n   175\u2192\tSetLogFormat(logOpts.GetLogFormat())\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192\t// Set default logger to output to stdout if no loggers are provided.\n   178\u2192\tif len(loggers) == 0 {\n   179\u2192\t\t// TODO: switch to a per-logger version when we upgrade to logrus >1.0.3\n   180\u2192\t\tlogrus.SetOutput(os.Stdout)\n   181\u2192\t}\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192\t// Updating the default log level, overriding the log options if the debug arg is being set\n   184\u2192\tif debug {\n   185\u2192\t\tSetLogLevelToDebug()\n   186\u2192\t} else {\n   187\u2192\t\tSetLogLevel(logOpts.GetLogLevel())\n   188\u2192\t}\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192\t// always suppress the default logger so libraries don't print things\n   191\u2192\tlogrus.SetLevel(logrus.PanicLevel)\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192\t// Iterate through all provided loggers and configure them according\n   194\u2192\t// to user-provided settings.\n   195\u2192\tfor _, logger := range loggers {\n   196\u2192\t\tswitch logger {\n   197\u2192\t\tcase Syslog:\n   198\u2192\t\t\tif err := setupSyslog(logOpts, tag, debug); err != nil {\n   199\u2192\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to set up syslog: %w\", err)\n   200\u2192\t\t\t}\n   201\u2192\t\tdefault:\n   202\u2192\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"provided log driver %q is not a supported log driver\", logger)\n   203\u2192\t\t}\n   204\u2192\t}\n   205\u2192\n   206\u2192\treturn nil\n   207\u2192}\n   208\u2192\n   209\u2192// GetFormatter returns a configured logrus.Formatter with some specific values\n   210\u2192// we want to have\n   211\u2192func GetFormatter(format LogFormat) logrus.Formatter {\n   212\u2192\tswitch format {\n   213\u2192\tcase LogFormatText:\n   214\u2192\t\treturn &logrus.TextFormatter{\n   215\u2192\t\t\tDisableTimestamp: true,\n   216\u2192\t\t\tDisableColors:    true,\n   217\u2192\t\t}\n   218\u2192\tcase LogFormatTextTimestamp:\n   219\u2192\t\treturn &logrus.TextFormatter{\n   220\u2192\t\t\tDisableTimestamp: false,\n   221\u2192\t\t\tDisableColors:    true,\n   222\u2192\t\t}\n   223\u2192\tcase LogFormatJSON:\n   224\u2192\t\treturn &logrus.JSONFormatter{\n   225\u2192\t\t\tDisableTimestamp: true,\n   226\u2192\t\t}\n   227\u2192\tcase LogFormatJSONTimestamp:\n   228\u2192\t\treturn &logrus.JSONFormatter{\n   229\u2192\t\t\tDisableTimestamp: false,\n   230\u2192\t\t\tTimestampFormat:  time.RFC3339Nano,\n   231\u2192\t\t}\n   232\u2192\t}\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192\treturn nil\n   235\u2192}\n   236\u2192\n   237\u2192// validateOpts iterates through all of the keys and values in logOpts, and errors out if\n   238\u2192// the key in logOpts is not a key in supportedOpts, or the value of corresponding key is\n   239\u2192// not listed in the value of validKVs.\n   240\u2192func (o LogOptions) validateOpts(logDriver string, supportedOpts map[string]bool, validKVs map[string][]string) error {\n   241\u2192\tfor k, v := range o {\n   242\u2192\t\tif !supportedOpts[k] {\n   243\u2192\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"provided configuration key %q is not supported as a logging option for log driver %s\", k, logDriver)\n   244\u2192\t\t}\n   245\u2192\t\tif validValues, ok := validKVs[k]; ok {\n   246\u2192\t\t\tvalid := false\n   247\u2192\t\t\tfor _, vv := range validValues {\n   248\u2192\t\t\t\tif v == vv {\n   249\u2192\t\t\t\t\tvalid = true\n   250\u2192\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n   251\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   252\u2192\t\t\t}\n   253\u2192\t\t\tif !valid {\n   254\u2192\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"provided configuration value %q is not a valid value for %q in log driver %s, valid values: %v\", v, k, logDriver, validValues)\n   255\u2192\t\t\t}\n   256\u2192\n   257\u2192\t\t}\n   258\u2192\t}\n   259\u2192\treturn nil\n   260\u2192}\n   261\u2192\n   262\u2192// getLogDriverConfig returns a map containing the key-value pairs that start\n   263\u2192// with string logDriver from map logOpts.\n   264\u2192func getLogDriverConfig(logDriver string, logOpts LogOptions) LogOptions {\n   265\u2192\tkeysToValidate := make(LogOptions)\n   266\u2192\tfor k, v := range logOpts {\n   267\u2192\t\tok, err := regexp.MatchString(logDriver+\".*\", k)\n   268\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   269\u2192\t\t\tDefaultLogger.Fatal(err)\n   270\u2192\t\t}\n   271\u2192\t\tif ok {\n   272\u2192\t\t\tkeysToValidate[k] = v\n   273\u2192\t\t}\n   274\u2192\t}\n   275\u2192\treturn keysToValidate\n   276\u2192}\n   277\u2192\n   278\u2192// MultiLine breaks a multi line text into individual log entries and calls the\n   279\u2192// logging function to log each entry\n   280\u2192func MultiLine(logFn func(args ...interface{}), output string) {\n   281\u2192\tscanner := bufio.NewScanner(bytes.NewReader([]byte(output)))\n   282\u2192\tfor scanner.Scan() {\n   283\u2192\t\tlogFn(scanner.Text())\n   284\u2192\t}\n   285\u2192}\n   286\u2192\n   287\u2192// CanLogAt returns whether a log message at the given level would be\n   288\u2192// logged by the given logger.\n   289\u2192func CanLogAt(logger *logrus.Logger, level logrus.Level) bool {\n   290\u2192\treturn GetLevel(logger) >= level\n   291\u2192}\n   292\u2192\n   293\u2192// GetLevel returns the log level of the given logger.\n   294\u2192func GetLevel(logger *logrus.Logger) logrus.Level {\n   295\u2192\treturn logrus.Level(atomic.LoadUint32((*uint32)(&logger.Level)))\n   296\u2192}\n   297\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

Output
"     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package policy\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\tipcacheTypes \"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/ipcache/types\"\n     8\u2192\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/labels\"\n     9\u2192\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock\"\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/source\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/time\"\n    14\u2192)\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192var (\n    17\u2192\tlog          = logging.DefaultLogger.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, \"policy\")\n    18\u2192\tmutex        lock.RWMutex // Protects enablePolicy\n    19\u2192\tenablePolicy string       // Whether policy enforcement is enabled.\n    20\u2192)\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192// SetPolicyEnabled sets the policy enablement configuration. Valid values are:\n    23\u2192// - endpoint.AlwaysEnforce\n    24\u2192// - endpoint.NeverEnforce\n    25\u2192// - endpoint.DefaultEnforcement\n    26\u2192func SetPolicyEnabled(val string) {\n    27\u2192\tmutex.Lock()\n    28\u2192\tenablePolicy = val\n    29\u2192\tmutex.Unlock()\n    30\u2192}\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192// GetPolicyEnabled returns the policy enablement configuration\n    33\u2192func GetPolicyEnabled() string {\n    34\u2192\tmutex.RLock()\n    35\u2192\tval := enablePolicy\n    36\u2192\tmutex.RUnlock()\n    37\u2192\treturn val\n    38\u2192}\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192// AddOptions are options which can be passed to PolicyAdd\n    41\u2192type AddOptions struct {\n    42\u2192\t// Replace if true indicates that existing rules with identical labels should be replaced\n    43\u2192\tReplace bool\n    44\u2192\t// ReplaceWithLabels if present indicates that existing rules with the\n    45\u2192\t// given LabelArray should be deleted.\n    46\u2192\tReplaceWithLabels labels.LabelArray\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192\t// Generated should be set as true to signalize a the policy being inserted\n    49\u2192\t// was generated by cilium-agent, e.g. dns poller.\n    50\u2192\tGenerated bool\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

51. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/pkg/logging/logfields/logfields.go",
  "limit": 100
}
Output
"     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192// Package logfields defines common logging fields which are used across packages\n     5\u2192package logfields\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192const (\n     8\u2192\t// Annotations are any annotations for Pods\n     9\u2192\tAnnotations = \"annotations\"\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192\t// LogSubsys is the field denoting the subsystem when logging\n    12\u2192\tLogSubsys = \"subsys\"\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192\t// Signal is the field to print os signals on exit etc.\n    15\u2192\tSignal = \"signal\"\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192\t// Node is a host machine in the cluster, running cilium\n    18\u2192\tNode = \"node\"\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192\t// NodeID is the node-scoped ID of the node as allocated by the agent\n    21\u2192\tNodeID = \"nodeID\"\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192\t// NodeName is a human readable name for the node\n    24\u2192\tNodeName = \"nodeName\"\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192\t// Endpoint is the endpoint name (e.g. wireguard)\n    27\u2192\tEndpoint = \"endpoint\"\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192\t// EndpointID is the numeric endpoint identifier\n    30\u2192\tEndpointID = \"endpointID\"\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192\t// EndpointState is the current endpoint state\n    33\u2192\tEndpointState = \"endpointState\"\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192\t// Error is the Go error\n    36\u2192\tError = \"error\"\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192\t// EventUUID is an event unique identifier\n    39\u2192\tEventUUID = \"eventID\"\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192\t// CNIAttachmentID uniquely identifies an endpoint\n    42\u2192\tCNIAttachmentID = \"cniAttachmentID\"\n    43\u2192\n    44\u2192\t// ContainerID is the container identifier\n    45\u2192\tContainerID = \"containerID\"\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192\t// ContainerInterface is the name of the interface in the container namespace\n    48\u2192\tContainerInterface = \"containerInterface\"\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192\t// IdentityLabels are the labels relevant for the security identity\n    51\u2192\tIdentityLabels = \"identityLabels\"\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192\t// InfoLabels are the labels relevant for the security identity\n    54\u2192\tInfoLabels = \"infoLabels\"\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192\t// Labels are any label, they may not be relevant to the security identity.\n    57\u2192\tLabels = \"labels\"\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192\t// Label is a singular label, where relevant\n    60\u2192\tLabel = \"label\"\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192\t// SourceFilter is the label or node information source\n    63\u2192\tSourceFilter = \"sourceFilter\"\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192\t// Controller is the name of the controller to log it.\n    66\u2192\tController = \"controller\"\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192\t// Identity is the identifier of a security identity\n    69\u2192\tIdentity = \"identity\"\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192\t// ConflictingIdentity is the identifier of a security identity that conflicts\n    72\u2192\t// with 'Identity'\n    73\u2192\tConflictingIdentity = \"conflictingIdentity\"\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192\t// Ingress is the identifier of an ingress object\n    76\u2192\tIngress = \"ingress\"\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192\t// IngressClass is the identifier of an ingress class object\n    79\u2192\tIngressClass = \"ingressClass\"\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192\t// OldIdentity is a previously used security identity\n    82\u2192\tOldIdentity = \"oldIdentity\"\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192\t// PolicyKey is a policy map key\n    85\u2192\tPolicyKey = \"policyKey\"\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192\t// PolicyEntry is a policy map value\n    88\u2192\tPolicyEntry = \"policyEntry\"\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192\t// PolicyRevision is the revision of the policy in the repository or of\n    91\u2192\t// the object in question\n    92\u2192\tPolicyRevision = \"policyRevision\"\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192\t// PolicyKeysAdded is a set of added policy map keys\n    95\u2192\tPolicyKeysAdded = \"policyKeysAdded\"\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192\t// PolicyKeysDeleted is a set of deleted policy map keys\n    98\u2192\tPolicyKeysDeleted = \"policyKeysDeleted\"\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192\t// PolicyEntriesOld is a set of old policy map keys and values\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
52. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/pkg/policy/l4.go",
  "limit": 80
}
Output
"     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n     3\u2192\n     4\u2192package policy\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192import (\n     7\u2192\t\"bytes\"\n     8\u2192\t\"encoding/json\"\n     9\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    10\u2192\t\"math/bits\"\n    11\u2192\t\"sort\"\n    12\u2192\t\"strconv\"\n    13\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    14\u2192\t\"sync/atomic\"\n    15\u2192\t\"testing\"\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192\tcilium \"github.com/cilium/proxy/go/cilium/api\"\n    18\u2192\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/api/v1/models\"\n    21\u2192\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/container/bitlpm\"\n    22\u2192\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/iana\"\n    23\u2192\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\"\n    24\u2192\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/labels\"\n    25\u2192\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock\"\n    26\u2192\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\"\n    27\u2192\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/option\"\n    28\u2192\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/policy/api\"\n    29\u2192\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/policy/trafficdirection\"\n    30\u2192\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/u8proto\"\n    31\u2192)\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192// covers returns true if 'l4rule' has the effect needed for the 'l3l4rule', when 'l4rule' is added\n    34\u2192// to the datapath, due to the l4-only rule matching if l3l4-rule is not present. This determination\n    35\u2192// can be done here only when both rules have the same port number (or both have a wildcarded port).\n    36\u2192func (l4rule *PerSelectorPolicy) covers(l3l4rule *PerSelectorPolicy) bool {\n    37\u2192\t// Deny takes highest precedence so it is dealt with first\n    38\u2192\tif l4rule != nil && l4rule.IsDeny {\n    39\u2192\t\t// l4-only deny takes precedence\n    40\u2192\t\treturn true\n    41\u2192\t} else if l3l4rule != nil && l3l4rule.IsDeny {\n    42\u2192\t\t// Must not skip if l3l4 rule is deny while l4-only rule is not\n    43\u2192\t\treturn false\n    44\u2192\t}\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192\t// Can not skip if currentRule has an explicit auth type and wildcardRule does not or if\n    47\u2192\t// both have different auth types.  In all other cases the auth type from the wildcardRule\n    48\u2192\t// can be used also for the current rule.\n    49\u2192\t// Note that the caller must deal with inheriting redirect from wildcardRule to currentRule,\n    50\u2192\t// if any.\n    51\u2192\tcHasAuth, cAuthType := l3l4rule.GetAuthType()\n    52\u2192\twHasAuth, wAuthType := l4rule.GetAuthType()\n    53\u2192\tif cHasAuth && !wHasAuth || cHasAuth && wHasAuth && cAuthType != wAuthType {\n    54\u2192\t\treturn false\n    55\u2192\t}\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192\tl3l4IsRedirect := l3l4rule.IsRedirect()\n    58\u2192\tl4OnlyIsRedirect := l4rule.IsRedirect()\n    59\u2192\tif l3l4IsRedirect && !l4OnlyIsRedirect {\n    60\u2192\t\t// Can not skip if l3l4-rule is redirect while l4-only is not\n    61\u2192\t\treturn false\n    62\u2192\t} else if l3l4IsRedirect && l4OnlyIsRedirect &&\n    63\u2192\t\t(l3l4rule.Listener != l4rule.Listener || l3l4rule.Priority != l4rule.Priority) {\n    64\u2192\t\t// L3l4 rule has a different listener or priority, it can not be skipped\n    65\u2192\t\treturn false\n    66\u2192\t}\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192\t// else can skip\n    69\u2192\treturn true\n    70\u2192}\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192// TLS context holds the secret values resolved from an 'api.TLSContext'\n    73\u2192type TLSContext struct {\n    74\u2192\tTrustedCA        string `json:\"trustedCA,omitempty\"`\n    75\u2192\tCertificateChain string `json:\"certificateChain,omitempty\"`\n    76\u2192\tPrivateKey       string `json:\"privateKey,omitempty\"`\n    77\u2192}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192// Equal returns true if 'a' and 'b' have the same contents.\n    80\u2192func (a *TLSContext) Equal(b *TLSContext) bool {\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

55. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "head -100 /workspace/pkg/policy/l4.go | tail -50",
  "description": "Show middle section of l4.go"
}
Output
"\tcHasAuth, cAuthType := l3l4rule.GetAuthType()\n\twHasAuth, wAuthType := l4rule.GetAuthType()\n\tif cHasAuth && !wHasAuth || cHasAuth && wHasAuth && cAuthType != wAuthType {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tl3l4IsRedirect := l3l4rule.IsRedirect()\n\tl4OnlyIsRedirect := l4rule.IsRedirect()\n\tif l3l4IsRedirect && !l4OnlyIsRedirect {\n\t\t// Can not skip if l3l4-rule is redirect while l4-only is not\n\t\treturn false\n\t} else if l3l4IsRedirect && l4OnlyIsRedirect &&\n\t\t(l3l4rule.Listener != l4rule.Listener || l3l4rule.Priority != l4rule.Priority) {\n\t\t// L3l4 rule has a different listener or priority, it can not be skipped\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// else can skip\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// TLS context holds the secret values resolved from an 'api.TLSContext'\ntype TLSContext struct {\n\tTrustedCA        string `json:\"trustedCA,omitempty\"`\n\tCertificateChain string `json:\"certificateChain,omitempty\"`\n\tPrivateKey       string `json:\"privateKey,omitempty\"`\n}\n\n// Equal returns true if 'a' and 'b' have the same contents.\nfunc (a *TLSContext) Equal(b *TLSContext) bool {\n\treturn a == nil && b == nil || a != nil && b != nil && *a == *b\n}\n\n// MarshalJSON marsahls a redacted version of the TLSContext. We want\n// to see which fields are present, but not reveal their values in any\n// logs, etc.\nfunc (t *TLSContext) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {\n\ttype tlsContext TLSContext\n\tvar redacted tlsContext\n\tif t.TrustedCA != \"\" {\n\t\tredacted.TrustedCA = \"[redacted]\"\n\t}\n\tif t.CertificateChain != \"\" {\n\t\tredacted.CertificateChain = \"[redacted]\"\n\t}\n\tif t.PrivateKey != \"\" {\n\t\tredacted.PrivateKey = \"[redacted]\"\n\t}\n\treturn json.Marshal(&redacted)\n}"

Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n\npackage policy\n\nimport (\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/api/v1/models\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/labels\"\n)\n\nfunc TestSearchContextString(t *testing.T) {\n\tfor expected, sc := range map[string]SearchContext{\n\t\t\"From: [unspec:a, unspec:b, unspec:c] => To: [unspec:d, unspec:e, unspec:f] Ports: [HTTP/TCP, HTTPs/TCP]\": {\n\t\t\tTrace: 1,\n\t\t\tDepth: 0,\n\t\t\tFrom:  labels.ParseLabelArray(\"a\", \"c\", \"b\"),\n\t\t\tTo:    labels.ParseLabelArray(\"d\", \"e\", \"f\"),\n\t\t\tDPorts: []*models.Port{\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tName:     \"HTTP\",\n\t\t\t\t\tPort:     80,\n\t\t\t\t\tProtocol: \"TCP\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tName:     \"HTTPs\",\n\t\t\t\t\tPort:     442,\n\t\t\t\t\tProtocol: \"TCP\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\trulesSelect: false,\n\t\t},\n\t\t\"From: [unspec:a, unspec:b, unspec:c] => To: [unspec:d, unspec:e, unspec:f] Ports: [80/TCP, 442/TCP]\": {\n\t\t\tTrace: 1,\n\t\t\tDepth: 0,\n\t\t\tFrom:  labels.ParseLabelArray(\"a\", \"c\", \"b\"),\n\t\t\tTo:    labels.ParseLabelArray(\"d\", \"e\", \"f\"),\n\t\t\tDPorts: []*models.Port{\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tPort:     80,\n\t\t\t\t\tProtocol: \"TCP\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\tPort:     442,\n\t\t\t\t\tProtocol: \"TCP\",\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t},",
    "filePath": "/workspace/pkg/policy/policy_test.go",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 112
  },
  "type": "text"
}
63. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n\npackage policy\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"io\"\n\n\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\"\n)\n\n// PolicyAuditLogger provides structured logging of policy evaluation decisions\n// for audit and debugging purposes.\ntype PolicyAuditLogger struct {\n\tmutex      lock.Mutex\n\tlogger     *logrus.Entry\n\tlogLevel   logrus.Level\n\tauditLog   *logrus.Logger\n\tauditEntry *logrus.Entry\n}\n\n// NewPolicyAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with the specified configuration.\n// If auditOutput is provided, audit logs will be written to it in addition to the standard logger.\nfunc NewPolicyAuditLogger(logLevel logrus.Level, auditOutput io.Writer) *PolicyAuditLogger {\n\tlogger := log.WithField(\"component\", \"audit\")\n\n\tpal := &PolicyAuditLogger{\n\t\tlogger:   logger,\n\t\tlogLevel: logLevel,\n\t}\n\n\t// If auditOutput is provided, create a separate audit logger\n\tif auditOutput != nil {\n\t\tauditLog := logrus.New()\n\t\tauditLog.SetFormatter(&logrus.JSONFormatter{})\n\t\tauditLog.SetOutput(auditOutput)\n\t\tauditLog.SetLevel(logLevel)\n\t\tpal.auditLog = auditLog\n\t\tpal.auditEntry = auditLog.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, \"policy-audit\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn pal\n}\n\n// LogDecision logs a policy decision with the given parameters.\n// This is used for audit trail of policy verdicts.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogDecision(\n\tidentity identity.NumericIdentity,\n\tpolicy string,\n\tverdict string,\n\treason string,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.Identity: identity,\n\t\t\"policy\":           policy,\n\t\t\"verdict\":          verdict,\n\t\t\"reason\":           reason,\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\n\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\n\t\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\t\tswitch verdict {\n\t\tcase \"allow\":\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision: allow\")\n\t\tcase \"deny\":\n\t\t\tentry.Warning(\"Policy decision: deny\")\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to audit logger if configured\n\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\"policy-decision\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// LogEvaluation logs a policy evaluation with full context.\n// This provides detailed information about how a policy was evaluated against identities.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogEvaluation(\n\tctx context.Context,\n\tpolicyKey string,\n\tselectorCache *SelectorCache,\n\tresult bool,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.PolicyKey: policyKey,\n\t\t\"result\":            result,\n\t}\n\n\t// Add selector cache info if available\n\tif selectorCache != nil {\n\t\t// Get the model representation for audit purposes\n\t\tmodel := selectorCache.GetModel()\n\t\tfields[\"selectors_count\"] = len(model)\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\n\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\n\t\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\t\tif result {\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy evaluation completed: pass\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy evaluation completed: fail\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to audit logger if configured\n\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\"policy-evaluation\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// SetLogLevel updates the log level for both standard and audit loggers.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) SetLogLevel(level logrus.Level) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tpal.logLevel = level\n\tif pal.auditLog != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditLog.SetLevel(level)\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetLogLevel returns the current log level.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) GetLogLevel() logrus.Level {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\treturn pal.logLevel\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger.go"
}
Output
{
  "content": "// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n\npackage policy\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"io\"\n\n\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\"\n)\n\n// PolicyAuditLogger provides structured logging of policy evaluation decisions\n// for audit and debugging purposes.\ntype PolicyAuditLogger struct {\n\tmutex      lock.Mutex\n\tlogger     *logrus.Entry\n\tlogLevel   logrus.Level\n\tauditLog   *logrus.Logger\n\tauditEntry *logrus.Entry\n}\n\n// NewPolicyAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with the specified configuration.\n// If auditOutput is provided, audit logs will be written to it in addition to the standard logger.\nfunc NewPolicyAuditLogger(logLevel logrus.Level, auditOutput io.Writer) *PolicyAuditLogger {\n\tlogger := log.WithField(\"component\", \"audit\")\n\n\tpal := &PolicyAuditLogger{\n\t\tlogger:   logger,\n\t\tlogLevel: logLevel,\n\t}\n\n\t// If auditOutput is provided, create a separate audit logger\n\tif auditOutput != nil {\n\t\tauditLog := logrus.New()\n\t\tauditLog.SetFormatter(&logrus.JSONFormatter{})\n\t\tauditLog.SetOutput(auditOutput)\n\t\tauditLog.SetLevel(logLevel)\n\t\tpal.auditLog = auditLog\n\t\tpal.auditEntry = auditLog.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, \"policy-audit\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn pal\n}\n\n// LogDecision logs a policy decision with the given parameters.\n// This is used for audit trail of policy verdicts.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogDecision(\n\tidentity identity.NumericIdentity,\n\tpolicy string,\n\tverdict string,\n\treason string,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.Identity: identity,\n\t\t\"policy\":           policy,\n\t\t\"verdict\":          verdict,\n\t\t\"reason\":           reason,\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\n\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\n\t\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\t\tswitch verdict {\n\t\tcase \"allow\":\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision: allow\")\n\t\tcase \"deny\":\n\t\t\tentry.Warning(\"Policy decision: deny\")\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to audit logger if configured\n\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\"policy-decision\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// LogEvaluation logs a policy evaluation with full context.\n// This provides detailed information about how a policy was evaluated against identities.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogEvaluation(\n\tctx context.Context,\n\tpolicyKey string,\n\tselectorCache *SelectorCache,\n\tresult bool,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.PolicyKey: policyKey,\n\t\t\"result\":            result,\n\t}\n\n\t// Add selector cache info if available\n\tif selectorCache != nil {\n\t\t// Get the model representation for audit purposes\n\t\tmodel := selectorCache.GetModel()\n\t\tfields[\"selectors_count\"] = len(model)\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\n\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\n\t\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\t\tif result {\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy evaluation completed: pass\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy evaluation completed: fail\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to audit logger if configured\n\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\"policy-evaluation\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// SetLogLevel updates the log level for both standard and audit loggers.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) SetLogLevel(level logrus.Level) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tpal.logLevel = level\n\tif pal.auditLog != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditLog.SetLevel(level)\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetLogLevel returns the current log level.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) GetLogLevel() logrus.Level {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\treturn pal.logLevel\n}\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger.go",
  "originalFile": null,
  "structuredPatch": [],
  "type": "create"
}
64. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n\npackage policy\n\nimport (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/labels\"\n)\n\nfunc TestNewPolicyAuditLogger(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test without audit output\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.DebugLevel, nil)\n\trequire.NotNil(t, logger)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.DebugLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n\trequire.Nil(t, logger.auditLog)\n\n\t// Test with audit output\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger = NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\trequire.NotNil(t, logger)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n\trequire.NotNil(t, logger.auditLog)\n}\n\nfunc TestLogDecision(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(1001)\n\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"test-policy\", \"allow\", \"matches selector\")\n\n\t// Verify audit log contains the decision\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\n\n\t// Parse JSON to verify structure\n\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\n\terr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-decision\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, float64(1001), auditEntry[\"identity\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"test-policy\", auditEntry[\"policy\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"allow\", auditEntry[\"verdict\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"matches selector\", auditEntry[\"reason\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogDecisionDeny(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(2002)\n\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"restrictive-policy\", \"deny\", \"denied by rule\")\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\n\n\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\n\terr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-decision\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"deny\", auditEntry[\"verdict\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"denied by rule\", auditEntry[\"reason\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogEvaluation(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tpolicyKey := \"test-policy-key\"\n\n\t// Create a minimal SelectorCache for testing\n\trepo := NewPolicyRepository(nil, nil, nil)\n\tselectorCache := repo.GetSelectorCache()\n\n\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, selectorCache, true)\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\n\n\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\n\terr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-evaluation\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, policyKey, auditEntry[logfields.PolicyKey])\n\trequire.Equal(t, true, auditEntry[\"result\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogEvaluationFailure(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tpolicyKey := \"failing-policy\"\n\n\trepo := NewPolicyRepository(nil, nil, nil)\n\tselectorCache := repo.GetSelectorCache()\n\n\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, selectorCache, false)\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\n\n\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\n\terr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-evaluation\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, false, auditEntry[\"result\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogEvaluationWithoutSelectorCache(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tpolicyKey := \"test-policy-no-cache\"\n\n\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, nil, true)\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\n\n\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\n\terr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-evaluation\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, policyKey, auditEntry[logfields.PolicyKey])\n}\n\nfunc TestSetLogLevel(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n\n\tlogger.SetLogLevel(logrus.DebugLevel)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.DebugLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n\n\tlogger.SetLogLevel(logrus.WarnLevel)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.WarnLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n}\n\nfunc TestGetLogLevel(t *testing.T) {\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.ErrorLevel, nil)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.ErrorLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n}\n\nfunc TestConcurrentLogging(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\t// Test concurrent access to LogDecision\n\tdone := make(chan bool)\n\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\n\t\tgo func(index int) {\n\t\t\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(3000 + uint32(index))\n\t\t\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"concurrent-policy\", \"allow\", \"test\")\n\t\t\tdone <- true\n\t\t}(i)\n\t}\n\n\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\n\t\t<-done\n\t}\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\n\n\t// Count the number of log entries\n\tlineCount := bytes.Count([]byte(logOutput), []byte(\"\\n\"))\n\trequire.Equal(t, 10, lineCount)\n}\n\nfunc TestConcurrentEvaluation(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\trepo := NewPolicyRepository(nil, nil, nil)\n\tselectorCache := repo.GetSelectorCache()\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\n\tdone := make(chan bool)\n\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\n\t\tgo func(index int) {\n\t\t\tpolicyKey := \"policy-\" + string(rune(index))\n\t\t\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, selectorCache, index%2 == 0)\n\t\t\tdone <- true\n\t\t}(i)\n\t}\n\n\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\n\t\t<-done\n\t}\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\n\n\t// Count the number of log entries\n\tlineCount := bytes.Count([]byte(logOutput), []byte(\"\\n\"))\n\trequire.Equal(t, 10, lineCount)\n}\n\nfunc TestMultipleDecisionsWithDifferentVerdicts(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\tverdicts := []string{\"allow\", \"deny\", \"allow\", \"deny\"}\n\tfor i, verdict := range verdicts {\n\t\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(5000 + uint32(i))\n\t\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"test-policy\", verdict, \"test reason\")\n\t}\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\n\n\tlines := bytes.Split(bytes.TrimSpace([]byte(logOutput)), []byte(\"\\n\"))\n\trequire.Equal(t, 4, len(lines))\n\n\tfor i, verdict := range verdicts {\n\t\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\n\t\terr := json.Unmarshal(lines[i], &auditEntry)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\trequire.Equal(t, verdict, auditEntry[\"verdict\"])\n\t}\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger_test.go"
}

65. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/pkg/logging/logfields/logfields.go",
  "limit": 50,
  "offset": 80
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "\n\t// OldIdentity is a previously used security identity\n\tOldIdentity = \"oldIdentity\"\n\n\t// PolicyKey is a policy map key\n\tPolicyKey = \"policyKey\"\n\n\t// PolicyEntry is a policy map value\n\tPolicyEntry = \"policyEntry\"\n\n\t// PolicyRevision is the revision of the policy in the repository or of\n\t// the object in question\n\tPolicyRevision = \"policyRevision\"\n\n\t// PolicyKeysAdded is a set of added policy map keys\n\tPolicyKeysAdded = \"policyKeysAdded\"\n\n\t// PolicyKeysDeleted is a set of deleted policy map keys\n\tPolicyKeysDeleted = \"policyKeysDeleted\"\n\n\t// PolicyEntriesOld is a set of old policy map keys and values\n\tPolicyEntriesOld = \"policyEntriesOld\"\n\n\t// DatapathPolicyRevision is the policy revision currently running in\n\t// the datapath\n\tDatapathPolicyRevision = \"datapathPolicyRevision\"\n\n\t// DesiredPolicyRevision is the latest policy revision as evaluated for\n\t// an endpoint. It is the desired policy revision to be implemented\n\t// into the datapath.\n\tDesiredPolicyRevision = \"desiredPolicyRevision\"\n\n\t// PolicyID is the identifier of a L3, L4 or L7 Policy. Ideally the .NumericIdentity\n\tPolicyID = \"policyID\"\n\n\t// AddedPolicyID is the .NumericIdentity, or set or them\n\tAddedPolicyID = \"policyID.Added\"\n\n\t// DeletedPolicyID is the .NumericIdentity, or set or them\n\tDeletedPolicyID = \"policyID.Deleted\"\n\n\t// AddedPolicyDenyID is the .NumericIdentity, or set or them\n\tAddedPolicyDenyID = \"policyID.Deny.Added\"\n\n\t// DeletedPolicyDenyID is the .NumericIdentity, or set or them\n\tDeletedPolicyDenyID = \"policyID.Deny.Deleted\"\n\n\t// L3PolicyID is the identifier of a L3 Policy\n\tL3PolicyID = \"policyID.L3\"\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/pkg/logging/logfields/logfields.go",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 80,
    "totalLines": 802
  },
  "type": "text"
}
66. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/pkg/policy/config.go",
  "limit": 50
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n\npackage policy\n\nimport (\n\tipcacheTypes \"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/ipcache/types\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/labels\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/source\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/time\"\n)\n\nvar (\n\tlog          = logging.DefaultLogger.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, \"policy\")\n\tmutex        lock.RWMutex // Protects enablePolicy\n\tenablePolicy string       // Whether policy enforcement is enabled.\n)\n\n// SetPolicyEnabled sets the policy enablement configuration. Valid values are:\n// - endpoint.AlwaysEnforce\n// - endpoint.NeverEnforce\n// - endpoint.DefaultEnforcement\nfunc SetPolicyEnabled(val string) {\n\tmutex.Lock()\n\tenablePolicy = val\n\tmutex.Unlock()\n}\n\n// GetPolicyEnabled returns the policy enablement configuration\nfunc GetPolicyEnabled() string {\n\tmutex.RLock()\n\tval := enablePolicy\n\tmutex.RUnlock()\n\treturn val\n}\n\n// AddOptions are options which can be passed to PolicyAdd\ntype AddOptions struct {\n\t// Replace if true indicates that existing rules with identical labels should be replaced\n\tReplace bool\n\t// ReplaceWithLabels if present indicates that existing rules with the\n\t// given LabelArray should be deleted.\n\tReplaceWithLabels labels.LabelArray\n\n\t// Generated should be set as true to signalize a the policy being inserted\n\t// was generated by cilium-agent, e.g. dns poller.\n\tGenerated bool",
    "filePath": "/workspace/pkg/policy/config.go",
    "numLines": 50,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 81
  },
  "type": "text"
}

70. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger.go"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n\npackage policy\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"io\"\n\n\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\"\n)\n\n// PolicyAuditLogger provides structured logging of policy evaluation decisions\n// for audit and debugging purposes.\ntype PolicyAuditLogger struct {\n\tmutex      lock.Mutex\n\tlogger     *logrus.Entry\n\tlogLevel   logrus.Level\n\tauditLog   *logrus.Logger\n\tauditEntry *logrus.Entry\n}\n\n// NewPolicyAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with the specified configuration.\n// If auditOutput is provided, audit logs will be written to it in addition to the standard logger.\nfunc NewPolicyAuditLogger(logLevel logrus.Level, auditOutput io.Writer) *PolicyAuditLogger {\n\tlogger := log.WithField(\"component\", \"audit\")\n\n\tpal := &PolicyAuditLogger{\n\t\tlogger:   logger,\n\t\tlogLevel: logLevel,\n\t}\n\n\t// If auditOutput is provided, create a separate audit logger\n\tif auditOutput != nil {\n\t\tauditLog := logrus.New()\n\t\tauditLog.SetFormatter(&logrus.JSONFormatter{})\n\t\tauditLog.SetOutput(auditOutput)\n\t\tauditLog.SetLevel(logLevel)\n\t\tpal.auditLog = auditLog\n\t\tpal.auditEntry = auditLog.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, \"policy-audit\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn pal\n}\n\n// LogDecision logs a policy decision with the given parameters.\n// This is used for audit trail of policy verdicts.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogDecision(\n\tidentity identity.NumericIdentity,\n\tpolicy string,\n\tverdict string,\n\treason string,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.Identity: identity,\n\t\t\"policy\":           policy,\n\t\t\"verdict\":          verdict,\n\t\t\"reason\":           reason,\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\n\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\n\t\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\t\tswitch verdict {\n\t\tcase \"allow\":\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision: allow\")\n\t\tcase \"deny\":\n\t\t\tentry.Warning(\"Policy decision: deny\")\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to audit logger if configured\n\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\"policy-decision\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// LogEvaluation logs a policy evaluation with full context.\n// This provides detailed information about how a policy was evaluated against identities.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogEvaluation(\n\tctx context.Context,\n\tpolicyKey string,\n\tselectorCache *SelectorCache,\n\tresult bool,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.PolicyKey: policyKey,\n\t\t\"result\":            result,\n\t}\n\n\t// Add selector cache info if available\n\tif selectorCache != nil {\n\t\t// Get the model representation for audit purposes\n\t\tmodel := selectorCache.GetModel()\n\t\tfields[\"selectors_count\"] = len(model)\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\n\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\n\t\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\t\tif result {\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy evaluation completed: pass\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy evaluation completed: fail\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to audit logger if configured\n\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\"policy-evaluation\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// SetLogLevel updates the log level for both standard and audit loggers.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) SetLogLevel(level logrus.Level) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tpal.logLevel = level\n\tif pal.auditLog != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditLog.SetLevel(level)\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetLogLevel returns the current log level.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) GetLogLevel() logrus.Level {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\treturn pal.logLevel\n}\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger.go",
    "numLines": 143,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 143
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger_test.go"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n\npackage policy\n\nimport (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/labels\"\n)\n\nfunc TestNewPolicyAuditLogger(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test without audit output\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.DebugLevel, nil)\n\trequire.NotNil(t, logger)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.DebugLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n\trequire.Nil(t, logger.auditLog)\n\n\t// Test with audit output\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger = NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\trequire.NotNil(t, logger)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n\trequire.NotNil(t, logger.auditLog)\n}\n\nfunc TestLogDecision(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(1001)\n\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"test-policy\", \"allow\", \"matches selector\")\n\n\t// Verify audit log contains the decision\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\n\n\t// Parse JSON to verify structure\n\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\n\terr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-decision\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, float64(1001), auditEntry[\"identity\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"test-policy\", auditEntry[\"policy\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"allow\", auditEntry[\"verdict\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"matches selector\", auditEntry[\"reason\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogDecisionDeny(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(2002)\n\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"restrictive-policy\", \"deny\", \"denied by rule\")\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\n\n\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\n\terr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-decision\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"deny\", auditEntry[\"verdict\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"denied by rule\", auditEntry[\"reason\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogEvaluation(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tpolicyKey := \"test-policy-key\"\n\n\t// Create a minimal SelectorCache for testing\n\trepo := NewPolicyRepository(nil, nil, nil)\n\tselectorCache := repo.GetSelectorCache()\n\n\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, selectorCache, true)\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\n\n\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\n\terr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-evaluation\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, policyKey, auditEntry[logfields.PolicyKey])\n\trequire.Equal(t, true, auditEntry[\"result\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogEvaluationFailure(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tpolicyKey := \"failing-policy\"\n\n\trepo := NewPolicyRepository(nil, nil, nil)\n\tselectorCache := repo.GetSelectorCache()\n\n\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, selectorCache, false)\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\n\n\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\n\terr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-evaluation\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, false, auditEntry[\"result\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogEvaluationWithoutSelectorCache(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tpolicyKey := \"test-policy-no-cache\"\n\n\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, nil, true)\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\n\n\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\n\terr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-evaluation\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, policyKey, auditEntry[logfields.PolicyKey])\n}\n\nfunc TestSetLogLevel(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n\n\tlogger.SetLogLevel(logrus.DebugLevel)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.DebugLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n\n\tlogger.SetLogLevel(logrus.WarnLevel)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.WarnLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n}\n\nfunc TestGetLogLevel(t *testing.T) {\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.ErrorLevel, nil)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.ErrorLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n}\n\nfunc TestConcurrentLogging(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\t// Test concurrent access to LogDecision\n\tdone := make(chan bool)\n\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\n\t\tgo func(index int) {\n\t\t\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(3000 + uint32(index))\n\t\t\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"concurrent-policy\", \"allow\", \"test\")\n\t\t\tdone <- true\n\t\t}(i)\n\t}\n\n\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\n\t\t<-done\n\t}\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\n\n\t// Count the number of log entries\n\tlineCount := bytes.Count([]byte(logOutput), []byte(\"\\n\"))\n\trequire.Equal(t, 10, lineCount)\n}\n\nfunc TestConcurrentEvaluation(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\trepo := NewPolicyRepository(nil, nil, nil)\n\tselectorCache := repo.GetSelectorCache()\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\n\tdone := make(chan bool)\n\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\n\t\tgo func(index int) {\n\t\t\tpolicyKey := \"policy-\" + string(rune(index))\n\t\t\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, selectorCache, index%2 == 0)\n\t\t\tdone <- true\n\t\t}(i)\n\t}\n\n\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\n\t\t<-done\n\t}\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\n\n\t// Count the number of log entries\n\tlineCount := bytes.Count([]byte(logOutput), []byte(\"\\n\"))\n\trequire.Equal(t, 10, lineCount)\n}\n\nfunc TestMultipleDecisionsWithDifferentVerdicts(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\tverdicts := []string{\"allow\", \"deny\", \"allow\", \"deny\"}\n\tfor i, verdict := range verdicts {\n\t\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(5000 + uint32(i))\n\t\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"test-policy\", verdict, \"test reason\")\n\t}\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\n\n\tlines := bytes.Split(bytes.TrimSpace([]byte(logOutput)), []byte(\"\\n\"))\n\trequire.Equal(t, 4, len(lines))\n\n\tfor i, verdict := range verdicts {\n\t\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\n\t\terr := json.Unmarshal(lines[i], &auditEntry)\n\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\t\trequire.Equal(t, verdict, auditEntry[\"verdict\"])\n\t}\n}\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger_test.go",
    "numLines": 240,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 240
  },
  "type": "text"
}

Input
{
  "description": "Review code for reuse opportunities",
  "prompt": "Review the following Cilium policy audit logger implementation for code reuse. Search the codebase for existing utilities, helpers, and patterns that could replace or improve the new code.\n\n## Files to Review:\n\n### audit_logger.go\n```go\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n\npackage policy\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"io\"\n\n\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\"\n)\n\n// PolicyAuditLogger provides structured logging of policy evaluation decisions\n// for audit and debugging purposes.\ntype PolicyAuditLogger struct {\n\tmutex      lock.Mutex\n\tlogger     *logrus.Entry\n\tlogLevel   logrus.Level\n\tauditLog   *logrus.Logger\n\tauditEntry *logrus.Entry\n}\n\n// NewPolicyAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with the specified configuration.\n// If auditOutput is provided, audit logs will be written to it in addition to the standard logger.\nfunc NewPolicyAuditLogger(logLevel logrus.Level, auditOutput io.Writer) *PolicyAuditLogger {\n\tlogger := log.WithField(\"component\", \"audit\")\n\n\tpal := &PolicyAuditLogger{\n\t\tlogger:   logger,\n\t\tlogLevel: logLevel,\n\t}\n\n\t// If auditOutput is provided, create a separate audit logger\n\tif auditOutput != nil {\n\t\tauditLog := logrus.New()\n\t\tauditLog.SetFormatter(&logrus.JSONFormatter{})\n\t\tauditLog.SetOutput(auditOutput)\n\t\tauditLog.SetLevel(logLevel)\n\t\tpal.auditLog = auditLog\n\t\tpal.auditEntry = auditLog.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, \"policy-audit\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn pal\n}\n\n// LogDecision logs a policy decision with the given parameters.\n// This is used for audit trail of policy verdicts.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogDecision(\n\tidentity identity.NumericIdentity,\n\tpolicy string,\n\tverdict string,\n\treason string,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.Identity: identity,\n\t\t\"policy\":           policy,\n\t\t\"verdict\":          verdict,\n\t\t\"reason\":           reason,\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\n\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\n\t\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\t\tswitch verdict {\n\t\tcase \"allow\":\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision: allow\")\n\t\tcase \"deny\":\n\t\t\tentry.Warning(\"Policy decision: deny\")\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to audit logger if configured\n\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\"policy-decision\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// LogEvaluation logs a policy evaluation with full context.\n// This provides detailed information about how a policy was evaluated against identities.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogEvaluation(\n\tctx context.Context,\n\tpolicyKey string,\n\tselectorCache *SelectorCache,\n\tresult bool,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.PolicyKey: policyKey,\n\t\t\"result\":            result,\n\t}\n\n\t// Add selector cache info if available\n\tif selectorCache != nil {\n\t\t// Get the model representation for audit purposes\n\t\tmodel := selectorCache.GetModel()\n\t\tfields[\"selectors_count\"] = len(model)\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\n\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\n\t\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\t\tif result {\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy evaluation completed: pass\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy evaluation completed: fail\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to audit logger if configured\n\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\"policy-evaluation\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// SetLogLevel updates the log level for both standard and audit loggers.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) SetLogLevel(level logrus.Level) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tpal.logLevel = level\n\tif pal.auditLog != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditLog.SetLevel(level)\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetLogLevel returns the current log level.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) GetLogLevel() logrus.Level {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\treturn pal.logLevel\n}\n```\n\n## Analysis Tasks:\n\n1. **Search for existing logging patterns** in pkg/policy/ and related packages for audit/debug logging that might already exist\n2. **Check for existing lock patterns** - are there standardized ways Cilium wraps loggers with thread-safety?\n3. **Look for existing JSON formatter configurations** - how does Cilium handle JSON audit logs elsewhere?\n4. **Check for existing verdict/decision logging patterns** - similar audit trails in the codebase?\n5. **Identify any utility functions** for setting up dual loggers (one JSON, one text)\n\nFor each finding, suggest:\n- The existing function/pattern to use instead\n- How it would simplify the code\n- File locations and line numbers\n\nReturn specific line numbers and suggestions for improvement.",
  "run_in_background": true,
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}

(\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"io\"\n\n\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\"\n)\n\n// PolicyAuditLogger provides structured logging of policy evaluation decisions\n// for audit and debugging purposes.\ntype PolicyAuditLogger struct {\n\tmutex      lock.Mutex\n\tlogger     *logrus.Entry\n\tlogLevel   logrus.Level\n\tauditLog   *logrus.Logger\n\tauditEntry *logrus.Entry\n}\n\n// NewPolicyAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with the specified configuration.\n// If auditOutput is provided, audit logs will be written to it in addition to the standard logger.\nfunc NewPolicyAuditLogger(logLevel logrus.Level, auditOutput io.Writer) *PolicyAuditLogger {\n\tlogger := log.WithField(\"component\", \"audit\")\n\n\tpal := &PolicyAuditLogger{\n\t\tlogger:   logger,\n\t\tlogLevel: logLevel,\n\t}\n\n\t// If auditOutput is provided, create a separate audit logger\n\tif auditOutput != nil {\n\t\tauditLog := logrus.New()\n\t\tauditLog.SetFormatter(&logrus.JSONFormatter{})\n\t\tauditLog.SetOutput(auditOutput)\n\t\tauditLog.SetLevel(logLevel)\n\t\tpal.auditLog = for audit trail of policy verdicts.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogDecision(\n\tidentity identity.NumericIdentity,\n\tpolicy string,\n\tverdict string,\n\treason string,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.Identity: identity,\n\t\t\"policy\":           policy,\n\t\t\"verdict\": verdict {\n\t\tcase \"allow\":\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision: allow\")\n\t\tcase \"deny\":\n\t\t\tentry.Warning(\"Policy decision: deny\")\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to audit logger if configured\n\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\"policy-decision\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// LogEvaluation logs a policy evaluation with full context.\n// This provides detailed information about how a policy was evaluated against identities.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogEvaluation(\n\tctx context.Context,\n\tpolicyKey string,\n\tselectorCache *SelectorCache,\n\tresult bool,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.PolicyKey: policyKey,\n\t\t\"result\":            result,\n\t}\n\n\t// Add selector cache info if available\n\tif selectorCache != nil {\n\t\t// Get the model representation for audit purposes\n\t\tmodel := selectorCache.GetModel()\n\t\tfields[\"selectors_count\"] = len(model)\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\n\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\n\t\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\t\tif result {\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy evaluation completed: pass\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy evaluation completed: fail\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to audit logger if configured\n\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\"policy-evaluation\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// SetLogLevel updates the log level for both standard and audit loggers.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) SetLogLevel(level logrus.Level) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tpal.logLevel = level\n\tif pal.auditLog != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditLog.SetLevel(level)\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetLogLevel returns the current log level.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) GetLogLevel() logrus.Level {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\treturn pal.logLevel\n}\n```\n\n## Analysis Tasks:\n\n1. **Search for existing logging patterns** in pkg/policy/ and related packages for audit/debug logging that might already 73. Agent @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Review code quality for design issues",
  "prompt": "Review the following Cilium policy audit logger for code quality issues. Look for:\n\n1. **Redundant state** - logLevel is stored when it's also in auditLog.Level - could derive instead\n2. **Parameter sprawl** - are there too many parameters in methods that could be reorganized?\n3. **Copy-paste patterns** - the LogDecision and LogEvaluation methods have very similar logic, could they share a helper?\n4. **Leaky abstractions** - is the dual logger setup (standard + audit) well-encapsulated?\n5. **String literals** - uses \"policy-decision\", \"policy-evaluation\", \"component\", \"audit\", \"verdict\", \"reason\" as strings - should these be constants?\n\n## Test File Issues:\n\nThe test file has several patterns:\n- Repeated `buf := &bytes.Buffer{}` and `logger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)` in many tests\n- Similar JSON parsing logic repeated: `json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)`\n- Similar log output verification patterns\n\nLook for:\n- Test helper functions that could be extracted\n- Unnecessary assertions or redundant checks\n- Areas where test setup could be shared\n\nProvide specific recommendations with line numbers for refactoring opportunities.",
  "run_in_background": true,
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "aea0fff0bced256bb",
  "canReadOutputFile": true,
  "description": "Review code quality for design issues",
  "isAsync": true,
  "outputFile": "/tmp/claude-1000/-workspace/tasks/aea0fff0bced256bb.output",
  "prompt": "Review the following Cilium policy audit logger for code quality issues. Look for:\n\n1. **Redundant state** - logLevel is stored when it's also in auditLog.Level - could derive instead\n2. **Parameter sprawl** - are there too many parameters in methods that could be reorganized?\n3. **Copy-paste patterns** - the LogDecision and LogEvaluation methods have very similar logic, could they share a helper?\n4. **Leaky abstractions** - is the dual logger setup (standard + audit) well-encapsulated?\n5. **String literals** - uses \"policy-decision\", \"policy-evaluation\", \"component\", \"audit\", \"verdict\", \"reason\" as strings - should these be constants?\n\n## Test File Issues:\n\nThe test file has several patterns:\n- Repeated `buf := &bytes.Buffer{}` and `logger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)` in many tests\n- Similar JSON parsing logic repeated: `json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)`\n- Similar log output verification patterns\n\nLook for:\n- Test helper functions that could be extracted\n- Unnecessary assertions or redundant checks\n- Areas where test setup could be shared\n\nProvide specific recommendations with line numbers for refactoring opportunities.",
  "status": "async_launched"
}
74. Agent @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Review code efficiency and performance",
  "prompt": "Review the following Cilium policy audit logger for efficiency issues:\n\n1. **Hot-path blocking** - are there expensive operations in LogDecision/LogEvaluation that shouldn't be?\n2. **Unnecessary work** - is logLevel being checked twice in LogDecision/LogEvaluation (once in the condition, once when logging)?\n3. **Memory usage** - the mutex.Lock/defer pattern and repeated field copying - any issues?\n4. **Concurrent access patterns** - is the dual-logger approach (standard + audit) handling concurrency correctly?\n5. **SelectorCache.GetModel() calls** - is this called in the logging hot path? Is it expensive?\n\nFor the test file:\n- Creating NewPolicyRepository(nil, nil, nil) in each test function - is this expensive? Could it be reused?\n- Creating 10 goroutines with done channel - are there goroutine leaks or race conditions?\n- JSON unmarshaling in tests - could slicing be more efficient?\n\nLook for:\n- N+1 patterns\n- Inefficient slice/string operations (like `logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]`)\n- Unnecessary allocations or copies\n- Lock contention issues\n- Hot-path improvements\n\nProvide specific line numbers and efficiency suggestions.",
  "run_in_background": true,
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
