-5b17-4855-aa5c-b4b108b21427\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:27.160Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"1416d70f-ca1a-4ba4-b542-38f68402d9d2\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01Fkzi4NzT86Qi6n3xS49raP\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192package hooks\\n     5\u2192\\n     6\u2192import (\\n     7\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/lumberjack/v2\\\"\\n     8\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\\\"\\n     9\u2192)\\n    10\u2192\\n    11\u2192// FileRotationOption provides all parameters for file rotation\\n    12\u2192type FileRotationOption struct {\\n    13\u2192\\tFileName   string\\n    14\u2192\\tMaxSize    int\\n    15\u2192\\tMaxAge     int\\n    16\u2192\\tMaxBackups int\\n    17\u2192\\tLocalTime  bool\\n    18\u2192\\tCompress   bool\\n    19\u2192}\\n    20\u2192\\n    21\u2192type Option func(*FileRotationOption)\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192// WithMaxSize provides way to adjust maxSize (in MBs). Defaults to\\n    24\u2192// 100 MBs.\\n    25\u2192func WithMaxSize(maxSize int) Option {\\n    26\u2192\\treturn func(option *FileRotationOption) {\\n    27\u2192\\t\\toption.MaxSize = maxSize\\n    28\u2192\\t}\\n    29\u2192}\\n    30\u2192\\n    31\u2192// WithMaxAge provides way to adjust max age (in days). The default is\\n    32\u2192// not to remove old log files based on age.\\n    33\u2192func WithMaxAge(maxAge int) Option {\\n    34\u2192\\treturn func(option *FileRotationOption) {\\n    35\u2192\\t\\toption.MaxAge = maxAge\\n    36\u2192\\t}\\n    37\u2192}\\n    38\u2192\\n    39\u2192// WithMaxBackups provides way to adjust max number of backups. Defaults\\n    40\u2192// to retain all old log files though MaxAge may still cause them to get\\n    41\u2192// deleted.\\n    42\u2192func WithMaxBackups(MaxBackups int) Option {\\n    43\u2192\\treturn func(option *FileRotationOption) {\\n    44\u2192\\t\\toption.MaxBackups = MaxBackups\\n    45\u2192\\t}\\n    46\u2192}\\n    47\u2192\\n    48\u2192// EnableLocalTime is to determine if the time used for formatting the\\n    49\u2192// timestamps in backup files is the computer's local time.  The default\\n    50\u2192// is to use UTC time.\\n    51\u2192func EnableLocalTime() Option {\\n    52\u2192\\treturn func(option *FileRotationOption) {\\n    53\u2192\\t\\toption.LocalTime = true\\n    54\u2192\\t}\\n    55\u2192}\\n    56\u2192\\n    57\u2192// EnableCompression is to enable old log file gzip compression. Defaults\\n    58\u2192// to false.\\n    59\u2192func EnableCompression() Option {\\n    60\u2192\\treturn func(option *FileRotationOption) {\\n    61\u2192\\t\\toption.Compress = true\\n    62\u2192\\t}\\n    63\u2192}\\n    64\u2192\\n    65\u2192// FileRotationLogHook stores the configuration of the hook\\n    66\u2192type FileRotationLogHook struct {\\n    67\u2192\\tlogger *lumberjack.Logger\\n    68\u2192}\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192// NewFileRotationLogHook creates a new FileRotationLogHook*/\\n    71\u2192func NewFileRotationLogHook(fileName string, opts ...Option) *FileRotationLogHook {\\n    72\u2192\\toptions := &FileRotationOption{\\n    73\u2192\\t\\tFileName:  fileName,\\n    74\u2192\\t\\tMaxSize:   100,   // MBs\\n    75\u2192\\t\\tLocalTime: false, // UTC\\n    76\u2192\\t\\tCompress:  false, // no compression with gzip\\n    77\u2192\\t}\\n    78\u2192\\n    79\u2192\\tfor _, opt := range opts {\\n    80\u2192\\t\\topt(options)\\n    81\u2192\\t}\\n    82\u2192\\n    83\u2192\\tlogger := &lumberjack.Logger{\\n    84\u2192\\t\\tFilename:   options.FileName,\\n    85\u2192\\t\\tMaxSize:    options.MaxSize,\\n    86\u2192\\t\\tMaxAge:     options.MaxAge,\\n    87\u2192\\t\\tMaxBackups: options.MaxBackups,\\n    88\u2192\\t\\tLocalTime:  options.LocalTime,\\n    89\u2192\\t\\tCompress:   options.Compress,\\n    90\u2192\\t}\\n    91\u2192\\n    92\u2192\\treturn &FileRotationLogHook{\\n    93\u2192\\t\\tlogger: logger,\\n    94\u2192\\t}\\n    95\u2192}\\n    96\u2192\\n    97\u2192// Fire is called when a log event is fired.\\n    98\u2192func (hook *FileRotationLogHook) Fire(entry *logrus.Entry) error {\\n    99\u2192\\tline, err := entry.String()\\n   100\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   101\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n   102\u2192\\t}\\n   103\u2192\\n   104\u2192\\t_, err = hook.logger.Write([]byte(line))\\n   105\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   106\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n   107\u2192\\t}\\n   108\u2192\\n   109\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   110\u2192}\\n   111\u2192\\n   112\u2192// Levels returns the available logging levels\\n   113\u2192func (hook *FileRotationLogHook) Levels() []logrus.Level {\\n   114\u2192\\treturn logrus.AllLevels\\n   115\u2192}\\n   116\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"0f5fb537-3a82-475f-821b-deb82384bbec\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:27.199Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"1416d70f-ca1a-4ba4-b542-38f68402d9d2\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"0f5fb537-3a82-475f-821b-deb82384bbec\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01Fkzi4NzT86Qi6n3xS49raP\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01Fkzi4NzT86Qi6n3xS49raP\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:27.199Z\",\"uuid\":\"ed9a4629-43ed-42e3-9202-8a440fd7734f\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"a38df8e6-bfde-4eb8-b358-8766cd6c1fa9\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_017ZVf5A9nmtPHe6ZCqqsQYh\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192package exporter\\n     5\u2192\\n     6\u2192import (\\n     7\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n     8\u2192\\t\\\"encoding/json\\\"\\n     9\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    10\u2192\\t\\\"io\\\"\\n    11\u2192\\t\\\"os\\\"\\n    12\u2192\\n    13\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/lumberjack/v2\\\"\\n    14\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\\\"\\n    15\u2192\\n    16\u2192\\tflowpb \\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/api/v1/flow\\\"\\n    17\u2192\\tobserverpb \\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/api/v1/observer\\\"\\n    18\u2192\\tv1 \\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/hubble/api/v1\\\"\\n    19\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/hubble/exporter/exporteroption\\\"\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/hubble/filters\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\tnodeTypes \\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/node/types\\\"\\n    22\u2192)\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192// exporter is an implementation of OnDecodedEvent interface that writes Hubble events to a file.\\n    25\u2192type exporter struct {\\n    26\u2192\\tFlowLogExporter\\n    27\u2192\\tctx     context.Context\\n    28\u2192\\tlogger  logrus.FieldLogger\\n    29\u2192\\tencoder *json.Encoder\\n    30\u2192\\twriter  io.WriteCloser\\n    31\u2192\\tflow    *flowpb.Flow\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192\\topts exporteroption.Options\\n    34\u2192}\\n    35\u2192\\n    36\u2192// NewExporter initializes an exporter.\\n    37\u2192func NewExporter(\\n    38\u2192\\tctx context.Context,\\n    39\u2192\\tlogger logrus.FieldLogger,\\n    40\u2192\\toptions ...exporteroption.Option) (*exporter, error) {\\n    41\u2192\\topts := exporteroption.Default // start with defaults\\n    42\u2192\\tfor _, opt := range options {\\n    43\u2192\\t\\tif err := opt(&opts); err != nil {\\n    44\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to apply option: %w\\\", err)\\n    45\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    46\u2192\\t}\\n    47\u2192\\tlogger.WithField(\\\"options\\\", opts).Info(\\\"Configuring Hubble event exporter\\\")\\n    48\u2192\\tvar writer io.WriteCloser\\n    49\u2192\\t// If hubble-export-file-path is set to \\\"stdout\\\", use os.Stdout as the writer.\\n    50\u2192\\tif opts.Path == \\\"stdout\\\" {\\n    51\u2192\\t\\twriter = &noopWriteCloser{w: os.Stdout}\\n    52\u2192\\t} else {\\n    53\u2192\\t\\twriter = &lumberjack.Logger{\\n    54\u2192\\t\\t\\tFilename:   opts.Path,\\n    55\u2192\\t\\t\\tMaxSize:    opts.MaxSizeMB,\\n    56\u2192\\t\\t\\tMaxBackups: opts.MaxBackups,\\n    57\u2192\\t\\t\\tCompress:   opts.Compress,\\n    58\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    59\u2192\\t}\\n    60\u2192\\treturn newExporter(ctx, logger, writer, opts)\\n    61\u2192}\\n    62\u2192\\n    63\u2192// newExporter let's you supply your own WriteCloser for tests.\\n    64\u2192func newExporter(ctx context.Context, logger logrus.FieldLogger, writer io.WriteCloser, opts exporteroption.Options) (*exporter, error) {\\n    65\u2192\\tencoder := json.NewEncoder(writer)\\n    66\u2192\\tvar flow *flowpb.Flow\\n    67\u2192\\tif opts.FieldMask.Active() {\\n    68\u2192\\t\\tflow = new(flowpb.Flow)\\n    69\u2192\\t\\topts.FieldMask.Alloc(flow.ProtoReflect())\\n    70\u2192\\t}\\n    71\u2192\\treturn &exporter{\\n    72\u2192\\t\\tctx:     ctx,\\n    73\u2192\\t\\tlogger:  logger,\\n    74\u2192\\t\\tencoder: encoder,\\n    75\u2192\\t\\twriter:  writer,\\n    76\u2192\\t\\tflow:    flow,\\n    77\u2192\\t\\topts:    opts,\\n    78\u2192\\t}, nil\\n    79\u2192}\\n    80\u2192\\n    81\u2192// eventToExportEvent converts Event to ExportEvent.\\n    82\u2192func (e *exporter) eventToExportEvent(event *v1.Event) *observerpb.ExportEvent {\\n    83\u2192\\tswitch ev := event.Event.(type) {\\n    84\u2192\\tcase *flowpb.Flow:\\n    85\u2192\\t\\tif e.opts.FieldMask.Active() {\\n    86\u2192\\t\\t\\te.opts.FieldMask.Copy(e.flow.ProtoReflect(), ev.ProtoReflect())\\n    87\u2192\\t\\t\\tev = e.flow\\n    88\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    89\u2192\\t\\treturn &observerpb.ExportEvent{\\n    90\u2192\\t\\t\\tTime:     ev.GetTime(),\\n    91\u2192\\t\\t\\tNodeName: ev.GetNodeName(),\\n    92\u2192\\t\\t\\tResponseTypes: &observerpb.ExportEvent_Flow{\\n    93\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tFlow: ev,\\n    94\u2192\\t\\t\\t},\\n    95\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    96\u2192\\tcase *flowpb.LostEvent:\\n    97\u2192\\t\\treturn &observerpb.ExportEvent{\\n    98\u2192\\t\\t\\tTime:     event.Timestamp,\\n    99\u2192\\t\\t\\tNodeName: nodeTypes.GetName(),\\n   100\u2192\\t\\t\\tResponseTypes: &observerpb.ExportEvent_LostEvents{\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192package logging\\n     5\u2192\\n     6\u2192import (\\n     7\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n     8\u2192\\t\\\"log/slog\\\"\\n     9\u2192\\t\\\"os\\\"\\n    10\u2192\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n    11\u2192\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n    12\u2192\\n    13\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\\\"\\n    14\u2192)\\n    15\u2192\\n    16\u2192// SlogNopHandler discards all logs.\\n    17\u2192var SlogNopHandler slog.Handler = nopHandler{}\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192type nopHandler struct{}\\n    20\u2192\\n    21\u2192func (nopHandler) Enabled(context.Context, slog.Level) bool  { return false }\\n    22\u2192func (nopHandler) Handle(context.Context, slog.Record) error { return nil }\\n    23\u2192func (n nopHandler) WithAttrs([]slog.Attr) slog.Handler      { return n }\\n    24\u2192func (n nopHandler) WithGroup(string) slog.Handler           { return n }\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192var slogHandlerOpts = &slog.HandlerOptions{\\n    27\u2192\\tAddSource:   false,\\n    28\u2192\\tLevel:       slog.LevelInfo,\\n    29\u2192\\tReplaceAttr: replaceLevelAndDropTime,\\n    30\u2192}\\n    31\u2192\\n    32\u2192// Default slog logger. Will be overwritten once initializeSlog is called.\\n    33\u2192var DefaultSlogLogger *slog.Logger = slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(\\n    34\u2192\\tos.Stderr,\\n    35\u2192\\tslogHandlerOpts,\\n    36\u2192))\\n    37\u2192\\n    38\u2192func slogLevel(l logrus.Level) slog.Level {\\n    39\u2192\\tswitch l {\\n    40\u2192\\tcase logrus.DebugLevel, logrus.TraceLevel:\\n    41\u2192\\t\\treturn slog.LevelDebug\\n    42\u2192\\tcase logrus.InfoLevel:\\n    43\u2192\\t\\treturn slog.LevelInfo\\n    44\u2192\\tcase logrus.WarnLevel:\\n    45\u2192\\t\\treturn slog.LevelWarn\\n    46\u2192\\tcase logrus.ErrorLevel, logrus.PanicLevel, logrus.FatalLevel:\\n    47\u2192\\t\\treturn slog.LevelError\\n    48\u2192\\tdefault:\\n    49\u2192\\t\\treturn slog.LevelInfo\\n    50\u2192\\t}\\n    51\u2192}\\n    52\u2192\\n    53\u2192// Approximates the logrus output via slog for job groups during the transition\\n    54\u2192// phase.\\n    55\u2192func initializeSlog(logOpts LogOptions, useStdout bool) {\\n    56\u2192\\topts := *slogHandlerOpts\\n    57\u2192\\topts.Level = slogLevel(logOpts.GetLogLevel())\\n    58\u2192\\n    59\u2192\\tlogFormat := logOpts.GetLogFormat()\\n    60\u2192\\tswitch logFormat {\\n    61\u2192\\tcase LogFormatJSON, LogFormatText:\\n    62\u2192\\t\\topts.ReplaceAttr = replaceLevelAndDropTime\\n    63\u2192\\tcase LogFormatJSONTimestamp, LogFormatTextTimestamp:\\n    64\u2192\\t\\topts.ReplaceAttr = replaceLevel\\n    65\u2192\\t}\\n    66\u2192\\n    67\u2192\\twriter := os.Stderr\\n    68\u2192\\tif useStdout {\\n    69\u2192\\t\\twriter = os.Stdout\\n    70\u2192\\t}\\n    71\u2192\\n    72\u2192\\tswitch logFormat {\\n    73\u2192\\tcase LogFormatJSON, LogFormatJSONTimestamp:\\n    74\u2192\\t\\tDefaultSlogLogger = slog.New(slog.NewJSONHandler(\\n    75\u2192\\t\\t\\twriter,\\n    76\u2192\\t\\t\\t&opts,\\n    77\u2192\\t\\t))\\n    78\u2192\\tcase LogFormatText, LogFormatTextTimestamp:\\n    79\u2192\\t\\tDefaultSlogLogger = slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(\\n    80\u2192\\t\\t\\twriter,\\n    81\u2192\\t\\t\\t&opts,\\n    82\u2192\\t\\t))\\n    83\u2192\\t}\\n    84\u2192}\\n    85\u2192\\n    86\u2192func replaceLevel(groups []string, a slog.Attr) slog.Attr {\\n    87\u2192\\tswitch a.Key {\\n    88\u2192\\tcase slog.TimeKey:\\n    89\u2192\\t\\t// Adjust to timestamp format that logrus uses; except that we can't\\n    90\u2192\\t\\t// force slog to quote the value like logrus does...\\n    91\u2192\\t\\treturn slog.String(slog.TimeKey, a.Value.Time().Format(time.RFC3339))\\n    92\u2192\\tcase slog.LevelKey:\\n    93\u2192\\t\\t// Lower-case the log level\\n    94\u2192\\t\\treturn slog.Attr{\\n    95\u2192\\t\\t\\tKey:   a.Key,\\n    96\u2192\\t\\t\\tValue: slog.StringValue(strings.ToLower(a.Value.String())),\\n    97\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    98\u2192\\t}\\n    99\u2192\\treturn a\\n   100\u2192}\\n   101\u2192\\n   102\u2192func replaceLevelAndDropTime(groups []string, a slog.Attr) slog.Attr {\\n   103\u2192\\tswitch a.Key {\\n   104\u2192\\tcase slog.TimeKey:\\n   105\u2192\\t\\t// Drop timestamps\\n   106\u2192\\t\\treturn slog.Attr{}\\n   107\u2192\\tcase slog.LevelKey:\\n   108\u2192\\t\\t// Lower-case the log level\\n   109\u2192\\t\\treturn slog.Attr{\\n   110\u2192\\t\\t\\tKey:   a.Key,\\n   111\u2192\\t\\t\\tValue: slog.StringValue(strings.ToLower(a.Value.String())),\\n   112\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   113\u2192\\t}\\n   114\u2192\\treturn a\\n   115\u2192}\\n   116\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"ab6f1ff0-9477-4e82-bfc7-0d1390ee6512\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:32.639Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"0715f02f-1e07-4a3d-8191-7021d43ad310\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"ab6f1ff0-9477-4e82-bfc7-0d1390ee6512\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01DdTfcoNonHThcVVxJtQ6xn\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01DdTfcoNonHThcVVxJtQ6xn\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:32.639Z\",\"uuid\":\"4be4f541-aea5-4a36-90f6-f0fee0331042\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"57d7461d-6e21-46b0-97b5-33f79551008f\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01Fr2EFHZ8Sv1hNw7cuPSgFE\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"c9278e64-942d-4ffa-8cda-41cb4491f57d\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:32.643Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"57d7461d-6e21-46b0-97b5-33f79551008f\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"c9278e64-942d-4ffa-8cda-41cb4491f57d\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_0156PdnnuKraJDCxZo2QZawx\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Let     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192package policy\\n     5\u2192\\n     6\u2192import (\\n     7\u2192\\t\\\"io\\\"\\n     8\u2192\\tstdlog \\\"log\\\"\\n     9\u2192\\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n    10\u2192\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n    11\u2192\\n    12\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/api/v1/models\\\"\\n    13\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/labels\\\"\\n    14\u2192)\\n    15\u2192\\n    16\u2192type Tracing int\\n    17\u2192\\n    18\u2192const (\\n    19\u2192\\tTRACE_DISABLED Tracing = iota\\n    20\u2192\\tTRACE_ENABLED\\n    21\u2192\\tTRACE_VERBOSE\\n    22\u2192)\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192// TraceEnabled returns true if the SearchContext requests tracing.\\n    25\u2192func (s *SearchContext) TraceEnabled() bool {\\n    26\u2192\\treturn s.Trace != TRACE_DISABLED\\n    27\u2192}\\n    28\u2192\\n    29\u2192// PolicyTrace logs the given message into the SearchContext logger only if\\n    30\u2192// TRACE_ENABLED or TRACE_VERBOSE is enabled in the receiver's SearchContext.\\n    31\u2192func (s *SearchContext) PolicyTrace(format string, a ...interface{}) {\\n    32\u2192\\tif s.TraceEnabled() {\\n    33\u2192\\t\\tlog.Debugf(format, a...)\\n    34\u2192\\t\\tif s.Logging != nil {\\n    35\u2192\\t\\t\\tformat = \\\"%-\\\" + s.CallDepth() + \\\"s\\\" + format\\n    36\u2192\\t\\t\\ta = append([]interface{}{\\\"\\\"}, a...)\\n    37\u2192\\t\\t\\ts.Logging.Printf(format, a...)\\n    38\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    39\u2192\\t}\\n    40\u2192}\\n    41\u2192\\n    42\u2192// PolicyTraceVerbose logs the given message into the SearchContext logger only\\n    43\u2192// if TRACE_VERBOSE is enabled in the receiver's SearchContext.\\n    44\u2192func (s *SearchContext) PolicyTraceVerbose(format string, a ...interface{}) {\\n    45\u2192\\tswitch s.Trace {\\n    46\u2192\\tcase TRACE_VERBOSE:\\n    47\u2192\\t\\tlog.Debugf(format, a...)\\n    48\u2192\\t\\tif s.Logging != nil {\\n    49\u2192\\t\\t\\ts.Logging.Printf(format, a...)\\n    50\u2192\\t\\t}\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code (\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"d7a8339d-eced-4608-8cd3-c1521c1bb7ba\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:38.648Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"fe1c4a99-d32f-4f56-a7ec-3bf95d5e1fe2\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"d7a8339d-eced-4608-8cd3-c1521c1bb7ba\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01ArkiU82mTEniavxnzHq8v3\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01RUnTr3Cz7GtxZ58iswNFLD\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/pkg/policy/resolve.go\",\"limit\":60},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":\"tool_use\",\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":4,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":167,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":48564,\"output_tokens\":79,\"server_tool_use\":{\"web_search_requests\":0,\"web_fetch_requests\":0},\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":167,\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0},\"inference_geo\":\"\",\"iterations\":[],\"speed\":\"standard\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYf38GjhYk4kxZuvsuw62\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"0d3a82c8-5ee1-4ddd-a2c3-722f61ab6c5f\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:40.567Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"0d3a82c8-5ee1-4ddd-a2c3-722f61ab6c5f\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01RUnTr3Cz7GtxZ58iswNFLD\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192package policy\\n     5\u2192\\n     6\u2192import (\\n     7\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\\\"\\n     8\u2192)\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192// selectorPolicy is a structure which contains the resolved policy for a\\n    11\u2192// particular Identity across all layers (L3, L4, and L7), with the policy\\n    12\u2192// still determined in terms of EndpointSelectors.\\n    13\u2192type selectorPolicy struct {\\n    14\u2192\\t// Revision is the revision of the policy repository used to generate\\n    15\u2192\\t// this selectorPolicy.\\n    16\u2192\\tRevision uint64\\n    17\u2192\\n    18\u2192\\t// SelectorCache managing selectors in L4Policy\\n    19\u2192\\tSelectorCache *SelectorCache\\n    20\u2192\\n    21\u2192\\t// L4Policy contains the computed L4 and L7 policy.\\n    22\u2192\\tL4Policy L4Policy\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192\\t// IngressPolicyEnabled specifies whether this policy contains any policy\\n    25\u2192\\t// at ingress.\\n    26\u2192\\tIngressPolicyEnabled bool\\n    27\u2192\\n    28\u2192\\t// EgressPolicyEnabled specifies whether this policy contains any policy\\n    29\u2192\\t// at egress.\\n    30\u2192\\tEgressPolicyEnabled bool\\n    31\u2192}\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192func (p *selectorPolicy) Attach(ctx PolicyContext) {\\n    34\u2192\\tp.L4Policy.Attach(ctx)\\n    35\u2192}\\n    36\u2192\\n    37\u2192// EndpointPolicy is a structure which contains the resolved policy across all\\n    38\u2192// layers (L3, L4, and L7), distilled against a set of identities.\\n    39\u2192type EndpointPolicy struct {\\n    40\u2192\\t// Note that all Endpoints sharing the same identity will be\\n    41\u2192\\t// referring to a shared selectorPolicy!\\n    42\u2192\\t*selectorPolicy\\n    43\u2192\\n    44\u2192\\t// policyMapState contains the state of this policy as it relates to the\\n    45\u2192\\t// datapath. In the future, this will be factored out of this object to\\n    46\u2192\\t// decouple the policy as it relates to the datapath vs. its userspace\\n    47\u2192\\t// representation.\\n    48\u2192\\t// It maps each Key to the proxy port if proxy redirection is needed.\\n    49\u2192\\t// Proxy port 0 indicates no proxy redirection.\\n    50\u2192\\t// All fields within the Key and the proxy port must be in host byte-order.\\n    51\u2192\\t// Must only be accessed with PolicyOwner (aka Endpoint) lock taken.\\n    52\u2192\\tpolicyMapState MapState\\n    53\u2192\\n    54\u2192\\t// policyMapChanges collects pending changes to the PolicyMapState\\n    55\u2192\\tpolicyMapChanges MapChanges\\n    56\u2192\\n    57\u2192\\t// PolicyOwner describes any type which consumes this EndpointPolicy object.\\n    58\u2192\\tPolicyOwner PolicyOwner\\n    59\u2192}\\n    60\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"8c766f01-9b02-4cf8-b269-0636baa80d03\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:40.596Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"0d3a82c8-5ee1-4ddd-a2c3-722f61ab6c5f\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"8c766f01-9b02-4cf8-b269-0636baa80d03\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01RUnTr3Cz7GtxZ58iswNFLD\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01RUnTr3Cz7GtxZ58iswNFLD\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:40.596Z\",\"uuid\":\"43893a13-adfc-428c-880d-ede26455a6f9\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"43893a13-adfc-428c-880d-ede26455a6f9\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01U3U1B9sRR3ntir85igf5g9\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Let     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192package policy\\n     5\u2192\\n     6\u2192import (\\n     7\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n     8\u2192\\t\\\"net\\\"\\n     9\u2192\\t\\\"slices\\\"\\n    10\u2192\\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n    11\u2192\\n    12\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/go-hclog\\\"\\n    13\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\\\"\\n    14\u2192\\t\\\"golang.org/x/exp/maps\\\"\\n    15\u2192\\n    16\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/container/bitlpm\\\"\\n    17\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\\\"\\n    18\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/ip\\\"\\n    19\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/labels\\\"\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/option\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/policy/trafficdirection\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\tpolicyTypes \\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/policy/types\\\"\\n    25\u2192)\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192// Key and Keys are types used both internally and externally.\\n    28\u2192// The types have been lifted out, but an alias is being used\\n    29\u2192// so we don't have to change all the code everywhere.\\n    30\u2192//\\n    31\u2192// Do not use these types outside of pkg/policy or pkg/endpoint,\\n    32\u2192// lest ye find yourself with hundreds of unnecessary imports.\\n    33\u2192type Key = policyTypes.Key\\n    34\u2192type Keys = policyTypes.Keys\\n    35\u2192\\n    36\u2192var (\\n    37\u2192\\t// localHostKey represents an ingress L3 allow from the local host.\\n    38\u2192\\tlocalHostKey = Key{\\n    39\u2192\\t\\tIdentity:         identity.ReservedIdentityHost.Uint32(),\\n    40\u2192\\t\\tInvertedPortMask: 0xffff, // This is a wildcard\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. 7\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n     8\u2192\\t\\\"io\\\"\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\\\"\\n    11\u2192\\n    12\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\\\"\\n    13\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock\\\"\\n    14\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\\\"\\n    15\u2192)\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192// PolicyAuditLogger provides structured logging of policy evaluation decisions\\n    18\u2192// for audit and debugging purposes.\\n    19\u2192type PolicyAuditLogger struct {\\n    20\u2192\\tmutex      lock.Mutex\\n    21\u2192\\tlogger     *logrus.Entry\\n    22\u2192\\tlogLevel   logrus.Level\\n    23\u2192\\tauditLog   *logrus.Logger\\n    24\u2192\\tauditEntry *logrus.Entry\\n    25\u2192}\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192// NewPolicyAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with the specified configuration.\\n    28\u2192// If auditOutput is provided, audit logs will be written to it in addition to the standard logger.\\n    29\u2192func NewPolicyAuditLogger(logLevel logrus.Level, auditOutput io.Writer) *PolicyAuditLogger {\\n    30\u2192\\tlogger := log.WithField(\\\"component\\\", \\\"audit\\\")\\n    31\u2192\\n    32\u2192\\tpal := &PolicyAuditLogger{\\n    33\u2192\\t\\tlogger:   logger,\\n    34\u2192\\t\\tlogLevel: logLevel,\\n    35\u2192\\t}\\n    36\u2192\\n    37\u2192\\t// If auditOutput is provided, create a separate audit logger\\n    38\u2192\\tif auditOutput != nil {\\n    39\u2192\\t\\tauditLog := logrus.New()\\n    40\u2192\\t\\tauditLog.SetFormatter(&logrus.JSONFormatter{})\\n    41\u2192\\t\\tauditLog.SetOutput(auditOutput)\\n    42\u2192\\t\\tauditLog.SetLevel(logLevel)\\n    43\u2192\\t\\tpal.auditLog = auditLog\\n    44\u2192\\t\\tpal.auditEntry = auditLog.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, \\\"policy-audit\\\")\\n    45\u2192\\t}\\n    46\u2192\\n    47\u2192\\treturn pal\\n    48\u2192}\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192// LogDecision logs a policy decision with the given parameters.\\n    51\u2192// This is used for audit trail of policy verdicts.\\n    52\u2192func (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogDecision(\\n    53\u2192\\tidentity identity.NumericIdentity,\\n    54\u2192\\tpolicy string,\\n    55\u2192\\tverdict string,\\n    56\u2192\\treason string,\\n    57\u2192) {\\n    58\u2192\\tpal.mutex.Lock()\\n    59\u2192\\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\\n    60\u2192\\n    61\u2192\\tfields := logrus.Fields{\\n    62\u2192\\t\\tlogfields.Identity: identity,\\n    63\u2192\\t\\t\\\"policy\\\":           policy,\\n    64\u2192\\t\\t\\\"verdict\\\":          verdict,\\n    65\u2192\\t\\t\\\"reason\\\":           reason,\\n    66\u2192\\t}\\n    67\u2192\\n    68\u2192\\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\\n    69\u2192\\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\\n    70\u2192\\t\\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\\n    71\u2192\\t\\tswitch verdict {\\n    72\u2192\\t\\tcase \\\"allow\\\":\\n    73\u2192\\t\\t\\tentry.Debug(\\\"Policy decision: allow\\\")\\n    74\u2192\\t\\tcase \\\"deny\\\":\\n    75\u2192\\t\\t\\tentry.Warning(\\\"Policy decision: deny\\\")\\n    76\u2192\\t\\tdefault:\\n    77\u2192\\t\\t\\tentry.Debug(\\\"Policy decision\\\")\\n    78\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    79\u2192\\t}\\n    80\u2192\\n    81\u2192\\t// Log to audit logger if configured\\n    82\u2192\\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\\n    83\u2192\\t\\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\\\"policy-decision\\\")\\n    84\u2192\\t}\\n    85\u2192}\\n    86\u2192\\n    87\u2192// LogEvaluation logs a policy evaluation with full context.\\n    88\u2192// This provides detailed information about how a policy was evaluated against identities.\\n    89\u2192func (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogEvaluation(\\n    90\u2192\\tctx context.Context,\\n    91\u2192\\tpolicyKey string,\\n    92\u2192\\tselectorCache *SelectorCache,\\n    93\u2192\\tresult bool,\\n    94\u2192) {\\n    95\u2192\\tpal.mutex.Lock()\\n    96\u2192\\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\\n    97\u2192\\n    98\u2192\\tfields := logrus.Fields{\\n    99\u2192\\t\\tlogfields.PolicyKey: policyKey,\\n   100\u2192\\t\\t\\\"result\\\":            result,\\n   101\u2192\\t}\\n   102\u2192\\n   103\u2192\\t// Add selector cache info if available\\n   104\u2192\\tif selectorCache != nil {\\n   105\u2192\\t\\t// Get the model representation for audit purposes\\n   106\u2192\\t\\tmodel := selectorCache.GetModel()\\n   107\u2192\\t\\tfields[\\\"selectors_count\\\"] = len(model)\\n   108\u2192\\t}\\n   109\u2192\\n   110\u2192\\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\\n   111\u2192\\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\\n   112\u2192\\t\\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\\n   113\u2192\\t\\tif result {\\n   114\u2192\\t\\t\\tentry.Debug(\\\"Policy evaluation completed: pass\\\")\\n   115\u2192\\t\\t} else {\\n   116\u2192\\t\\t\\tentry.Debug(\\\"Policy evaluation completed: fail\\\")\\n   117\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   118\u2192\\t}\\n   119\u2192\\n   120\u2192\\t// Log to audit logger if configured\\n   121\u2192\\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\\n   122\u2192\\t\\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\\\"policy-evaluation\\\")\\n   123\u2192\\t}\\n   124\u2192}\\n   125\u2192\\n   126\u2192// SetLogLevel updates the log level for both standard and audit loggers.\\n   127\u2192func (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) SetLogLevel(level logrus.Level) {\\n   128\u2192\\tpal.mutex.Lock()\\n   129\u2192\\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192\\tpal.logLevel = level\\n   132\u2192\\tif pal.auditLog != nil {\\n   133\u2192\\t\\tpal.auditLog.SetLevel(level)\\n   134\u2192\\t}\\n   135\u2192}\\n   136\u2192\\n   137\u2192// GetLogLevel returns the current log level.\\n   138\u2192func (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) GetLogLevel() logrus.Level {\\n   139\u2192\\tpal.mutex.Lock()\\n   140\u2192\\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\\n   141\u2192\\treturn pal.logLevel\\n   142\u2192}\\n   143\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code.     "prompt": "Review the following Cilium policy audit logger implementation for code reuse. Search the codebase for existing utilities, helpers, and patterns that could replace or improve the new code.\n\n## Files to Review:\n\n### audit_logger.go\n```go\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n\npackage policy\n\nimport (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"io\"\n\n\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\"\n)\n\n// PolicyAuditLogger provides structured logging of policy evaluation decisions\n// for audit and debugging purposes.\ntype PolicyAuditLogger struct {\n\tmutex      lock.Mutex\n\tlogger     *logrus.Entry\n\tlogLevel   logrus.Level\n\tauditLog   *logrus.Logger\n\tauditEntry *logrus.Entry\n}\n\n// NewPolicyAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with the specified configuration.\n// If auditOutput is provided, audit logs will be written to it in addition to the standard logger.\nfunc NewPolicyAuditLogger(logLevel logrus.Level, auditOutput io.Writer) *PolicyAuditLogger {\n\tlogger := log.WithField(\"component\", \"audit\")\n\n\tpal := &PolicyAuditLogger{\n\t\tlogger:   logger,\n\t\tlogLevel: logLevel,\n\t}\n\n\t// If auditOutput is provided, create a separate audit logger\n\tif auditOutput != nil {\n\t\tauditLog := logrus.New()\n\t\tauditLog.SetFormatter(&logrus.JSONFormatter{})\n\t\tauditLog.SetOutput(auditOutput)\n\t\tauditLog.SetLevel(logLevel)\n\t\tpal.auditLog = auditLog\n\t\tpal.auditEntry = auditLog.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, \"policy-audit\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn pal\n}\n\n// LogDecision logs a policy decision with the given parameters.\n// This is used for audit trail of policy verdicts.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogDecision(\n\tidentity identity.NumericIdentity,\n\tpolicy string,\n\tverdict string,\n\treason string,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.Identity: identity,\n\t\t\"policy\":           policy,\n\t\t\"verdict\":          verdict,\n\t\t\"reason\":           reason,\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\n\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\n\t\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\t\tswitch verdict {\n\t\tcase \"allow\":\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision: allow\")\n\t\tcase \"deny\":\n\t\t\tentry.Warning(\"Policy decision: deny\")\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to audit logger if configured\n\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\"policy-decision\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// LogEvaluation logs a policy evaluation with full context.\n// This provides detailed information about how a policy was evaluated against identities.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogEvaluation(\n\tctx context.Context,\n\tpolicyKey string,\n\tselectorCache *SelectorCache,\n\tresult bool,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.PolicyKey: policyKey,\n\t\t\"result\":            result,\n\t}\n\n\t// Add selector cache info if available\n\tif selectorCache != nil {\n\t\t// Get the model representation for audit purposes\n\t\tmodel := selectorCache.GetModel()\n\t\tfields[\"selectors_count\"] = len(model)\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\n\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\n\t\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\t\tif result {\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy evaluation completed: pass\")\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy evaluation completed: fail\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to audit logger if configured\n\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\"policy-evaluation\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// SetLogLevel updates the log level for both standard and audit loggers.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) SetLogLevel(level logrus.Level) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tpal.logLevel = level\n\tif pal.auditLog != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditLog.SetLevel(level)\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetLogLevel returns the current log level.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) GetLogLevel() logrus.Level {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\treturn pal.logLevel\n}\n```\n\n## Analysis Tasks:\n\n1. **Search for existing logging patterns** in pkg/policy/ and related packages for audit/debug logging that might already exist\n2. **Check for existing lock patterns** - are there standardized ways Cilium wraps loggers with thread-safety?\n3. **Look for existing JSON formatter configurations** - how does Cilium handle JSON audit logs elsewhere?\n4. **Check for existing verdict/decision logging patterns** - similar audit trails in the codebase?\n5. **Identify any utility functions** for setting up dual loggers (one JSON, one text)\n\nFor each finding, suggest:\n- The existing function/pattern to use instead\n- How it would simplify the code\n- File locations and line numbers\n\nReturn specific line numbers and suggestions for improvement.",

Search the codebase for existing utilities, helpers, and patterns that could replace or improve the new code.\\n\\n## Files to Review:\\n\\n### audit_logger.go\\n```go\\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\n// Copyright Authors of Cilium\\n\\npackage policy\\n\\nimport (\\n\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n\\t\\\"io\\\"\\n\\n\\t\\\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\\\"\\n\\n\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\\\"\\n\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock\\\"\\n\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\\\"\\n)\\n\\n// PolicyAuditLogger provides structured logging of policy evaluation decisions\\n// for audit and debugging purposes.\\ntype PolicyAuditLogger struct {\\n\\tmutex      lock.Mutex\\n\\tlogger     *logrus.Entry\\n\\tlogLevel   logrus.Level\\n\\tauditLog   *logrus.Logger\\n\\tauditEntry *logrus.Entry\\n}\\n\\n// NewPolicyAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with the specified configuration.\\n// If auditOutput is provided, audit logs will be written to it in addition to the standard logger.\\nfunc NewPolicyAuditLogger(logLevel logrus.Level, auditOutput io.Writer) *PolicyAuditLogger {\\n\\tlogger := log.WithField(\\\"component\\\", \\\"audit\\\")\\n\\n\\tpal := &PolicyAuditLogger{\\n\\t\\tlogger:   logger,\\n\\t\\tlogLevel: logLevel,\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// If auditOutput is provided, create a separate audit logger\\n\\tif auditOutput != nil {\\n\\t\\tauditLog := logrus.New()\\n\\t\\tauditLog.SetFormatter(&logrus.JSONFormatter{})\\n\\t\\tauditLog.SetOutput(auditOutput)\\n\\t\\tauditLog.SetLevel(logLevel)\\n\\t\\tpal.auditLog = auditLog\\n\\t\\tpal.auditEntry = auditLog.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, \\\"policy-audit\\\")\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\treturn pal\\n}\\n\\n// LogDecision logs a policy decision with the given parameters.\\n// This is used for audit trail of policy verdicts.\\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogDecision(\\n\\tidentity identity.NumericIdentity,\\n\\tpolicy string,\\n\\tverdict string,\\n\\treason string,\\n) {\\n\\tpal.mutex.Lock()\\n\\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\\n\\n\\tfields := logrus.Fields{\\n\\t\\tlogfields.Identity: identity,\\n\\t\\t\\\"policy\\\":           policy,\\n\\t\\t\\\"verdict\\\":          verdict,\\n\\t\\t\\\"reason\\\":           reason,\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\\n\\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\\n\\t\\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\\n\\t\\tswitch verdict {\\n\\t\\tcase \\\"allow\\\":\\n\\t\\t\\tentry.Debug(\\\"Policy decision: allow\\\")\\n\\t\\tcase \\\"deny\\\":\\n\\t\\t\\tentry.Warning(\\\"Policy decision: deny\\\")\\n\\t\\tdefault:\\n\\t\\t\\tentry.Debug(\\\"Policy decision\\\")\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// Log to audit logger if configured\\n\\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\\n\\t\\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\\\"policy-decision\\\")\\n\\t}\\n}\\n\\n// LogEvaluation logs a policy evaluation with full context.\\n// This provides detailed information about how a policy was evaluated against identities.\\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogEvaluation(\\n\\tctx context.Context,\\n\\tpolicyKey string,\\n\\tselectorCache *SelectorCache,\\n\\tresult bool,\\n) {\\n\\tpal.mutex.Lock()\\n\\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\\n\\n\\tfields := logrus.Fields{\\n\\t\\tlogfields.PolicyKey: policyKey,\\n\\t\\t\\\"result\\\":            result,\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// Add selector cache info if available\\n\\tif selectorCache != nil {\\n\\t\\t// Get the model representation for audit purposes\\n\\t\\tmodel := selectorCache.GetModel()\\n\\t\\tfields[\\\"selectors_count\\\"] = len(model)\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// Log to standard logger if enabled\\n\\tif pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {\\n\\t\\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\\n\\t\\tif result {\\n\\t\\t\\tentry.Debug(\\\"Policy evaluation completed: pass\\\")\\n\\t\\t} else {\\n\\t\\t\\tentry.Debug(\\\"Policy evaluation completed: fail\\\")\\n\\t\\t}\\n\\t}\\n\\n\\t// Log to audit logger if configured\\n\\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\\n\\t\\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(\\\"policy-evaluation\\\")\\n\\t}\\n}\\n\\n// SetLogLevel updates the log level for both standard and audit loggers.\\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) SetLogLevel(level logrus.Level) {\\n\\tpal.mutex.Lock()\\n\\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\\n\\n\\tpal.logLevel = level\\n\\tif pal.auditLog != nil {\\n\\t\\tpal.auditLog.SetLevel(level)\\n\\t}\\n}\\n\\n// GetLogLevel returns the current log level.\\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) GetLogLevel() logrus.Level {\\n\\tpal.mutex.Lock()\\n\\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\\n\\treturn pal.logLevel\\n}\\n```\\n\\n## Analysis Tasks:\\n\\n1. **Search for existing logging patterns** in pkg/policy/ and related packages for audit/debug logging that might already exist\\n2. **Check for existing lock patterns** - are there standardized ways Cilium wraps loggers with thread-safety?\\n3. **Look for existing JSON formatter configurations** - how does QkVKiTM6QByBh8rfzWK\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01MxoNqFxuUkyeGeQp6bW2eF\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/pkg/endpoint/log.go\"},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":null,\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":802,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":631,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":14699,\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0,\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":631},\"output_tokens\":3,\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"inference_geo\":\"not_available\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYf36MPwFHsW24NpWrMMp\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"16e98891-7881-422e-8365-a67aed4eaa58\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:14.219Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"16e98891-7881-422e-8365-a67aed4eaa58\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01HNtQkVKiTM6QByBh8rfzWK\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01AAE9bAZj8mjFLv3z2A9V6F\",\"name\":\"Grep\",\"input\":{\"pattern\":\"WithField|WithFields|WithContext\",\"path\":\"/workspace/pkg/policy\",\"type\":\"go\",\"output_mode\":\"content\",\"head_limit\":50},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":\"tool_use\",\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":802,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":631,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":14699,\"output_tokens\":242,\"server_tool_use\":{\"web_search_requests\":0,\"web_fetch_requests\":0},\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":631,\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0},\"inference_geo\":\"\",\"iterations\":[],\"speed\":\"standard\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYf36MPwFHsW24NpWrMMp\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"5d7bb77e-e918-419e-8575-21de3026b8a8\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:14.752Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"8ac8dd58-033a-49cc-a53b-75fd72849398\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01A4eQbfc1k7ddP1iGr2pw7t\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192package logging\\n     5\u2192\\n     6\u2192import (\\n     7\u2192\\t\\\"bufio\\\"\\n     8\u2192\\t\\\"bytes\\\"\\n     9\u2192\\t\\\"flag\\\"\\n    10\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    11\u2192\\t\\\"os\\\"\\n    12\u2192\\t\\\"regexp\\\"\\n    13\u2192\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n    14\u2192\\t\\\"sync/atomic\\\"\\n    15\u2192\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\\\"\\n    18\u2192\\t\\\"k8s.io/klog/v2\\\"\\n    19\u2192\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\\\"\\n    21\u2192)\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192type LogFormat string\\n    24\u2192\\n    25\u2192const (\\n    26\u2192\\tSyslog    = \\\"syslog\\\"\\n    27\u2192\\tLevelOpt  = \\\"level\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\tFormatOpt = \\\"format\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\n    30\u2192\\tLogFormatText          LogFormat = \\\"text\\\"\\n    31\u2192\\tLogFormatTextTimestamp LogFormat = \\\"text-ts\\\"\\n    32\u2192\\tLogFormatJSON          LogFormat = \\\"json\\\"\\n    33\u2192\\tLogFormatJSONTimestamp LogFormat = \\\"json-ts\\\"\\n    34\u2192\\n    35\u2192\\t// DefaultLogFormat is the string representation of the default logrus.Formatter\\n    36\u2192\\t// we want to use (possible values: text or json)\\n    37\u2192\\tDefaultLogFormat LogFormat = LogFormatText\\n    38\u2192\\n    39\u2192\\t// DefaultLogFormatTimestamp is the string representation of the default logrus.Formatter\\n    40\u2192\\t// including timestamps.\\n    41\u2192\\t// We don't use this for general runtime logs since kubernetes log capture handles those.\\n    42\u2192\\t// This is only used for applications such as CNI which is written to disk so we have no\\n    43\u2192\\t// way to correlate with other logs.\\n    44\u2192\\tDefaultLogFormatTimestamp LogFormat = LogFormatTextTimestamp\\n    45\u2192\\n    46\u2192\\t// DefaultLogLevel is the default log level we want to use for our logrus.Formatter\\n    47\u2192\\tDefaultLogLevel logrus.Level = logrus.InfoLevel\\n    48\u2192)\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192// DefaultLogger is the base logrus logger. It is different from the logrus\\n    51\u2192// default to avoid external dependencies from writing out unexpectedly\\n    52\u2192var DefaultLogger = initializeDefaultLogger()\\n    53\u2192\\n    54\u2192func initializeKLog() {\\n    55\u2192\\tlog := DefaultLogger.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, \\\"klog\\\")\\n    56\u2192\\n    57\u2192\\t//Create a new flag set and set error handler\\n    58\u2192\\tklogFlags := flag.NewFlagSet(\\\"cilium\\\", flag.ExitOnError)\\n    59\u2192\\n    60\u2192\\t// Make sure that klog logging variables are initialized so that we can\\n    61\u2192\\t// update them from this file.\\n    62\u2192\\tklog.InitFlags(klogFlags)\\n    63\u2192\\n    64\u2192\\t// Make sure klog does not log to stderr as we want it to control the output\\n    65\u2192\\t// of klog so we want klog to log the errors to each writer of each level.\\n    66\u2192\\tklogFlags.Set(\\\"logtostderr\\\", \\\"false\\\")\\n    67\u2192\\n    68\u2192\\t// We don't need all headers because logrus will already print them if\\n    69\u2192\\t// necessary.\\n    70\u2192\\tklogFlags.Set(\\\"skip_headers\\\", \\\"true\\\")\\n    71\u2192\\n    72\u2192\\tklog.SetOutputBySeverity(\\\"INFO\\\", log.WriterLevel(logrus.InfoLevel))\\n    73\u2192\\tklog.SetOutputBySeverity(\\\"WARNING\\\", log.WriterLevel(logrus.WarnLevel))\\n    74\u2192\\tklog.SetOutputBySeverity(\\\"ERROR\\\", log.WriterLevel(logrus.ErrorLevel))\\n    75\u2192\\tklog.SetOutputBySeverity(\\\"FATAL\\\", log.WriterLevel(logrus.FatalLevel))\\n    76\u2192\\n    77\u2192\\t// Do not repeat log messages on all severities in klog\\n    78\u2192\\tklogFlags.Set(\\\"one_output\\\", \\\"true\\\")\\n    79\u2192}\\n    80\u2192\\n    81\u2192// LogOptions maps configuration key-value pairs related to logging.\\n    82\u2192type LogOptions map[string]string\\n    83\u2192\\n    84\u2192// initializeDefaultLogger returns a logrus Logger with the default logging\\n    85\u2192// settings.\\n    86\u2192func initializeDefaultLogger() (logger *logrus.Logger) {\\n    87\u2192\\tlogger = logrus.New()\\n    88\u2192\\tlogger.SetFormatter(GetFormatter(DefaultLogFormatTimestamp))\\n    89\u2192\\tlogger.SetLevel(DefaultLogLevel)\\n    90\u2192\\treturn\\n    91\u2192}\\n    92\u2192\\n    93\u2192// GetLogLevel returns the log level specified in the provided LogOptions. If\\n    94\u2192// it is not set in the options, it will return the default level.\\n    95\u2192func (o LogOptions) GetLogLevel() (level logrus.Level) {\\n    96\u2192\\tlevelOpt, ok := o[LevelOpt]\\n    97\u2192\\tif !ok {\\n    98\u2192\\t\\treturn DefaultLogLevel\\n    99\u2192\\t}\\n   100\u2192\\n   101\u2192\\tvar err error\\n   102\u2192\\tif level, err = logrus.ParseLevel(levelOpt); err != nil {\\n   103\u2192\\t\\tlogrus.WithError(err).Warning(\\\"Ignoring user-configured log level\\\")\\n   104\u2192\\t\\treturn DefaultLogLevel\\n   105\u2192\\t}\\n   106\u2192\\n   107\u2192\\treturn\\n   108\u2192}\\n   109\u2192\\n   110\u2192// GetLogFormat returns the log format specified in the provided LogOptions. If\\n   111\u2192// it is not set in the options or is invalid, it will return the default format.\\n   112\u2192func (o LogOptions) GetLogFormat() LogFormat {\\n   113\u2192\\tformatOpt, ok := o[FormatOpt]\\n   114\u2192\\tif !ok {\\n   115\u2192\\t\\treturn DefaultLogFormatTimestamp\\n   116\u2192\\t}\\n   117\u2192\\n   118\u2192\\tformatOpt = strings.ToLower(formatOpt)\\n   119\u2192\\tre := regexp.MustCompile(`^(text|text-ts|json|json-ts)$`)\\n   120\u2192\\tif !re.MatchString(formatOpt) {\\n   121\u2192\\t\\tlogrus.WithError(\\n   122\u2192\\t\\t\\tfmt.Errorf(\\\"incorrect log format configured '%s', expected 'text', 'text-ts', 'json' or 'json-ts'\\\", formatOpt),\\n   123\u2192\\t\\t).Warning(\\\"Ignoring user-configured log format\\\")\\n   124\u2192\\t\\treturn DefaultLogFormatTimestamp\\n   125\u2192\\t}\\n   126\u2192\\n   127\u2192\\treturn LogFormat(formatOpt)\\n   128\u2192}\\n   129\u2192\\n   130\u2192// SetLogLevel updates the DefaultLogger with a new logrus.Level\\n   131\u2192func SetLogLevel(logLevel logrus.Level) {\\n   132\u2192\\tDefaultLogger.SetLevel(logLevel)\\n   133\u2192}\\n   134\u2192\\n   135\u2192// SetDefaultLogLevel updates the DefaultLogger with the DefaultLogLevel\\n   136\u2192func SetDefaultLogLevel() {\\n   137\u2192\\tDefaultLogger.SetLevel(DefaultLogLevel)\\n   138\u2192}\\n   139\u2192\\n   140\u2192// SetLogLevelToDebug updates the DefaultLogger with the logrus.DebugLevel\\n   141\u2192func SetLogLevelToDebug() {\\n   142\u2192\\tDefaultLogger.SetLevel(logrus.DebugLevel)\\n   143\u2192}\\n   144\u2192\\n   145\u2192// SetLogFormat updates the DefaultLogger with a new LogFormat\\n   146\u2192func SetLogFormat(logFormat LogFormat) {\\n   147\u2192\\tDefaultLogger.SetFormatter(GetFormatter(logFormat))\\n   148\u2192}\\n   149\u2192\\n   150\u2192// SetDefaultLogFormat updates the DefaultLogger with the DefaultLogFormat\\n   151\u2192func SetDefaultLogFormat() {\\n   152\u2192\\tDefaultLogger.SetFormatter(GetFormatter(DefaultLogFormatTimestamp))\\n   153\u2192}\\n   154\u2192\\n   155\u2192// AddHooks adds additional logrus hook to default logger\\n   156\u2192func AddHooks(hooks ...logrus.Hook) {\\n   157\u2192\\tfor _, hook := range hooks {\\n   158\u2192\\t\\tDefaultLogger.AddHook(hook)\\n   159\u2192\\t}\\n   160\u2192}\\n   161\u2192\\n   162\u2192// SetupLogging sets up each logging service provided in loggers and configures\\n   163\u2192// each logger with the provided logOpts.\\n   164\u2192func SetupLogging(loggers []string, logOpts LogOptions, tag string, debug bool) error {\\n   165\u2192\\t// Bridge klog to logrus. Note that this will open multiple pipes and fork\\n   166\u2192\\t// background goroutines that are not cleaned up.\\n   167\u2192\\tinitializeKLog()\\n   168\u2192\\n   169\u2192\\tif debug {\\n   170\u2192\\t\\tlogOpts[LevelOpt] = \\\"debug\\\"\\n   171\u2192\\t}\\n   172\u2192\\tinitializeSlog(logOpts, len(loggers) == 0)\\n   173\u2192\\n   174\u2192\\t// Updating the default log format\\n   175\u2192\\tSetLogFormat(logOpts.GetLogFormat())\\n   176\u2192\\n   177\u2192\\t// Set default logger to output to stdout if no loggers are provided.\\n   178\u2192\\tif len(loggers) == 0 {\\n   179\u2192\\t\\t// TODO: switch to a per-logger version when we upgrade to logrus >1.0.3\\n   180\u2192\\t\\tlogrus.SetOutput(os.Stdout)\\n   181\u2192\\t}\\n   182\u2192\\n   183\u2192\\t// Updating the default log level, overriding the log options if the debug arg is being set\\n   184\u2192\\tif debug {\\n   185\u2192\\t\\tSetLogLevelToDebug()\\n   186\u2192\\t} else {\\n   187\u2192\\t\\tSetLogLevel(logOpts.GetLogLevel())\\n   188\u2192\\t}\\n   189\u2192\\n   190\u2192\\t// always suppress the default logger so libraries don't print things\\n   191\u2192\\tlogrus.SetLevel(logrus.PanicLevel)\\n   192\u2192\\n   193\u2192\\t// Iterate through all provided loggers and configure them according\\n   194\u2192\\t// to user-provided settings.\\n   195\u2192\\tfor _, logger := range loggers {\\n   196\u2192\\t\\tswitch logger {\\n   197\u2192\\t\\tcase Syslog:\\n   198\u2192\\t\\t\\tif err := setupSyslog(logOpts, tag, debug); err != nil {\\n   199\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to set up syslog: %w\\\", err)\\n   200\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   201\u2192\\t\\tdefault:\\n   202\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"provided log driver %q is not a supported log driver\\\", logger)\\n   203\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   204\u2192\\t}\\n   205\u2192\\n   206\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   207\u2192}\\n   208\u2192\\n   209\u2192// GetFormatter returns a configured logrus.Formatter with some specific values\\n   210\u2192// we want to have\\n   211\u2192func GetFormatter(format LogFormat) logrus.Formatter {\\n   212\u2192\\tswitch format {\\n   213\u2192\\tcase LogFormatText:\\n   214\u2192\\t\\treturn &logrus.TextFormatter{\\n   215\u2192\\t\\t\\tDisableTimestamp: true,\\n   216\u2192\\t\\t\\tDisableColors:    true,\\n   217\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   218\u2192\\tcase LogFormatTextTimestamp:\\n   219\u2192\\t\\treturn &logrus.TextFormatter{\\n   220\u2192\\t\\t\\tDisableTimestamp: false,\\n   221\u2192\\t\\t\\tDisableColors:    true,\\n   222\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   223\u2192\\tcase LogFormatJSON:\\n   224\u2192\\t\\treturn &logrus.JSONFormatter{\\n   225\u2192\\t\\t\\tDisableTimestamp: true,\\n   226\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   227\u2192\\tcase LogFormatJSONTimestamp:\\n   228\u2192\\t\\treturn &logrus.JSONFormatter{\\n   229\u2192\\t\\t\\tDisableTimestamp: false,\\n   230\u2192\\t\\t\\tTimestampFormat:  time.RFC3339Nano,\\n   231\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   232\u2192\\t}\\n   233\u2192\\n   234\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   235\u2192}\\n   236\u2192\\n   237\u2192// validateOpts iterates through all of the keys and values in logOpts, and errors out if\\n   238\u2192// the key in logOpts is not a key in supportedOpts, or the value of corresponding key is\\n   239\u2192// not listed in the value of validKVs.\\n   240\u2192func (o LogOptions) validateOpts(logDriver string, supportedOpts map[string]bool, validKVs map[string][]string) error {\\n   241\u2192\\tfor k, v := range o {\\n   242\u2192\\t\\tif !supportedOpts[k] {\\n   243\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"provided configuration key %q is not supported as a logging option for log driver %s\\\", k, logDriver)\\n   244\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   245\u2192\\t\\tif validValues, ok := validKVs[k]; ok {\\n   246\u2192\\t\\t\\tvalid := false\\n   247\u2192\\t\\t\\tfor _, vv := range validValues {\\n   248\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tif v == vv {\\n   249\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tvalid = true\\n   250\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tbreak\\n   251\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n   252\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   253\u2192\\t\\t\\tif !valid {\\n   254\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\treturn fmt.Errorf(\\\"provided configuration value %q is not a valid value for %q in log driver %s, valid values: %v\\\", v, k, logDriver, validValues)\\n   255\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   256\u2192\\n   257\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   258\u2192\\t}\\n   259\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   260\u2192}\\n   261\u2192\\n   262\u2192// getLogDriverConfig returns a map containing the key-value pairs that start\\n   263\u2192// with string logDriver from map logOpts.\\n   264\u2192func getLogDriverConfig(logDriver string, logOpts LogOptions) LogOptions {\\n   265\u2192\\tkeysToValidate := make(LogOptions)\\n   266\u2192\\tfor k, v := range logOpts {\\n   267\u2192\\t\\tok, err := regexp.MatchString(logDriver+\\\".*\\\", k)\\n   268\u2192\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n   269\u2192\\t\\t\\tDefaultLogger.Fatal(err)\\n   270\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   271\u2192\\t\\tif ok {\\n   272\u2192\\t\\t\\tkeysToValidate[k] = v\\n   273\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   274\u2192\\t}\\n   275\u2192\\treturn keysToValidate\\n   276\u2192}\\n   277\u2192\\n   278\u2192// MultiLine breaks a multi line text into individual log entries and calls the\\n   279\u2192// logging function to log each entry\\n   280\u2192func MultiLine(logFn func(args ...interface{}), output string) {\\n   281\u2192\\tscanner := bufio.NewScanner(bytes.NewReader([]byte(output)))\\n   282\u2192\\tfor scanner.Scan() {\\n   283\u2192\\t\\tlogFn(scanner.Text())\\n   284\u2192\\t}\\n   285\u2192}\\n   286\u2192\\n   287\u2192// CanLogAt returns whether a log message at the given level would be\\n   288\u2192// logged by the given logger.\\n   289\u2192func CanLogAt(logger *logrus.Logger, level logrus.Level) bool {\\n   290\u2192\\treturn GetLevel(logger) >= level\\n   291\u2192}\\n   292\u2192\\n   293\u2192// GetLevel returns the log level of the given logger.\\n   294\u2192func GetLevel(logger *logrus.Logger) logrus.Level {\\n   295\u2192\\treturn logrus.Level(atomic.LoadUint32((*uint32)(&logger.Level)))\\n   296\u2192}\\n   297\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192package endpoint\\n     5\u2192\\n     6\u2192import (\\n     7\u2192\\t\\\"os\\\"\\n     8\u2192\\t\\\"path/filepath\\\"\\n     9\u2192\\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n    10\u2192\\t\\\"sync\\\"\\n    11\u2192\\n    12\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/lumberjack/v2\\\"\\n 17\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/option\\\"\\n    18\u2192)\\n    19\u2192\\n    20\u2192var (\\n    21\u2192\\tlog       = logging.DefaultLogger.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, subsystem)\\n    22\u2192\\tpolicyLog = logrus.New()\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192\\tpolicyLogOnce sync.Once\\n    25\u2192)\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192const (\\n    28\u2192\\tsubsystem = \\\"endpoint\\\"\\n    29\u2192\\n    30\u2192\\tfieldRegenLevel = \\\"regeneration-level\\\"\\n    31\u2192)\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192// getLogger returns a logrus object with EndpointID, containerID and the Endpoint\\n    34\u2192// revision fields.\\n    35\u2192func (e *Endpoint) getLogger() *logrus.Entry {\\n    36\u2192\\treturn e.logger.Load()\\n    37\u2192}\\n    38\u2192\\n    39\u2192// getPolicyLogger returns a logger to be used for policy update debugging, or nil,\\n    40\u2192// if not configured.\\n    41\u2192func (e *Endpoint) getPolicyLogger() *logrus.Entry {\\n    42\u2192\\treturn e.policyLogger.Load()\\n    43\u2192}\\n    44\u2192\\n    45\u2192// PolicyDebug logs the 'msg' with 'fields' if policy debug logging is enabled.\\n    46\u2192func (e *Endpoint) PolicyDebug(fields logrus.Fields, msg string) {\\n    47\u2192\\tif dbgLog := e.getPolicyLogger(); dbgLog != nil {\\n    48\u2192\\t\\tif fields == nil {\\n    49\u2192\\t\\t\\tdbgLog.Debug(msg)\\n    50\u2192\\t\\t} else {\\n    51\u2192\\t\\t\\tdbgLog.WithFields(fields).Debug(msg)\\n    52\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    53\u2192\\t}\\n    54\u2192}\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192// Logger returns a logrus object with EndpointID, containerID and the Endpoint\\n    57\u2192// revision fields. The caller must specify their subsystem.\\n    58\u2192func (e *Endpoint) Logger(subsystem string) *logrus.Entry {\\n    59\u2192\\tif e == nil {\\n    60\u2192\\t\\treturn log.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, subsystem)\\n    61\u2192\\t}\\n    62\u2192\\n    63\u2192\\treturn e.getLogger().WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, subsystem)\\n    64\u2192}\\n    65\u2192\\n    66\u2192// UpdateLogger creates a logger instance specific to this endpoint. It will\\n    67\u2192// create a custom Debug logger for this endpoint when the option on it is set.\\n    68\u2192// If fields is not nil only the those specific fields will be updated in the\\n    69\u2192// endpoint's logger, otherwise a full update of those fields is executed.\\n    70\u2192//\\n    71\u2192// Note: You must hold Endpoint.mutex.Lock() to synchronize logger pointer\\n    72\u2192// updates if the endpoint is already exposed. Callers that create new\\n    73\u2192// endpoints do not need locks to call this.\\n    74\u2192func (e *Endpoint) UpdateLogger(fields map[string]interface{}) {\\n    75\u2192\\te.updatePolicyLogger(fields)\\n    76\u2192\\tepLogger := e.logger.Load()\\n    77\u2192\\tif fields != nil && epLogger != nil {\\n    78\u2192\\t\\tnewLogger := epLogger.WithFields(fields)\\n    79\u2192\\t\\te.logger.Store(newLogger)\\n    80\u2192\\t\\treturn\\n    81\u2192\\t}\\n    82\u2192\\n    83\u2192\\t// We need to update if\\n    84\u2192\\t// - e.logger is nil (this happens on the first ever call to UpdateLogger via\\n    85\u2192\\t//   Logger above). This clause has to come first to guard the others.\\n    86\u2192\\t// - If any of EndpointID, containerID or policyRevision are different on the\\n    87\u2192\\t//   endpoint from the logger.\\n    88\u2192\\t// - The debug option on the endpoint is true, and the logger is not debug,\\n    89\u2192\\t//   or vice versa.\\n    90\u2192\\tshouldUpdate := epLogger == nil || (e.Options != nil &&\\n    91\u2192\\t\\te.Options.IsEnabled(option.Debug) != (epLogger.Level == logrus.DebugLevel))\\n    92\u2192\\n    93\u2192\\t// do nothing if we do not need an update\\n    94\u2192\\tif !shouldUpdate {\\n    95\u2192\\t\\treturn\\n    96\u2192\\t}\\n    97\u2192\\n    98\u2192\\t// When adding new fields, make sure they are abstracted by a setter\\n    99\u2192\\t// and update the logger when the value is set.\\n   100\u2192\\tf := logrus.Fields{\\n   101\u2192\\t\\tlogfields.LogSubsys:              subsystem,\\n   102\u2192\\t\\tlogfields.EndpointID:             e.ID,\\n   103\u2192\\t\\tlogfields.ContainerID:            e.GetShortContainerID(),\\n   104\u2192\\t\\tlogfields.ContainerInterface:     e.containerIfName,\\n   105\u2192\\t\\tlogfields.DatapathPolicyRevision: e.policyRevision,\\n   106\u2192\\t\\tlogfields.DesiredPolicyRevision:  e.nextPolicyRevision,\\n   107\u2192\\t\\tlogfields.IPv4:                   e.GetIPv4Address(),\\n   108\u2192\\t\\tlogfields.IPv6:                   e.GetIPv6Address(),\\n   109\u2192\\t\\tlogfields.K8sPodName:             e.GetK8sNamespaceAndPodName(),\\n   110\u2192\\t\\tlogfields.CEPName:                e.GetK8sNamespaceAndCEPName(),\\n   111\u2192\\t}\\n   112\u2192\\n   113\u2192\\tif e.SecurityIdentity != nil {\\n   114\u2192\\t\\tf[logfields.Identity] = e.SecurityIdentity.ID.StringID()\\n   115\u2192\\t}\\n   116\u2192\\n   117\u2192\\t// Inherit properties from default logger.\\n   118\u2192\\tbaseLogger := logging.DefaultLogger.WithFields(f)\\n   119\u2192\\n   120\u2192\\t// If this endpoint is set to debug ensure it will print debug by giving it\\n   121\u2192\\t// an independent logger.\\n   122\u2192\\t// If this endpoint is not set to debug, it will use the log level set by the user.\\n   123\u2192\\tif e.Options != nil && e.Options.IsEnabled(option.Debug) {\\n   124\u2192\\t\\tbaseLogger.Logger.SetLevel(logrus.DebugLevel)\\n   125\u2192\\t}\\n   126\u2192\\n   127\u2192\\te.logger.Store(baseLogger)\\n   128\u2192}\\n   129\u2192\\n   130\u2192// Only to be called from UpdateLogger() above\\n   131\u2192func (e *Endpoint) updatePolicyLogger(fields map[string]interface{}) {\\n   132\u2192\\tpolicyLogger := e.policyLogger.Load()\\n   133\u2192\\t// e.Options check needed for unit testing.\\n   134\u2192\\tif policyLogger == nil && e.Options != nil && e.Options.IsEnabled(option.DebugPolicy) {\\n   135\u2192\\t\\tpolicyLogOnce.Do(func() {\\n   136\u2192\\t\\t\\tmaxSize := 10 // 10 MB\\n   137\u2192\\t\\t\\tif ms := os.Getenv(\\\"CILIUM_DBG_POLICY_LOG_MAX_SIZE\\\"); ms != \\\"\\\" {\\n   138\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tif ms, err := strconv.Atoi(ms); err == nil {\\n   139\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmaxSize = ms\\n   140\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n   141\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   142\u2192\\t\\t\\tmaxBackups := 3\\n   143\u2192\\t\\t\\tif mb := os.Getenv(\\\"CILIUM_DBG_POLICY_LOG_MAX_BACKUPS\\\"); mb != \\\"\\\" {\\n   144\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tif mb, err := strconv.Atoi(mb); err == nil {\\n   145\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tmaxBackups = mb\\n   146\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n   147\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   148\u2192\\t\\t\\tlumberjackLogger := &lumberjack.Logger{\\n   149\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tFilename:   filepath.Join(option.Config.StateDir, \\\"endpoint-policy.log\\\"),\\n   150\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tMaxSize:    maxSize,\\n   151\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tMaxBackups: maxBackups,\\n   152\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tMaxAge:     28, // days\\n   153\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tLocalTime:  true,\\n   154\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tCompress:   true,\\n   155\u2192\\t\\t\\t}\\n   156\u2192\\t\\t\\tpolicyLog.SetOutput(lumberjackLogger)\\n   157\u2192\\t\\t\\tpolicyLog.SetLevel(logrus.DebugLevel)\\n   158\u2192\\t\\t})\\n   159\u2192\\t\\tpolicyLogger = logrus.NewEntry(policyLog)\\n   160\u2192\\t}\\n   161\u2192\\tif policyLogger == nil || e.Options == nil {\\n   162\u2192\\t\\treturn\\n   163\u2192\\t}\\n   164\u2192\\n   165\u2192\\tif !e.Options.IsEnabled(option.DebugPolicy) {\\n   166\u2192\\t\\tpolicyLogger = nil\\n   167\u2192\\t} else if fields != nil {\\n   168\u2192\\t\\tpolicyLogger = policyLogger.WithFields(fields)\\n   169\u2192\\t} else {\\n   170\u2192\\t\\tf := logrus.Fields{\\n   171\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogfields.LogSubsys:              subsystem,\\n   172\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogfields.EndpointID:             e.ID,\\n   173\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogfields.ContainerID:            e.GetShortContainerID(),\\n   174\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogfields.DatapathPolicyRevision: e.policyRevision,\\n   175\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogfields.DesiredPolicyRevision:  e.nextPolicyRevision,\\n   176\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogfields.IPv4:                   e.GetIPv4Address(),\\n   177\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogfields.IPv6: {\\n   182\u2192\\t\\t\\tf[logfields.Identity] = e.SecurityIdentity.ID\\n   183\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   184\u2192\\n   185\u2192\\t\\tpolicyLogger = policyLogger.WithFields(f)\\n   186\u2192\\t}\\n
