    1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192// Package logfields defines common logging fields which are used across packages\\n     5\u2192package logfields\\n     6\u2192\\n     7\u2192const (\\n     8\u2192\\t// Annotations are any annotations for Pods\\n     9\u2192\\tAnnotations = \\\"annotations\\\"\\n    10\u2192\\n    11\u2192\\t// LogSubsys is the field denoting the subsystem when logging\\n    12\u2192\\tLogSubsys = \\\"subsys\\\"\\n    13\u2192\\n    14\u2192\\t// Signal is the field to print os signals on exit etc.\\n    15\u2192\\tSignal = \\\"signal\\\"\\n    16\u2192\\n    17\u2192\\t// Node is a host machine in the cluster, running cilium\\n    18\u2192\\tNode = \\\"node\\\"\\n    19\u2192\\n    20\u2192\\t// NodeID is the node-scoped ID of the node as allocated by the agent\\n    21\u2192\\tNodeID = \\\"nodeID\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192\\t// NodeName is a human readable name for the node\\n    24\u2192\\tNodeName = \\\"nodeName\\\"\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192\\t// Endpoint is the endpoint name (e.g. wireguard)\\n    27\u2192\\tEndpoint = \\\"endpoint\\\"\\n    28\u2192\\n    29\u2192\\t// EndpointID is the numeric endpoint identifier\\n    30\u2192\\tEndpointID = \\\"endpointID\\\"\\n    31\u2192\\n    32\u2192\\t// EndpointState is the current endpoint state\\n    33\u2192\\tEndpointState = \\\"endpointState\\\"\\n    34\u2192\\n    35\u2192\\t// Error is the Go error\\n    36\u2192\\tError = \\\"error\\\"\\n    37\u2192\\n    38\u2192\\t// EventUUID is an event unique identifier\\n    39\u2192\\tEventUUID = \\\"eventID\\\"\\n    40\u2192\\n    41\u2192\\t// CNIAttachmentID uniquely identifies an endpoint\\n    42\u2192\\tCNIAttachmentID = \\\"cniAttachmentID\\\"\\n    43\u2192\\n    44\u2192\\t// ContainerID is the container identifier\\n    45\u2192\\tContainerID = \\\"containerID\\\"\\n    46\u2192\\n    47\u2192\\t// ContainerInterface is the name of the interface in the container namespace\\n    48\u2192\\tContainerInterface = \\\"containerInterface\\\"\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192\\t// IdentityLabels are the labels relevant for the security identity\\n    51\u2192\\tIdentityLabels = \\\"identityLabels\\\"\\n    52\u2192\\n    53\u2192\\t// InfoLabels are the labels relevant for the security identity\\n    54\u2192\\tInfoLabels = \\\"infoLabels\\\"\\n    55\u2192\\n    56\u2192\\t// Labels are any label, they may not be relevant to the security identity.\\n    57\u2192\\tLabels = \\\"labels\\\"\\n    58\u2192\\n    59\u2192\\t// Label is a singular label, where relevant\\n    60\u2192\\tLabel = \\\"label\\\"\\n    61\u2192\\n    62\u2192\\t// SourceFilter is the label or node information source\\n    63\u2192\\tSourceFilter = \\\"sourceFilter\\\"\\n    64\u2192\\n    65\u2192\\t// Controller is the name of the controller to log it.\\n    66\u2192\\tController = \\\"controller\\\"\\n    67\u2192\\n    68\u2192\\t// Identity is the identifier of a security identity\\n    69\u2192\\tIdentity = \\\"identity\\\"\\n    70\u2192\\n    71\u2192\\t// ConflictingIdentity is the identifier of a security identity that conflicts\\n    72\u2192\\t// with 'Identity'\\n    73\u2192\\tConflictingIdentity = \\\"conflictingIdentity\\\"\\n    74\u2192\\n    75\u2192\\t// Ingress is the identifier of an ingress object\\n    76\u2192\\tIngress = \\\"ingress\\\"\\n    77\u2192\\n    78\u2192\\t// IngressClass is the identifier of an ingress class object\\n    79\u2192\\tIngressClass = \\\"ingressClass\\\"\\n    80\u2192\\n    81\u2192\\t// OldIdentity is a previously used security identity\\n    82\u2192\\tOldIdentity = \\\"oldIdentity\\\"\\n    83\u2192\\n    84\u2192\\t// PolicyKey is a policy map key\\n    85\u2192\\tPolicyKey = \\\"policyKey\\\"\\n    86\u2192\\n    87\u2192\\t// PolicyEntry is a policy map value\\n    88\u2192\\tPolicyEntry = \\\"policyEntry\\\"\\n    89\u2192\\n    90\u2192\\t// PolicyRevision is the revision of the policy in the repository or of\\n    91\u2192\\t// the object in question\\n    92\u2192\\tPolicyRevision = \\\"policyRevision\\\"\\n    93\u2192\\n    94\u2192\\t// PolicyKeysAdded is a set of added policy map keys\\n    95\u2192\\tPolicyKeysAdded = \\\"policyKeysAdded\\\"\\n    96\u2192\\n    97\u2192\\t// PolicyKeysDeleted is a set of deleted policy map keys\\n    98\u2192\\tPolicyKeysDeleted = \\\"policyKeysDeleted\\\"\\n    99\u2192\\n   100\u2192\\t// PolicyEntriesOld is a set of old policy map keys and values\\n   101\u2192\\tPolicyEntriesOld = \\\"policyEntriesOld\\\"\\n   102\u2192\\n   103\u2192\\t// DatapathPolicyRevision is the policy revision currently running in\\n   104\u2192\\t// the datapath\\n   105\u2192\\tDatapathPolicyRevision = \\\"datapathPolicyRevision\\\"\\n   106\u2192\\n   107\u2192\\t// DesiredPolicyRevision is the latest policy revision as evaluated for\\n   108\u2192\\t// an endpoint. It is the desired policy revision to be implemented\\n   109\u2192\\t// into the datapath.\\n   110\u2192\\tDesiredPolicyRevision = \\\"desiredPolicyRevision\\\"\\n   111\u2192\\n   112\u2192\\t// PolicyID is the identifier of a L3, L4 or L7 Policy. Ideally the .NumericIdentity\\n   113\u2192\\tPolicyID = \\\"policyID\\\"\\n   114\u2192\\n   115\u2192\\t// AddedPolicyID is the .NumericIdentity, or set or them\\n   116\u2192\\tAddedPolicyID = \\\"policyID.Added\\\"\\n   117\u2192\\n   118\u2192\\t// DeletedPolicyID is the .NumericIdentity, or set or them\\n   119\u2192\\tDeletedPolicyID = \\\"policyID.Deleted\\\"\\n   120\u2192\\n   121\u2192\\t// AddedPolicyDenyID is the .NumericIdentity, or set or them\\n   122\u2192\\tAddedPolicyDenyID = \\\"policyID.Deny.Added\\\"\\n   123\u2192\\n   124\u2192\\t// DeletedPolicyDenyID is the .NumericIdentity, or set or them\\n   125\u2192\\tDeletedPolicyDenyID = \\\"policyID.Deny.Deleted\\\"\\n   126\u2192\\n   127\u2192\\t// L3PolicyID is the identifier of a L3 Policy\\n   128\u2192\\tL3PolicyID = \\\"policyID.L3\\\"\\n   129\u2192\\n   130\u2192\\t// L4PolicyID is the identifier of a L4 Policy\\n   131\u2192\\tL4PolicyID = \\\"PolicyID.L4\\\"\\n   132\u2192\\n   133\u2192\\t// AuthType is an enum for the type of authentication required, if any.\\n   134\u2192\\tAuthType = \\\"AuthType\\\"\\n   135\u2192\\n   136\u2192\\t// IsRedirect is a boolean for if the entry is a redirect or not\\n   137\u2192\\tIsRedirect = \\\"IsRedirect\\\"\\n   138\u2192\\n   139\u2192\\t// DNSName is a FQDN or not fully qualified name intended for DNS lookups\\n   140\u2192\\tDNSName = \\\"dnsName\\\"\\n   141\u2192\\n   142\u2192\\t// DNSRequestID is the DNS request id used by dns-proxy\\n   143\u2192\\tDNSRequestID = \\\"DNSRequestID\\\"\\n   144\u2192\\n   145\u2192\\t// MACAddr is a MAC address\\n   146\u2192\\tMACAddr = \\\"macAddr\\\"\\n   147\u2192\\n   148\u2192\\t// NextHop is an IPV4 or IPv6 address for the next hop\\n   149\u2192\\tNextHop = \\\"nextHop\\\"\\n   150\u2192\\n   151\u2192\\t// Address is an IPV4, IPv6 or FQDN address\\n   152\u2192\\tAddress = \\\"address\\\"\\n   153\u2192\\n   154\u2192\\t// IPAddr is an IPV4 or IPv6 address\\n   155\u2192\\tIPAddr = \\\"ipAddr\\\"\\n   156\u2192\\n   157\u2192\\t// IPMask is an IPV4 or IPv6 address mask\\n   158\u2192\\tIPMask = \\\"ipMask\\\"\\n   159\u2192\\n   160\u2192\\t// IPv4 is an IPv4 address\\n   161\u2192\\tIPv4 = \\\"ipv4\\\"\\n   162\u2192\\n   163\u2192\\t// IPv6 is an IPv6 address\\n   164\u2192\\tIPv6 = \\\"ipv6\\\"\\n   165\u2192\\n   166\u2192\\t// BPFCompilationTime is the time elapsed to build a BPF endpoint program\\n   167\u2192\\tBPFCompilationTime = \\\"BPFCompilationTime\\\"\\n   168\u2192\\n   169\u2192\\t// StartTime is the start time of an event\\n   170\u2192\\tStartTime = \\\"startTime\\\"\\n   171\u2192\\n   172\u2192\\t// EndTime is the end time of an event\\n   173\u2192\\tEndTime = \\\"endTime\\\"\\n   174\u2192\\n   175\u2192\\t// Interval is the duration for periodic execution of an operation.\\n   176\u2192\\tInterval = \\\"interval\\\"\\n   177\u2192\\n   178\u2192\\t// Duration is the duration of a measured operation\\n   179\u2192\\tDuration = \\\"duration\\\"\\n   180\u2192\\n   181\u2192\\t// V4HealthIP is an address used to contact the cilium-health endpoint\\n   182\u2192\\tV4HealthIP = \\\"v4healthIP.IPv4\\\"\\n   183\u2192\\n   184\u2192\\t// V6HealthIP is an address used to contact the cilium-health endpoint\\n   185\u2192\\tV6HealthIP = \\\"v6healthIP.IPv6\\\"\\n   186\u2192\\n   187\u2192\\t// V4IngressIP is an address used to contact the cilium-Ingress endpoint\\n   188\u2192\\tV4IngressIP = \\\"v4IngressIP.IPv4\\\"\\n   189\u2192\\n   190\u2192\\t// V6IngressIP is an address used to contact the cilium-Ingress endpoint\\n   191\u2192\\tV6IngressIP = \\\"v6IngressIP.IPv6\\\"\\n   192\u2192\\n   193\u2192\\t// V4CiliumHostIP is an address used for the cilium_host interface.\\n   194\u2192\\tV4CiliumHostIP = \\\"v4CiliumHostIP.IPv4\\\"\\n   195\u2192\\n   196\u2192\\t// V6CiliumHostIP is an address used for the cilium_host interface.\\n   197\u2192\\tV6CiliumHostIP = \\\"v6CiliumHostIP.IPv6\\\"\\n   198\u2192\\n   199\u2192\\t// L3n4Addr is a L3 (IP) + L4 (port and protocol) address object.\\n   200\u2192\\tL3n4Addr = \\\"l3n4Addr\\\"\\n   201\u2192\\n   202\u2192\\t// L3n4AddrID is the allocated ID for a L3n4Addr object\\n   203\u2192\\tL3n4AddrID = \\\"l3n4AddrID\\\"\\n   204\u2192\\n   205\u2192\\t// Port is a L4 port\\n   206\u2192\\tPort = \\\"port\\\"\\n   207\u2192\\n   208\u2192\\t// Ports is a list of L4 ports\\n   209\u2192\\tPorts = \\\"ports\\\"\\n   210\u2192\\n   211\u2192\\t// PortName is a k8s ContainerPort Name\\n   212\u2192\\tPortName = \\\"portName\\\"\\n   213\u2192\\n   214\u2192\\t// NamedPorts is a set of named ports\\n   215\u2192\\tNamedPorts = \\\"namedPorts\\\"\\n   216\u2192\\n   217\u2192\\t// Family is the L3 protocol family\\n   218\u2192\\tFamily = \\\"family\\\"\\n   219\u2192\\n   220\u2192\\t// Protocol is the L4 protocol\\n   221\u2192\\tProtocol = \\\"protocol\\\"\\n   222\u2192\\n   223\u2192\\t// V4Prefix is a IPv4 subnet/CIDR prefix\\n   224\u2192\\tV4Prefix = \\\"v4Prefix\\\"\\n   225\u2192\\n   226\u2192\\t// V6Prefix is a IPv6 subnet/CIDR prefix\\n   227\u2192\\tV6Prefix = \\\"v6Prefix\\\"\\n   228\u2192\\n   229\u2192\\t// IPv4CIDRs is a list of IPv4 CIDRs\\n   230\u2192\\tIPv4CIDRs = \\\"ipv4CIDRs\\\"\\n   231\u2192\\n   232\u2192\\t// IPv6CIDRs is a list of IPv6 CIDRs\\n   233\u2192\\tIPv6CIDRs = \\\"ipv6CIDRs\\\"\\n   234\u2192\\n   235\u2192\\t// CIDR is a IPv4/IPv6 subnet/CIDR\\n   236\u2192\\tCIDR = \\\"cidr\\\"\\n   237\u2192\\n   238\u2192\\t// CIDRS is a list of IPv4/IPv6 CIDRs\\n   239\u2192\\tCIDRS = \\\"cidrs\\\"\\n   240\u2192\\n   241\u2192\\t// OldCIDR is the previous subnet/CIDR\\n   242\u2192\\tOldCIDR = \\\"oldCIDR\\\"\\n   243\u2192\\n   244\u2192\\t// NewCIDR is the new subnet/CIDR\\n   245\u2192\\tNewCIDR = \\\"newCIDR\\\"\\n   246\u2192\\n   247\u2192\\t// IPAddrs is a list of IP addrs\\n   248\u2192\\tIPAddrs = \\\"ipAddrs\\\"\\n   249\u2192\\n   250\u2192\\t// MTU is the maximum transmission unit of one interface\\n   251\u2192\\tMTU = \\\"mtu\\\"\\n   252\u2192\\n   253\u2192\\t// Interface is an interface id/name on the system\\n   254\u2192\\tInterface = \\\"interface\\\"\\n   255\u2192\\n   256\u2192\\t// Veth is a veth object or ID\\n   257\u2192\\tVeth = \\\"veth\\\"\\n   258\u2192\\n   259\u2192\\t// VethPair is a tuple of Veth that are paired\\n   260\u2192\\tVethPair = \\\"vethPair\\\"\\n   261\u2192\\n   262\u2192\\t// Netkit is a netkit object or ID\\n   263\u2192\\tNetkit = \\\"netkit\\\"\\n   264\u2192\\n   265\u2192\\t// NetkitPair is a tuple of Netkit that are paired\\n   266\u2192\\tNetkitPair = \\\"netkitPair\\\"\\n   267\u2192\\n   268\u2192\\t// NetNSName is a name of a network namespace\\n   269\u2192\\tNetNSName = \\\"netNSName\\\"\\n   270\u2192\\n   271\u2192\\t// HardwareAddr is L2 addr of a network iface\\n   272\u2192\\tHardwareAddr = \\\"hardwareAddr\\\"\\n   273\u2192\\n   274\u2192\\t// Hash is a hash of something\\n   275\u2192\\tHash = \\\"hash\\\"\\n   276\u2192\\n   277\u2192\\t// ServerNames is the list of TLS SNIs\\n   278\u2192\\tServerNames = \\\"serverNames\\\"\\n   279\u2192\\n   280\u2192\\t// ServiceName is the orchestration framework name for a service\\n   281\u2192\\tServiceName = \\\"serviceName\\\"\\n   282\u2192\\n   283\u2192\\t// ServiceNamespace is the orchestration framework namespace of a service name\\n   284\u2192\\tServiceNamespace = \\\"serviceNamespace\\\"\\n   285\u2192\\n   286\u2192\\t// SessionAffinity indicates whether the ClientIP session affinity is enabled\\n   287\u2192\\t// for the service\\n   288\u2192\\tSessionAffinity = \\\"sessionAffinity\\\"\\n   289\u2192\\n   290\u2192\\t// SessionAffinityTimeout is a timeout for the session affinity\\n   291\u2192\\tSessionAffinityTimeout = \\\"sessionAffinityTimeout\\\"\\n   292\u2192\\n   293\u2192\\t// LoadBalancerSourceRanges is the LB SVC source ranges\\n   294\u2192\\tLoadBalancerSourceRanges = \\\"loadBalancerSourceRanges\\\"\\n   295\u2192\\n   296\u2192\\t// ClusterName is the name of the cluster\\n   297\u2192\\tClusterName = \\\"clusterName\\\"\\n   298\u2192\\n   299\u2192\\t// ClusterID is the ID of the cluster\\n   300\u2192\\tClusterID = \\\"clusterID\\\"\\n   301\u2192\\n   302\u2192\\t// AddrCluster is a pair of IP address and ClusterID\\n   303\u2192\\tAddrCluster = \\\"addrCluster\\\"\\n   304\u2192\\n   305\u2192\\t// ServiceID is the orchestration unique ID of a service\\n   306\u2192\\tServiceID = \\\"serviceID\\\"\\n   307\u2192\\n   308\u2192\\t// ServiceIP is the IP of the service\\n   309\u2192\\tServiceIP = \\\"serviceIP\\\"\\n   310\u2192\\n   311\u2192\\t// ServiceKey is the key of the service in a BPF map\\n   312\u2192\\tServiceKey = \\\"svcKey\\\"\\n   313\u2192\\n   314\u2192\\t// ServiceValue is the value of the service in a BPF map\\n   315\u2192\\tServiceValue = \\\"svcVal\\\"\\n   316\u2192\\n   317\u2192\\t// ServiceType is the type of the service\\n   318\u2192\\tServiceType = \\\"svcType\\\"\\n   319\u2192\\n   320\u2192\\t// ServiceHealthCheckNodePort is the port on which we serve health checks\\n   321\u2192\\tServiceHealthCheckNodePort = \\\"svcHealthCheckNodePort\\\"\\n   322\u2192\\n   323\u2192\\t// ServiceExtTrafficPolicy is the external traffic policy of the service\\n   324\u2192\\tServiceExtTrafficPolicy = \\\"svcExtTrafficPolicy\\\"\\n   325\u2192\\n   326\u2192\\t// ServiceIntTrafficPolicy is the internal traffic policy of the service\\n   327\u2192\\tServiceIntTrafficPolicy = \\\"svcIntTrafficPolicy\\\"\\n   328\u2192\\n   329\u2192\\t// BackendIDs is the map of backend IDs (lbmap) indexed by backend address\\n   330\u2192\\tBackendIDs = \\\"backendIDs\\\"\\n   331\u2192\\n   332\u2192\\t// BackendID is the ID of the backend\\n   333\u2192\\tBackendID = \\\"backendID\\\"\\n   334\u2192\\n   335\u2192\\t// BackendWeight is a weight of service backend.\\n   336\u2192\\tBackendWeight = \\\"backendWeight\\\"\\n   337\u2192\\n   338\u2192\\t// Backends is the list of the service backends\\n   339\u2192\\tBackends = \\\"backends\\\"\\n   340\u2192\\n   341\u2192\\t// BackendName is the name of the backend\\n   342\u2192\\tBackendName = \\\"backendName\\\"\\n   343\u2192\\n   344\u2192\\t// BackendSlot is the backend slot number in a service BPF map\\n   345\u2192\\tBackendSlot = \\\"backendSlot\\\"\\n   346\u2192\\n   347\u2192\\t// ProxyName is the name of a proxy (e.g., \\\"Envoy\\\")\\n   348\u2192\\tProxyName = \\\"proxyName\\\"\\n   349\u2192\\n   350\u2192\\t// ProxyPort is the port number of an L7 proxy listener.\\n   351\u2192\\tProxyPort = \\\"ProxyPort\\\"\\n   352\u2192\\n   353\u2192\\t// L7LBProxyPort is the port number of the Envoy listener a L7 LB service redirects traffic to for load balancing.\\n   354\u2192\\tL7LBProxyPort = \\\"l7LBProxyPort\\\"\\n   355\u2192\\n   356\u2192\\t// L7LBFrontendPorts is the list of frontend ports for load balancing.\\n   357\u2192\\tL7LBFrontendPorts = \\\"l7LBFrontendPorts\\\"\\n   358\u2192\\n   359\u2192\\t// BackendState is the state of the backend\\n   360\u2192\\tBackendState = \\\"backendState\\\"\\n   361\u2192\\n   362\u2192\\t// BackendPreferred is the indicator if this backend is preferred if active.\\n   363\u2192\\tBackendPreferred = \\\"backendPreferred\\\"\\n   364\u2192\\n   365\u2192\\t// CiliumNetworkPolicy is a cilium specific NetworkPolicy\\n   366\u2192\\tCiliumNetworkPolicy = \\\"ciliumNetworkPolicy\\\"\\n   367\u2192\\n   368\u2192\\t// CiliumNetworkPolicyName is the name of a CiliumNetworkPolicy\\n   369\u2192\\tCiliumNetworkPolicyName = \\\"ciliumNetworkPolicyName\\\"\\n   370\u2192\\n   371\u2192\\t// CiliumClusterwideNetworkPolicyName is the name of the CiliumClusterWideNetworkPolicy\\n   372\u2192\\tCiliumClusterwideNetworkPolicyName = \\\"ciliumClusterwideNetworkPolicyName\\\"\\n   373\u2192\\n   374\u2192\\t// BPFClockSource denotes the internal clock source (ktime vs jiffies)\\n   375\u2192\\tBPFClockSource = \\\"bpfClockSource\\\"\\n   376\u2192\\n   377\u2192\\t// CiliumLocalRedirectPolicyName is the name of a CiliumLocalRedirectPolicy\\n   378\u2192\\tCiliumLocalRedirectName = \\\"ciliumLocalRedirectPolicyName\\\"\\n   379\u2192\\n   380\u2192\\t// CiliumEgressGatewayPolicyName is the name of a CiliumEgressGatewayPolicy\\n   381\u2192\\tCiliumEgressGatewayPolicyName = \\\"ciliumEgressGatewayPolicyName\\\"\\n   382\u2192\\n   383\u2192\\t// CiliumClusterwideEnvoyConfigName is the name of a CiliumClusterwideEnvoyConfig\\n   384\u2192\\tCiliumClusterwideEnvoyConfigName = \\\"ciliumClusterwideEnvoyConfigName\\\"\\n   385\u2192\\n   386\u2192\\t// CiliumEnvoyConfigName is the name of a CiliumEnvoyConfig\\n   387\u2192\\tCiliumEnvoyConfigName = \\\"ciliumEnvoyConfigName\\\"\\n   388\u2192\\n   389\u2192\\t// Listener is the name of an Envoy Listener defined in CEC or CCEC\\n   390\u2192\\tListener = \\\"listener\\\"\\n   391\u2192\\n   392\u2192\\t// ListenerPriority is the priority of an Envoy Listener defined in CEC or CCEC\\n   393\u2192\\tListenerPriority = \\\"listenerPriority\\\"\\n   394\u2192\\n   395\u2192\\t// BPFMapKey is a key from a BPF map\\n   396\u2192\\tBPFMapKey = \\\"bpfMapKey\\\"\\n   397\u2192\\n   398\u2192\\t// BPFMapValue is a value from a BPF map\\n   399\u2192\\tBPFMapValue = \\\"bpfMapValue\\\"\\n   400\u2192\\n   401\u2192\\t// Device is the device name\\n   402\u2192\\tDevice = \\\"device\\\"\\n   403\u2192\\n   404\u2192\\t// Devices is the devices name\\n   405\u2192\\tDevices = \\\"devices\\\"\\n   406\u2192\\n   407\u2192\\t// DirectRoutingDevice is the name of the direct routing device\\n   408\u2192\\tDirectRoutingDevice = \\\"directRoutingDevice\\\"\\n   409\u2192\\n   410\u2192\\t// DatapathMode is the datapath mode name\\n   411\u2192\\tDatapathMode = \\\"datapathMode\\\"\\n   412\u2192\\n   413\u2192\\t// Tunnel is the tunnel name\\n   414\u2192\\tTunnel = \\\"tunnel\\\"\\n   415\u2192\\n   416\u2192\\t// TunnelPeer is the tunnel peer address\\n   417\u2192\\tTunnelPeer = \\\"tunnelPeer\\\"\\n   418\u2192\\n   419\u2192\\t// ConflictingTunnelPeer is the address of a tunnel peer which conflicts\\n   420\u2192\\t// with TunnelPeer\\n   421\u2192\\tConflictingTunnelPeer = \\\"conflictingTunnelPeer\\\"\\n   422\u2192\\n   423\u2192\\t// Type is the address type\\n   424\u2192\\tType = \\\"type\\\"\\n   425\u2192\\n   426\u2192\\t// Selector is a selector of any sort: endpoint, CIDR, toFQDNs\\n   427\u2192\\tSelector = \\\"Selector\\\"\\n   428\u2192\\n   429\u2192\\t// EndpointLabelSelector is a selector for Endpoints by label\\n   430\u2192\\tEndpointLabelSelector = \\\"EndpointLabelSelector\\\"\\n   431\u2192\\n   432\u2192\\t// EndpointSelector is a selector for Endpoints\\n   433\u2192\\tEndpointSelector = \\\"EndpointSelector\\\"\\n   434\u2192\\n   435\u2192\\t// Path is a filesystem path. It can be a file or directory.\\n   436\u2192\\t// Note: pkg/proxy/accesslog points to this variable so be careful when\\n   437\u2192\\t// changing the value\\n   438\u2192\\tPath = \\\"file-path\\\"\\n   439\u2192\\n   440\u2192\\t// Line is a line number within a file\\n   441\u2192\\tLine = \\\"line\\\"\\n   442\u2192\\n   443\u2192\\t// LinkIndex is a network iface index\\n   444\u2192\\tLinkIndex = \\\"linkIndex\\\"\\n   445\u2192\\n   446\u2192\\t// Object is used when \\\"%+v\\\" printing Go objects for debug or error handling.\\n   447\u2192\\t// It is often paired with logfields.Repr to render the object.\\n   448\u2192\\tObject = \\\"obj\\\"\\n   449\u2192\\n   450\u2192\\t// Request is a request object received by us, reported in debug or error.\\n   451\u2192\\t// It is often paired with logfields.Repr to render the object.\\n   452\u2192\\tRequest = \\\"req\\\"\\n   453\u2192\\n   454\u2192\\t// Params are the parameters of a request, reported in debug or error.\\n   455\u2192\\tParams = \\\"params\\\"\\n   456\u2192\\n   457\u2192\\t// Response is a response object received by us, reported in debug or error.\\n   458\u2192\\t// It is often paired with logfields.Repr to render the object.\\n   459\u2192\\tResponse = \\\"resp\\\"\\n   460\u2192\\n   461\u2192\\t// Resource is a resource\\n   462\u2192\\tResource = \\\"resource\\\"\\n   463\u2192\\n   464\u2192\\t// ConflictingResource is a resource that conflicts with 'Resource'\\n   465\u2192\\tConflictingResource = \\\"conflictingResource\\\"\\n   466\u2192\\n   467\u2192\\t// Route is a L2 or L3 Linux route\\n   468\u2192\\tRoute = \\\"route\\\"\\n   469\u2192\\n   470\u2192\\t// RetryUUID is an UUID identical for all retries of a set\\n   471\u2192\\tRetryUUID = \\\"retryUUID\\\"\\n   472\u2192\\n   473\u2192\\t// Rule is an ip rule\\n   474\u2192\\tRule = \\\"rule\\\"\\n   475\u2192\\n   476\u2192\\t// Envoy xDS-protocol-specific\\n   477\u2192\\n   478\u2192\\t// XDSStreamID is the ID of an xDS request stream.\\n   479\u2192\\tXDSStreamID = \\\"xdsStreamID\\\"\\n   480\u2192\\n   481\u2192\\t// XDSAckedVersion is the version of an xDS resource acked by Envoy.\\n   482\u2192\\tXDSAckedVersion = \\\"xdsAckedVersion\\\"\\n   483\u2192\\n   484\u2192\\t// XDSCachedVersion is the version of an xDS resource currently in cache.\\n   485\u2192\\tXDSCachedVersion = \\\"xdsCachedVersion\\\"\\n   486\u2192\\n   487\u2192\\t// XDSTypeURL is the URL that identifies an xDS resource type.\\n   488\u2192\\tXDSTypeURL = \\\"xdsTypeURL\\\"\\n   489\u2192\\n   490\u2192\\t// XDSNonce is a nonce sent in xDS requests and responses.\\n   491\u2192\\tXDSNonce = \\\"xdsNonce\\\"\\n   492\u2192\\n   493\u2192\\t// XDSCanary is a boolean indicating whether a response is a dry run.\\n   494\u2192\\tXDSCanary = \\\"xdsCanary\\\"\\n   495\u2192\\n   496\u2192\\t// XDSResourceName is the name of an xDS resource.\\n   497\u2192\\tXDSResourceName = \\\"xdsResourceName\\\"\\n   498\u2192\\n   499\u2192\\t// XDSClientNode is the ID of an XDS client, e.g. an Envoy node.\\n   500\u2192\\tXDSClientNode = \\\"xdsClientNode\\\"\\n   501\u2192\\n   502\u2192\\t// XDSResource is an xDS resource message.\\n   503\u2192\\tXDSResource = \\\"xdsResource\\\"\\n   504\u2192\\n   505\u2192\\t// XDSDetail is detail string included in XDS NACKs.\\n   506\u2192\\tXDSDetail = \\\"xdsDetail\\\"\\n   507\u2192\\n   508\u2192\\t// K8s-specific\\n   509\u2192\\n   510\u2192\\t// K8sNodeID is the k8s ID of a K8sNode\\n   511\u2192\\tK8sNodeID = \\\"k8sNodeID\\\"\\n   512\u2192\\n   513\u2192\\t// K8sPodName is the name of a k8s pod\\n   514\u2192\\tK8sPodName = \\\"k8sPodName\\\"\\n   515\u2192\\n   516\u2192\\t// K8sSvcName is the name of a K8s service\\n   517\u2192\\tK8sSvcName = \\\"k8sSvcName\\\"\\n   518\u2192\\n   519\u2192\\t// K8sSvcID is the K8s service name and namespace\\n   520\u2192\\tK8sSvcID = \\\"k8sSvcID\\\"\\n   521\u2192\\n   522\u2192\\t// K8sSvcType is the k8s service type (e.g. NodePort, Loadbalancer etc.)\\n   523\u2192\\tK8sSvcType = \\\"k8sSvcType\\\"\\n   524\u2192\\n   525\u2192\\t// K8sEndpointName is the k8s name for a k8s Endpoint (not a cilium Endpoint)\\n   526\u2192\\tK8sEndpointName = \\\"k8sEndpointName\\\"\\n   527\u2192\\n   528\u2192\\t// K8sNamespace is the namespace something belongs to\\n   529\u2192\\tK8sNamespace = \\\"k8sNamespace\\\"\\n   530\u2192\\n   531\u2192\\t// K8sIdentityAnnotation is a k8s non-identifying annotations on k8s objects\\n   532\u2192\\tK8sIdentityAnnotation = \\\"k8sIdentityAnnotation\\\"\\n   533\u2192\\n   534\u2192\\t// K8sNetworkPolicy is a k8s NetworkPolicy object (not a CiliumNetworkObject, above).\\n   535\u2192\\tK8sNetworkPolicy = \\\"k8sNetworkPolicy\\\"\\n   536\u2192\\n   537\u2192\\t// K8sNetworkPolicyName is the name of a K8sPolicyObject\\n   538\u2192\\tK8sNetworkPolicyName = \\\"k8sNetworkPolicyName\\\"\\n   539\u2192\\n   540\u2192\\t// K8sIngress is a k8s Ingress service object\\n   541\u2192\\tK8sIngress = \\\"k8sIngress\\\"\\n   542\u2192\\n   543\u2192\\t// K8sIngressName is the name of a K8sIngress\\n   544\u2192\\tK8sIngressName = \\\"k8sIngressName\\\"\\n   545\u2192\\n   546\u2192\\t// K8sAPIVersion is the version of the k8s API an object has\\n   547\u2192\\tK8sAPIVersion = \\\"k8sApiVersion\\\"\\n   548\u2192\\n   549\u2192\\t// K8sNodeIP is the k8s Node IP (either InternalIP or ExternalIP)\\n   550\u2192\\tK8sNodeIP = \\\"k8sNodeIP\\\"\\n   551\u2192\\n   552\u2192\\t// K8sUID is the UID of a K8s object\\n   553\u2192\\tK8sUID = \\\"k8sUID\\\"\\n   554\u2192\\n   555\u2192\\t// Attempt is the attempt number if an operation is attempted multiple times\\n   556\u2192\\tAttempt = \\\"attempt\\\"\\n   557\u2192\\n   558\u2192\\t// TrafficDirection represents the directionality of traffic with respect\\n   559\u2192\\t// to an endpoint.\\n   560\u2192\\tTrafficDirection = \\\"trafficDirection\\\"\\n   561\u2192\\n   562\u2192\\t// Modification represents a type of state change operation (insert, delete,\\n   563\u2192\\t// upsert, etc.).\\n   564\u2192\\tModification = \\\"modification\\\"\\n   565\u2192\\n   566\u2192\\t// BPFMapName is the name of a BPF map.\\n   567\u2192\\tBPFMapName = \\\"bpfMapName\\\"\\n   568\u2192\\n   569\u2192\\t// BPFHeaderHash is the hash of the BPF header.\\n   570\u2192\\tBPFHeaderfileHash = \\\"bpfHeaderfileHash\\\"\\n   571\u2192\\n   572\u2192\\t// BPFMapPath is the path of a BPF map in the filesystem.\\n   573\u2192\\tBPFMapPath = \\\"bpfMapPath\\\"\\n   574\u2192\\n   575\u2192\\t// BPFMapFD is the file descriptor for a BPF map.\\n   576\u2192\\tBPFMapFD = \\\"bpfMapFileDescriptor\\\"\\n   577\u2192\\n   578\u2192\\t// ThreadID is the Envoy thread ID.\\n   579\u2192\\tThreadID = \\\"threadID\\\"\\n   580\u2192\\n   581\u2192\\t// Reason is a human readable string describing why an operation was\\n   582\u2192\\t// performed\\n   583\u2192\\tReason = \\\"reason\\\"\\n   584\u2192\\n   585\u2192\\t// Limit is a numerical limit that has been exceeded\\n   586\u2192\\tLimit = \\\"limit\\\"\\n   587\u2192\\n   588\u2192\\t// Count is a measure being compared to the Limit\\n   589\u2192\\tCount = \\\"count\\\"\\n   590\u2192\\n   591\u2192\\t// Debug is a boolean value for whether debug is set or not.\\n   592\u2192\\tDebug = \\\"debug\\\"\\n   593\u2192\\n   594\u2192\\t// PID is an integer value for the process identifier of a process.\\n   595\u2192\\tPID = \\\"pid\\\"\\n   596\u2192\\n   597\u2192\\t// PIDFile is a string value for the path to a file containing a PID.\\n   598\u2192\\tPIDFile = \\\"pidfile\\\"\\n   599\u2192\\n   600\u2192\\t// Probe is the name of a status probe.\\n   601\u2192\\tProbe = \\\"probe\\\"\\n   602\u2192\\n   603\u2192\\t// Key is the identity of the encryption key\\n   604\u2192\\tKey = \\\"key\\\"\\n   605\u2192\\n   606\u2192\\t// ConflictingKey is the identity of the encryption key which conflicts with\\n   607\u2192\\t// Key\\n   608\u2192\\tConflictingKey = \\\"conflictingKey\\\"\\n   609\u2192\\n   610\u2192\\t// URL represents a Uniform Resource Locator.\\n   611\u2192\\tURL = \\\"url\\\"\\n   612\u2192\\n   613\u2192\\t// SysParamName is the name of the kernel parameter (sysctl)\\n   614\u2192\\tSysParamName = \\\"sysParamName\\\"\\n   615\u2192\\n   616\u2192\\t// SysParamValue is the value of the kernel parameter (sysctl)\\n   617\u2192\\tSysParamValue = \\\"sysParamValue\\\"\\n   618\u2192\\n   619\u2192\\t// HashSeed is the seed value for the hashing algorithm\\n   620\u2192\\tHashSeed = \\\"hashSeed\\\"\\n   621\u2192\\n   622\u2192\\t// HelpMessage is the help message corresponding to a log message.\\n   623\u2192\\t// This is to make sure we keep separate contexts for logs and help messages.\\n   624\u2192\\tHelpMessage = \\\"helpMessage\\\"\\n   625\u2192\\n   626\u2192\\t// LRPName is the parsed name of the Local Redirect Policy.\\n   627\u2192\\tLRPName = \\\"lrpName\\\"\\n   628\u2192\\n   629\u2192\\t// LRPFrontend is the parsed frontend mappings of the Local Redirect Policy.\\n   630\u2192\\tLRPFrontends = \\\"lrpFrontends\\\"\\n   631\u2192\\n   632\u2192\\t// LRPLocalEndpointSelector is the local endpoint selector of the Local Redirect Policy.\\n   633\u2192\\tLRPLocalEndpointSelector = \\\"lrpLocalEndpointSelector\\\"\\n   634\u2192\\n   635\u2192\\t// LRPBackendPorts are the parsed backend ports of the Local Redirect Policy.\\n   636\u2192\\tLRPBackendPorts = \\\"lrpBackendPorts\\\"\\n   637\u2192\\n   638\u2192\\t// LRPType is the type of the Local Redirect Policy.\\n   639\u2192\\tLRPType = \\\"lrpType\\\"\\n   640\u2192\\n   641\u2192\\t// LRPFrontendType is the parsed frontend type of the Local Redirect Policy.\\n   642\u2192\\tLRPFrontendType = \\\"lrpFrontendType\\\"\\n   643\u2192\\n   644\u2192\\t// ENPName is the name of the egress nat policy\\n   645\u2192\\tENPName = \\\"enpName\\\"\\n   646\u2192\\n   647\u2192\\t// Mode describes an operations mode\\n   648\u2192\\tMode = \\\"mode\\\"\\n   649\u2192\\n   650\u2192\\t// PubKey is the public key\\n   651\u2192\\tPubKey = \\\"pubKey\\\"\\n   652\u2192\\n   653\u2192\\t// NodeIPv4 is the node IPv4 addr\\n   654\u2192\\tNodeIPv4 = \\\"nodeIPv4\\\"\\n   655\u2192\\n   656\u2192\\t// NodeIPv6 is the node IPv4 addr\\n   657\u2192\\tNodeIPv6 = \\\"nodeIPv6\\\"\\n   658\u2192\\n   659\u2192\\t// OldNode refers to the node before the update\\n   660\u2192\\tOldNode = \\\"oldNode\\\"\\n   661\u2192\\n   662\u2192\\t// NewNode refers to the node after the update\\n   663\u2192\\tNewNode = \\\"newNode\\\"\\n   664\u2192\\n   665\u2192\\t// AttachedENIs are the ENIs which have been attached to the node\\n   666\u2192\\tAttachedENIs = \\\"attachedENIs\\\"\\n   667\u2192\\n   668\u2192\\t// ExpectedENIs are the ENIs which are expected to be available\\n   669\u2192\\tExpectedENIs = \\\"expectedENIs\\\"\\n   670\u2192\\n   671\u2192\\t// Hint helps nudge the user in the right direction when troubleshooting.\\n   672\u2192\\tHint = \\\"hint\\\"\\n   673\u2192\\n   674\u2192\\t// CEPName is the name of the CiliumEndpoint.\\n   675\u2192\\tCEPName = \\\"ciliumEndpointName\\\"\\n   676\u2192\\n   677\u2192\\t// CEPCount is the count of the CiliumEndpoint.\\n   678\u2192\\tCEPCount = \\\"ciliumEndpointCount\\\"\\n   679\u2192\\n   680\u2192\\t// CEPUID is the UID of the CiliumEndpoint.\\n   681\u2192\\tCEPUID = \\\"ciliumEndpointUID\\\"\\n   682\u2192\\n   683\u2192\\t// CESName is the name of the CiliumEndpointSlice.\\n   684\u2192\\tCESName = \\\"ciliumEndpointSliceName\\\"\\n   685\u2192\\n   686\u2192\\t// WorkQueueQPSLimit is the QPS limit for WorkQueues.\\n   687\u2192\\tWorkQueueQPSLimit = \\\"workQueueQPSLimit\\\"\\n   688\u2192\\n   689\u2192\\t// WorkQueueBurstLimit is the burst limit for WorkQueues.\\n   690\u2192\\tWorkQueueBurstLimit = \\\"workQueueBurstLimit\\\"\\n   691\u2192\\n   692\u2192\\t// WorkQueueSyncBackoff is the backoff time used by workqueues before an attempt to retry sync with k8s-apiserver.\\n   693\u2192\\tWorkQueueSyncBackOff = \\\"workQueueSyncBackOff\\\"\\n   694\u2192\\n   695\u2192\\t// SourceIP is a source IP\\n   696\u2192\\tSourceIP = \\\"sourceIP\\\"\\n   697\u2192\\n   698\u2192\\tDestinationIP = \\\"destinationIP\\\"\\n   699\u2192\\n   700\u2192\\tLocalIP = \\\"localIP\\\"\\n   701\u2192\\n   702\u2192\\tRemoteIP = \\\"remoteIP\\\"\\n   703\u2192\\n   704\u2192\\tSourceCIDR = \\\"sourceCIDR\\\"\\n   705\u2192\\n   706\u2192\\t// DestinationCIDR is a destination CIDR\\n   707\u2192\\tDestinationCIDR = \\\"destinationCIDR\\\"\\n   708\u2192\\n   709\u2192\\t// EgressIP is the egress IP used in a given egress policy\\n   710\u2192\\tEgressIP = \\\"egressIP\\\"\\n   711\u2192\\n   712\u2192\\t// GatewayIP is the gateway IP used in a given egress policy\\n   713\u2192\\tGatewayIP = \\\"gatewayIP\\\"\\n   714\u2192\\n   715\u2192\\t// Number of Backends failed while restoration.\\n   716\u2192\\tRestoredBackends = \\\"restoredBackends\\\"\\n   717\u2192\\n   718\u2192\\t// Number of Backends failed while restoration.\\n   719\u2192\\tFailedBackends = \\\"failedBackends\\\"\\n   720\u2192\\n   721\u2192\\t// SkippedBackends is the number of Backends that were skipped during restore\\n   722\u2192\\t// as duplicates.\\n   723\u2192\\tSkippedBackends = \\\"skippedBackends\\\"\\n   724\u2192\\n   725\u2192\\t// OrphanBackends is the number Backends that are not associated with any services.\\n   726\u2192\\tOrphanBackends = \\\"orphanBackends\\\"\\n   727\u2192\\n   728\u2192\\t// Number of Services failed while restoration.\\n   729\u2192\\tRestoredSVCs = \\\"restoredServices\\\"\\n   730\u2192\\n   731\u2192\\t// Number of Services failed while restoration.\\n   732\u2192\\tFailedSVCs = \\\"failedServices\\\"\\n   733\u2192\\n   734\u2192\\t// Chain is an Iptables chain\\n   735\u2192\\tChain = \\\"chain\\\"\\n   736\u2192\\n   737\u2192\\t// IPSec SPI\\n   738\u2192\\tSPI = \\\"spi\\\"\\n   739\u2192\\n   740\u2192\\t// IPSec old SPI\\n   741\u2192\\tOldSPI = \\\"oldSPI\\\"\\n   742\u2192\\n   743\u2192\\t// CGroupId is the numerical cgroup id\\n   744\u2192\\tCGroupID = \\\"cgroupID\\\"\\n   745\u2192\\n   746\u2192\\t// Expected is an expected value\\n   747\u2192\\tExpected = \\\"expected\\\"\\n   748\u2192\\n   749\u2192\\t// ConfigSource is a configuration source (for process options, e.g. agent)\\n   750\u2192\\tConfigSource = \\\"configSource\\\"\\n   751\u2192\\n   752\u2192\\t// ConfigKey is a single key in a configuration source\\n   753\u2192\\tConfigKey = \\\"configKey\\\"\\n   754\u2192\\n   755\u2192\\t// ConfigAnnotation is an annotation on a node\\n   756\u2192\\tConfigAnnotation = \\\"configAnnotation\\\"\\n   757\u2192\\n   758\u2192\\t// User identifies a given user\\n   759\u2192\\tUser = \\\"user\\\"\\n   760\u2192\\n   761\u2192\\t// CIDRGroupRef is a references to a CiliumCIDRGroup object.\\n   762\u2192\\tCIDRGroupRef = \\\"cidrGroupRef\\\"\\n   763\u2192\\n   764\u2192\\t// Workers represents the number of workers.\\n   765\u2192\\tWorkers = \\\"workers\\\"\\n   766\u2192\\n   767\u2192\\t// Event identifies the type of an event.\\n   768\u2192\\tEvent = \\\"event\\\"\\n   769\u2192\\n   770\u2192\\t// Prefix identifies a given prefix.\\n   771\u2192\\tPrefix = \\\"prefix\\\"\\n   772\u2192\\n   773\u2192\\t// Value identifies a generic value (e.g., of a key/value pair).\\n   774\u2192\\tValue = \\\"value\\\"\\n   775\u2192\\n   776\u2192\\t// State is the state of an individual component (apiserver, kvstore etc)\\n   777\u2192\\tState = \\\"state\\\"\\n   778\u2192\\n   779\u2192\\t// EtcdQPSLimit is the QPS limit for an etcd client.\\n   780\u2192\\tEtcdQPSLimit = \\\"etcdQPSLimit\\\"\\n   781\u2192\\n   782\u2192\\t// LeaseID identifies a KVStore lease\\n   783\u2192\\tLeaseID = \\\"leaseID\\\"\\n   784\u2192\\n   785\u2192\\t// EventType identifies the type of KVStore events\\n   786\u2192\\tEventType = \\\"eventType\\\"\\n   787\u2192\\n   788\u2192\\t// Entries specifies the number of KVStore entries\\n   789\u2192\\tEntries = \\\"entries\\\"\\n   790\u2192\\t// Action is the summarized action from a reconciliation.\\n   791\u2192\\tAction = \\\"action\\\"\\n   792\u2192\\n   793\u2192\\t// EtcdClusterID is the ID of the etcd cluster\\n   794\u2192\\tEtcdClusterID = \\\"etcdClusterID\\\"\\n   795\u2192\\n   796\u2192\\t// NetnsCookie is the Linux kernel netns cookie.\\n   797\u2192\\tNetnsCookie = \\\"netnsCookie\\\"\\n   798\u2192\\n   799\u2192\\t// Target identifies a target value\\n   800\u2192\\tTarget = \\\"target\\\"\\n   801\u2192)\\n   802\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. :\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"59ab3af7-03c1-4dc9-8488-e0a68da0af2a\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:21.690Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"59ab3af7-03c1-4dc9-8488-e0a68da0af2a\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01PgDGiaLdD4NyggwMzZXqv9\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192package policy\\n     5\u2192\\n     6\u2192import (\\n     7\u2192\\t\\\"bytes\\\"\\n     8\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n     9\u2192\\t\\\"encoding/json\\\"\\n    10\u2192\\t\\\"testing\\\"\\n    11\u2192\\n    12\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\\\"\\n    13\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\\\"\\n    14\u2192\\n    15\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\\\"\\n    16\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/labels\\\"\\n    17\u2192)\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192func TestNewPolicyAuditLogger(t *testing.T) {\\n    20\u2192\\t// Test without audit output\\n    21\u2192\\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.DebugLevel, nil)\\n    22\u2192\\trequire.NotNil(t, logger)\\n    23\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.DebugLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\\n    24\u2192\\trequire.Nil(t, logger.auditLog)\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192\\t// Test with audit output\\n    27\u2192\\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\\n    28\u2192\\tlogger = NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\\n    29\u2192\\trequire.NotNil(t, logger)\\n    30\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\\n    31\u2192\\trequire.NotNil(t, logger.auditLog)\\n    32\u2192}\\n    33\u2192\\n    34\u2192func TestLogDecision(t *testing.T) {\\n    35\u2192\\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\\n    36\u2192\\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\\n    37\u2192\\n    38\u2192\\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(1001)\\n    39\u2192\\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \\\"test-policy\\\", \\\"allow\\\", \\\"matches selector\\\")\\n    40\u2192\\n    41\u2192\\t// Verify audit log contains the decision\\n    42\u2192\\tlogOutput := buf.String()\\n    43\u2192\\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\\n    44\u2192\\n    45\u2192\\t// Parse JSON to verify structure\\n    46\u2192\\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\\n    47\u2192\\terr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)\\n    48\u2192\\trequire.NoError(t, err)\\n    49\u2192\\n    50\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, \\\"policy-decision\\\", auditEntry[\\\"msg\\\"])\\n    51\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, float64(1001), auditEntry[\\\"identity\\\"])\\n    52\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, \\\"test-policy\\\", auditEntry[\\\"policy\\\"])\\n    53\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, \\\"allow\\\", auditEntry[\\\"verdict\\\"])\\n    54\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, \\\"matches selector\\\", auditEntry[\\\"reason\\\"])\\n    55\u2192}\\n    56\u2192\\n    57\u2192func TestLogDecisionDeny(t *testing.T) {\\n    58\u2192\\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\\n    59\u2192\\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\\n    60\u2192\\n    61\u2192\\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(2002)\\n    62\u2192\\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \\\"restrictive-policy\\\", \\\"deny\\\", \\\"denied by rule\\\")\\n    63\u2192\\n    64\u2192\\tlogOutput := buf.String()\\n    65\u2192\\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\\n    66\u2192\\n    67\u2192\\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\\n    68\u2192\\terr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)\\n    69\u2192\\trequire.NoError(t, err)\\n    70\u2192\\n    71\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, \\\"policy-decision\\\", auditEntry[\\\"msg\\\"])\\n    72\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, \\\"deny\\\", auditEntry[\\\"verdict\\\"])\\n    73\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, \\\"denied by rule\\\", auditEntry[\\\"reason\\\"])\\n    74\u2192}\\n    75\u2192\\n    76\u2192func TestLogEvaluation(t *testing.T) {\\n    77\u2192\\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\\n    78\u2192\\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\\n    79\u2192\\n    80\u2192\\tctx := context.Background()\\n    81\u2192\\tpolicyKey := \\\"test-policy-key\\\"\\n    82\u2192\\n    83\u2192\\t// Create a minimal SelectorCache for testing\\n    84\u2192\\trepo := NewPolicyRepository(nil, nil, nil)\\n    85\u2192\\tselectorCache := repo.GetSelectorCache()\\n    86\u2192\\n    87\u2192\\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, selectorCache, true)\\n    88\u2192\\n    89\u2192\\tlogOutput := buf.String()\\n    90\u2192\\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\\n    91\u2192\\n    92\u2192\\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\\n    93\u2192\\terr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)\\n    94\u2192\\trequire.NoError(t, err)\\n    95\u2192\\n    96\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, \\\"policy-evaluation\\\", auditEntry[\\\"msg\\\"])\\n    97\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, policyKey, auditEntry[logfields.PolicyKey])\\n    98\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, true, auditEntry[\\\"result\\\"])\\n    99\u2192}\\n   100\u2192\\n   101\u2192func TestLogEvaluationFailure(t *testing.T) {\\n   102\u2192\\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\\n   103\u2192\\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\\n   104\u2192\\n   105\u2192\\tctx := context.Background()\\n   106\u2192\\tpolicyKey := \\\"failing-policy\\\"\\n   107\u2192\\n   108\u2192\\trepo := NewPolicyRepository(nil, nil, nil)\\n   109\u2192\\tselectorCache := repo.GetSelectorCache()\\n   110\u2192\\n   111\u2192\\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, selectorCache, false)\\n   112\u2192\\n   113\u2192\\tlogOutput := buf.String()\\n   114\u2192\\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\\n   115\u2192\\n   116\u2192\\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\\n   117\u2192\\terr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)\\n   118\u2192\\trequire.NoError(t, err)\\n   119\u2192\\n   120\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, \\\"policy-evaluation\\\", auditEntry[\\\"msg\\\"])\\n   121\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, false, auditEntry[\\\"result\\\"])\\n   122\u2192}\\n   123\u2192\\n   124\u2192func TestLogEvaluationWithoutSelectorCache(t *testing.T) {\\n   125\u2192\\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\\n   126\u2192\\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\\n   127\u2192\\n   128\u2192\\tctx := context.Background()\\n   129\u2192\\tpolicyKey := \\\"test-policy-no-cache\\\"\\n   130\u2192\\n   131\u2192\\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, nil, true)\\n   132\u2192\\n   133\u2192\\tlogOutput := buf.String()\\n   134\u2192\\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\\n   135\u2192\\n   136\u2192\\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\\n   137\u2192\\terr := json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)\\n   138\u2192\\trequire.NoError(t, err)\\n   139\u2192\\n   140\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, \\\"policy-evaluation\\\", auditEntry[\\\"msg\\\"])\\n   141\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, policyKey, auditEntry[logfields.PolicyKey])\\n   142\u2192}\\n   143\u2192\\n   144\u2192func TestSetLogLevel(t *testing.T) {\\n   145\u2192\\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\\n   146\u2192\\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\\n   147\u2192\\n   148\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\\n   149\u2192\\n   150\u2192\\tlogger.SetLogLevel(logrus.DebugLevel)\\n   151\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.DebugLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\\n   152\u2192\\n   153\u2192\\tlogger.SetLogLevel(logrus.WarnLevel)\\n   154\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.WarnLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\\n   155\u2192}\\n   156\u2192\\n   157\u2192func TestGetLogLevel(t *testing.T) {\\n   158\u2192\\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.ErrorLevel, nil)\\n   159\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.ErrorLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\\n   160\u2192}\\n   161\u2192\\n   162\u2192func TestConcurrentLogging(t *testing.T) {\\n   163\u2192\\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\\n   164\u2192\\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\\n   165\u2192\\n   166\u2192\\t// Test concurrent access to LogDecision\\n   167\u2192\\tdone := make(chan bool)\\n   168\u2192\\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\\n   169\u2192\\t\\tgo func(index int) {\\n   170\u2192\\t\\t\\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(3000 + uint32(index))\\n   171\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \\\"concurrent-policy\\\", \\\"allow\\\", \\\"test\\\")\\n   172\u2192\\t\\t\\tdone <- true\\n   173\u2192\\t\\t}(i)\\n   174\u2192\\t}\\n   175\u2192\\n   176\u2192\\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\\n   177\u2192\\t\\t<-done\\n   178\u2192\\t}\\n   179\u2192\\n   180\u2192\\tlogOutput := buf.String()\\n   181\u2192\\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\\n   182\u2192\\n   183\u2192\\t// Count the number of log entries\\n   184\u2192\\tlineCount := bytes.Count([]byte(logOutput), []byte(\\\"\\\\n\\\"))\\n   185\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, 10, lineCount)\\n   186\u2192}\\n   187\u2192\\n   188\u2192func TestConcurrentEvaluation(t *testing.T) {\\n   189\u2192\\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\\n   190\u2192\\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\\n   191\u2192\\n   192\u2192\\trepo := NewPolicyRepository(nil, nil, nil)\\n   193\u2192\\tselectorCache := repo.GetSelectorCache()\\n   194\u2192\\tctx := context.Background()\\n   195\u2192\\n   196\u2192\\tdone := make(chan bool)\\n   197\u2192\\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\\n   198\u2192\\t\\tgo func(index int) {\\n   199\u2192\\t\\t\\tpolicyKey := \\\"policy-\\\" + string(rune(index))\\n   200\u2192\\t\\t\\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, selectorCache, index%2 == 0)\\n   201\u2192\\t\\t\\tdone <- true\\n   202\u2192\\t\\t}(i)\\n   203\u2192\\t}\\n   204\u2192\\n   205\u2192\\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\\n   206\u2192\\t\\t<-done\\n   207\u2192\\t}\\n   208\u2192\\n   209\u2192\\tlogOutput := buf.String()\\n   210\u2192\\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\\n   211\u2192\\n   212\u2192\\t// Count the number of log entries\\n   213\u2192\\tlineCount := bytes.Count([]byte(logOutput), []byte(\\\"\\\\n\\\"))\\n   214\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, 10, lineCount)\\n   215\u2192}\\n   216\u2192\\n   217\u2192func TestMultipleDecisionsWithDifferentVerdicts(t *testing.T) {\\n   218\u2192\\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\\n   219\u2192\\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\\n   220\u2192\\n   221\u2192\\tverdicts := []string{\\\"allow\\\", \\\"deny\\\", \\\"allow\\\", \\\"deny\\\"}\\n   222\u2192\\tfor i, verdict := range verdicts {\\n   223\u2192\\t\\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(5000 + uint32(i))\\n   224\u2192\\t\\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \\\"test-policy\\\", verdict, \\\"test reason\\\")\\n   225\u2192\\t}\\n   226\u2192\\n   227\u2192\\tlogOutput := buf.String()\\n   228\u2192\\trequire.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)\\n   229\u2192\\n   230\u2192\\tlines := bytes.Split(bytes.TrimSpace([]byte(logOutput)), []byte(\\\"\\\\n\\\"))\\n   231\u2192\\trequire.Equal(t, 4, len(lines))\\n   232\u2192\\n   233\u2192\\tfor i, verdict := range verdicts {\\n   234\u2192\\t\\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\\n   235\u2192\\t\\terr := json.Unmarshal(lines[i], &auditEntry)\\n   236\u2192\\t\\trequire.NoError(t, err)\\n   237\u2192\\t\\trequire.Equal(t, verdict, auditEntry[\\\"verdict\\\"])\\n   238\u2192\\t}\\n   239\u2192}\\n   240\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code -5b17-4855-aa5c-b4b108b21427\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:27.160Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"1416d70f-ca1a-4ba4-b542-38f68402d9d2\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01Fkzi4NzT86Qi6n3xS49raP\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192package hooks\\n     5\u2192\\n     6\u2192import (\\n     7\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/lumberjack/v2\\\"\\n     8\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\\\"\\n     9\u2192)\\n    10\u2192\\n    11\u2192// FileRotationOption provides all parameters for file rotation\\n    12\u2192type FileRotationOption struct {\\n    13\u2192\\tFileName   string\\n    14\u2192\\tMaxSize    int\\n    15\u2192\\tMaxAge     int\\n    16\u2192\\tMaxBackups int\\n    17\u2192\\tLocalTime  bool\\n    18\u2192\\tCompress   bool\\n    19\u2192}\\n    20\u2192\\n    21\u2192type Option func(*FileRotationOption)\\n    22\u2192\\n    23\u2192// WithMaxSize provides way to adjust maxSize (in MBs). Defaults to\\n    24\u2192// 100 MBs.\\n    25\u2192func WithMaxSize(maxSize int) Option {\\n    26\u2192\\treturn func(option *FileRotationOption) {\\n    27\u2192\\t\\toption.MaxSize = maxSize\\n    28\u2192\\t}\\n    29\u2192}\\n    30\u2192\\n    31\u2192// WithMaxAge provides way to adjust max age (in days). The default is\\n    32\u2192// not to remove old log files based on age.\\n    33\u2192func WithMaxAge(maxAge int) Option {\\n    34\u2192\\treturn func(option *FileRotationOption) {\\n    35\u2192\\t\\toption.MaxAge = maxAge\\n    36\u2192\\t}\\n    37\u2192}\\n    38\u2192\\n    39\u2192// WithMaxBackups provides way to adjust max number of backups. Defaults\\n    40\u2192// to retain all old log files though MaxAge may still cause them to get\\n    41\u2192// deleted.\\n    42\u2192func WithMaxBackups(MaxBackups int) Option {\\n    43\u2192\\treturn func(option *FileRotationOption) {\\n    44\u2192\\t\\toption.MaxBackups = MaxBackups\\n    45\u2192\\t}\\n    46\u2192}\\n    47\u2192\\n    48\u2192// EnableLocalTime is to determine if the time used for formatting the\\n    49\u2192// timestamps in backup files is the computer's local time.  The default\\n    50\u2192// is to use UTC time.\\n    51\u2192func EnableLocalTime() Option {\\n    52\u2192\\treturn func(option *FileRotationOption) {\\n    53\u2192\\t\\toption.LocalTime = true\\n    54\u2192\\t}\\n    55\u2192}\\n    56\u2192\\n    57\u2192// EnableCompression is to enable old log file gzip compression. Defaults\\n    58\u2192// to false.\\n    59\u2192func EnableCompression() Option {\\n    60\u2192\\treturn func(option *FileRotationOption) {\\n    61\u2192\\t\\toption.Compress = true\\n    62\u2192\\t}\\n    63\u2192}\\n    64\u2192\\n    65\u2192// FileRotationLogHook stores the configuration of the hook\\n    66\u2192type FileRotationLogHook struct {\\n    67\u2192\\tlogger *lumberjack.Logger\\n    68\u2192}\\n    69\u2192\\n    70\u2192// NewFileRotationLogHook creates a new FileRotationLogHook*/\\n    71\u2192func NewFileRotationLogHook(fileName string, opts ...Option) *FileRotationLogHook {\\n    72\u2192\\toptions := &FileRotationOption{\\n    73\u2192\\t\\tFileName:  fileName,\\n    74\u2192\\t\\tMaxSize:   100,   // MBs\\n    75\u2192\\t\\tLocalTime: false, // UTC\\n    76\u2192\\t\\tCompress:  false, // no compression with gzip\\n    77\u2192\\t}\\n    78\u2192\\n    79\u2192\\tfor _, opt := range opts {\\n    80\u2192\\t\\topt(options)\\n    81\u2192\\t}\\n    82\u2192\\n    83\u2192\\tlogger := &lumberjack.Logger{\\n    84\u2192\\t\\tFilename:   options.FileName,\\n    85\u2192\\t\\tMaxSize:    options.MaxSize,\\n    86\u2192\\t\\tMaxAge:     options.MaxAge,\\n    87\u2192\\t\\tMaxBackups: options.MaxBackups,\\n    88\u2192\\t\\tLocalTime:  options.LocalTime,\\n    89\u2192\\t\\tCompress:   options.Compress,\\n    90\u2192\\t}\\n    91\u2192\\n    92\u2192\\treturn &FileRotationLogHook{\\n    93\u2192\\t\\tlogger: logger,\\n    94\u2192\\t}\\n    95\u2192}\\n    96\u2192\\n    97\u2192// Fire is called when a log event is fired.\\n    98\u2192func (hook *FileRotationLogHook) Fire(entry *logrus.Entry) error {\\n    99\u2192\\tline, err := entry.String()\\n   100\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   101\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n   102\u2192\\t}\\n   103\u2192\\n   104\u2192\\t_, err = hook.logger.Write([]byte(line))\\n   105\u2192\\tif err != nil {\\n   106\u2192\\t\\treturn err\\n   107\u2192\\t}\\n   108\u2192\\n   109\u2192\\treturn nil\\n   110\u2192}\\n   111\u2192\\n   112\u2192// Levels returns the available logging levels\\n   113\u2192func (hook *FileRotationLogHook) Levels() []logrus.Level {\\n   114\u2192\\treturn logrus.AllLevels\\n   115\u2192}\\n   116\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"0f5fb537-3a82-475f-821b-deb82384bbec\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:27.199Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"1416d70f-ca1a-4ba4-b542-38f68402d9d2\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"0f5fb537-3a82-475f-821b-deb82384bbec\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01Fkzi4NzT86Qi6n3xS49raP\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01Fkzi4NzT86Qi6n3xS49raP\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:27.199Z\",\"uuid\":\"ed9a4629-43ed-42e3-9202-8a440fd7734f\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"a38df8e6-bfde-4eb8-b358-8766cd6c1fa9\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_017ZVf5A9nmtPHe6ZCqqsQYh\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192package exporter\\n     5\u2192\\n     6\u2192import (\\n     7\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n     8\u2192\\t\\\"encoding/json\\\"\\n     9\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n    10\u2192\\t\\\"io\\\"\\n    11\u2192\\t\\\"os\\\"\\n    12\u2192\\n    13\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/lumberjack/v2\\\"\\n    14\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\\\"\\n    15\u2192\\n    16\u2192\\tflowpb \\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/api/v1/flow\\\"\\n    17\u2192\\tobserverpb \\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/api/v1/observer\\\"\\n    18\u2192\\tv1 \\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/hubble/api/v1\\\"\\n    19\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/hubble/exporter/exporteroption\\\"\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/hubble/filters\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\tnodeTypes \\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/node/types\\\"\\n    22\u2192)\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192// exporter is an implementation of OnDecodedEvent interface that writes Hubble events to a file.\\n    25\u2192type exporter struct {\\n    26\u2192\\tFlowLogExporter\\n    27\u2192\\tctx     context.Context\\n    28\u2192\\tlogger  logrus.FieldLogger\\n    29\u2192\\tencoder *json.Encoder\\n    30\u2192\\twriter  io.WriteCloser\\n    31\u2192\\tflow    *flowpb.Flow\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192\\topts exporteroption.Options\\n    34\u2192}\\n    35\u2192\\n    36\u2192// NewExporter initializes an exporter.\\n    37\u2192func NewExporter(\\n    38\u2192\\tctx context.Context,\\n    39\u2192\\tlogger logrus.FieldLogger,\\n    40\u2192\\toptions ...exporteroption.Option) (*exporter, error) {\\n    41\u2192\\topts := exporteroption.Default // start with defaults\\n    42\u2192\\tfor _, opt := range options {\\n    43\u2192\\t\\tif err := opt(&opts); err != nil {\\n    44\u2192\\t\\t\\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\\\"failed to apply option: %w\\\", err)\\n    45\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    46\u2192\\t}\\n    47\u2192\\tlogger.WithField(\\\"options\\\", opts).Info(\\\"Configuring Hubble event exporter\\\")\\n    48\u2192\\tvar writer io.WriteCloser\\n    49\u2192\\t// If hubble-export-file-path is set to \\\"stdout\\\", use os.Stdout as the writer.\\n    50\u2192\\tif opts.Path == \\\"stdout\\\" {\\n    51\u2192\\t\\twriter = &noopWriteCloser{w: os.Stdout}\\n    52\u2192\\t} else {\\n    53\u2192\\t\\twriter = &lumberjack.Logger{\\n    54\u2192\\t\\t\\tFilename:   opts.Path,\\n    55\u2192\\t\\t\\tMaxSize:    opts.MaxSizeMB,\\n    56\u2192\\t\\t\\tMaxBackups: opts.MaxBackups,\\n    57\u2192\\t\\t\\tCompress:   opts.Compress,\\n    58\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    59\u2192\\t}\\n    60\u2192\\treturn newExporter(ctx, logger, writer, opts)\\n    61\u2192}\\n    62\u2192\\n    63\u2192// newExporter let's you supply your own WriteCloser for tests.\\n    64\u2192func newExporter(ctx context.Context, logger logrus.FieldLogger, writer io.WriteCloser, opts exporteroption.Options) (*exporter, error) {\\n    65\u2192\\tencoder := json.NewEncoder(writer)\\n    66\u2192\\tvar flow *flowpb.Flow\\n    67\u2192\\tif opts.FieldMask.Active() {\\n    68\u2192\\t\\tflow = new(flowpb.Flow)\\n    69\u2192\\t\\topts.FieldMask.Alloc(flow.ProtoReflect())\\n    70\u2192\\t}\\n    71\u2192\\treturn &exporter{\\n    72\u2192\\t\\tctx:     ctx,\\n    73\u2192\\t\\tlogger:  logger,\\n    74\u2192\\t\\tencoder: encoder,\\n    75\u2192\\t\\twriter:  writer,\\n    76\u2192\\t\\tflow:    flow,\\n    77\u2192\\t\\topts:    opts,\\n    78\u2192\\t}, nil\\n    79\u2192}\\n    80\u2192\\n    81\u2192// eventToExportEvent converts Event to ExportEvent.\\n    82\u2192func (e *exporter) eventToExportEvent(event *v1.Event) *observerpb.ExportEvent {\\n    83\u2192\\tswitch ev := event.Event.(type) {\\n    84\u2192\\tcase *flowpb.Flow:\\n    85\u2192\\t\\tif e.opts.FieldMask.Active() {\\n    86\u2192\\t\\t\\te.opts.FieldMask.Copy(e.flow.ProtoReflect(), ev.ProtoReflect())\\n    87\u2192\\t\\t\\tev = e.flow\\n    88\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    89\u2192\\t\\treturn &observerpb.ExportEvent{\\n    90\u2192\\t\\t\\tTime:     ev.GetTime(),\\n    91\u2192\\t\\t\\tNodeName: ev.GetNodeName(),\\n    92\u2192\\t\\t\\tResponseTypes: &observerpb.ExportEvent_Flow{\\n    93\u2192\\t\\t\\t\\tFlow: ev,\\n    94\u2192\\t\\t\\t},\\n    95\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    96\u2192\\tcase *flowpb.LostEvent:\\n    97\u2192\\t\\treturn &observerpb.ExportEvent{\\n    98\u2192\\t\\t\\tTime:     event.Timestamp,\\n    99\u2192\\t\\t\\tNodeName: nodeTypes.GetName(),\\n   100\u2192\\t\\t\\tResponseTypes: &observerpb.ExportEvent_LostEvents{\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192package logging\\n     5\u2192\\n     6\u2192import (\\n     7\u2192\\t\\\"context\\\"\\n     8\u2192\\t\\\"log/slog\\\"\\n     9\u2192\\t\\\"os\\\"\\n    10\u2192\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n    11\u2192\\t\\\"time\\\"\\n    12\u2192\\n    13\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\\\"\\n    14\u2192)\\n    15\u2192\\n    16\u2192// SlogNopHandler discards all logs.\\n    17\u2192var SlogNopHandler slog.Handler = nopHandler{}\\n    18\u2192\\n    19\u2192type nopHandler struct{}\\n    20\u2192\\n    21\u2192func (nopHandler) Enabled(context.Context, slog.Level) bool  { return false }\\n    22\u2192func (nopHandler) Handle(context.Context, slog.Record) error { return nil }\\n    23\u2192func (n nopHandler) WithAttrs([]slog.Attr) slog.Handler      { return n }\\n    24\u2192func (n nopHandler) WithGroup(string) slog.Handler           { return n }\\n    25\u2192\\n    26\u2192var slogHandlerOpts = &slog.HandlerOptions{\\n    27\u2192\\tAddSource:   false,\\n    28\u2192\\tLevel:       slog.LevelInfo,\\n    29\u2192\\tReplaceAttr: replaceLevelAndDropTime,\\n    30\u2192}\\n    31\u2192\\n    32\u2192// Default slog logger. Will be overwritten once initializeSlog is called.\\n    33\u2192var DefaultSlogLogger *slog.Logger = slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(\\n    34\u2192\\tos.Stderr,\\n    35\u2192\\tslogHandlerOpts,\\n    36\u2192))\\n    37\u2192\\n    38\u2192func slogLevel(l logrus.Level) slog.Level {\\n    39\u2192\\tswitch l {\\n    40\u2192\\tcase logrus.DebugLevel, logrus.TraceLevel:\\n    41\u2192\\t\\treturn slog.LevelDebug\\n    42\u2192\\tcase logrus.InfoLevel:\\n    43\u2192\\t\\treturn slog.LevelInfo\\n    44\u2192\\tcase logrus.WarnLevel:\\n    45\u2192\\t\\treturn slog.LevelWarn\\n    46\u2192\\tcase logrus.ErrorLevel, logrus.PanicLevel, logrus.FatalLevel:\\n    47\u2192\\t\\treturn slog.LevelError\\n    48\u2192\\tdefault:\\n    49\u2192\\t\\treturn slog.LevelInfo\\n    50\u2192\\t}\\n    51\u2192}\\n    52\u2192\\n    53\u2192// Approximates the logrus output via slog for job groups during the transition\\n    54\u2192// phase.\\n    55\u2192func initializeSlog(logOpts LogOptions, useStdout bool) {\\n    56\u2192\\topts := *slogHandlerOpts\\n    57\u2192\\topts.Level = slogLevel(logOpts.GetLogLevel())\\n    58\u2192\\n    59\u2192\\tlogFormat := logOpts.GetLogFormat()\\n    60\u2192\\tswitch logFormat {\\n    61\u2192\\tcase LogFormatJSON, LogFormatText:\\n    62\u2192\\t\\topts.ReplaceAttr = replaceLevelAndDropTime\\n    63\u2192\\tcase LogFormatJSONTimestamp, LogFormatTextTimestamp:\\n    64\u2192\\t\\topts.ReplaceAttr = replaceLevel\\n    65\u2192\\t}\\n    66\u2192\\n    67\u2192\\twriter := os.Stderr\\n    68\u2192\\tif useStdout {\\n    69\u2192\\t\\twriter = os.Stdout\\n    70\u2192\\t}\\n    71\u2192\\n    72\u2192\\tswitch logFormat {\\n    73\u2192\\tcase LogFormatJSON, LogFormatJSONTimestamp:\\n    74\u2192\\t\\tDefaultSlogLogger = slog.New(slog.NewJSONHandler(\\n    75\u2192\\t\\t\\twriter,\\n    76\u2192\\t\\t\\t&opts,\\n    77\u2192\\t\\t))\\n    78\u2192\\tcase LogFormatText, LogFormatTextTimestamp:\\n    79\u2192\\t\\tDefaultSlogLogger = slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(\\n    80\u2192\\t\\t\\twriter,\\n    81\u2192\\t\\t\\t&opts,\\n    82\u2192\\t\\t))\\n    83\u2192\\t}\\n    84\u2192}\\n    85\u2192\\n    86\u2192func replaceLevel(groups []string, a slog.Attr) slog.Attr {\\n    87\u2192\\tswitch a.Key {\\n    88\u2192\\tcase slog.TimeKey:\\n    89\u2192\\t\\t// Adjust to timestamp format that logrus uses; except that we can't\\n    90\u2192\\t\\t// force slog to quote the value like logrus does...\\n    91\u2192\\t\\treturn slog.String(slog.TimeKey, a.Value.Time().Format(time.RFC3339))\\n    92\u2192\\tcase slog.LevelKey:\\n    93\u2192\\t\\t// Lower-case the log level\\n    94\u2192\\t\\treturn slog.Attr{\\n    95\u2192\\t\\t\\tKey:   a.Key,\\n    96\u2192\\t\\t\\tValue: slog.StringValue(strings.ToLower(a.Value.String())),\\n    97\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    98\u2192\\t}\\n    99\u2192\\treturn a\\n   100\u2192}\\n   101\u2192\\n   102\u2192func replaceLevelAndDropTime(groups []string, a slog.Attr) slog.Attr {\\n   103\u2192\\tswitch a.Key {\\n   104\u2192\\tcase slog.TimeKey:\\n   105\u2192\\t\\t// Drop timestamps\\n   106\u2192\\t\\treturn slog.Attr{}\\n   107\u2192\\tcase slog.LevelKey:\\n   108\u2192\\t\\t// Lower-case the log level\\n   109\u2192\\t\\treturn slog.Attr{\\n   110\u2192\\t\\t\\tKey:   a.Key,\\n   111\u2192\\t\\t\\tValue: slog.StringValue(strings.ToLower(a.Value.String())),\\n   112\u2192\\t\\t}\\n   113\u2192\\t}\\n   114\u2192\\treturn a\\n   115\u2192}\\n   116\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"ab6f1ff0-9477-4e82-bfc7-0d1390ee6512\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:32.639Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"0715f02f-1e07-4a3d-8191-7021d43ad310\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"ab6f1ff0-9477-4e82-bfc7-0d1390ee6512\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01DdTfcoNonHThcVVxJtQ6xn\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01DdTfcoNonHThcVVxJtQ6xn\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:32.639Z\",\"uuid\":\"4be4f541-aea5-4a36-90f6-f0fee0331042\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"57d7461d-6e21-46b0-97b5-33f79551008f\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01Fr2EFHZ8Sv1hNw7cuPSgFE\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"c9278e64-942d-4ffa-8cda-41cb4491f57d\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:32.643Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"57d7461d-6e21-46b0-97b5-33f79551008f\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"c9278e64-942d-4ffa-8cda-41cb4491f57d\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_0156PdnnuKraJDCxZo2QZawx\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Let     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192package policy\\n     5\u2192\\n     6\u2192import (\\n     7\u2192\\t\\\"io\\\"\\n     8\u2192\\tstdlog \\\"log\\\"\\n     9\u2192\\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n    10\u2192\\t\\\"strings\\\"\\n    11\u2192\\n    12\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/api/v1/models\\\"\\n    13\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/labels\\\"\\n    14\u2192)\\n    15\u2192\\n    16\u2192type Tracing int\\n    17\u2192\\n    18\u2192const (\\n    19\u2192\\tTRACE_DISABLED Tracing = iota\\n    20\u2192\\tTRACE_ENABLED\\n    21\u2192\\tTRACE_VERBOSE\\n    22\u2192)\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192// TraceEnabled returns true if the SearchContext requests tracing.\\n    25\u2192func (s *SearchContext) TraceEnabled() bool {\\n    26\u2192\\treturn s.Trace != TRACE_DISABLED\\n    27\u2192}\\n    28\u2192\\n    29\u2192// PolicyTrace logs the given message into the SearchContext logger only if\\n    30\u2192// TRACE_ENABLED or TRACE_VERBOSE is enabled in the receiver's SearchContext.\\n    31\u2192func (s *SearchContext) PolicyTrace(format string, a ...interface{}) {\\n    32\u2192\\tif s.TraceEnabled() {\\n    33\u2192\\t\\tlog.Debugf(format, a...)\\n    34\u2192\\t\\tif s.Logging != nil {\\n    35\u2192\\t\\t\\tformat = \\\"%-\\\" + s.CallDepth() + \\\"s\\\" + format\\n    36\u2192\\t\\t\\ta = append([]interface{}{\\\"\\\"}, a...)\\n    37\u2192\\t\\t\\ts.Logging.Printf(format, a...)\\n    38\u2192\\t\\t}\\n    39\u2192\\t}\\n    40\u2192}\\n    41\u2192\\n    42\u2192// PolicyTraceVerbose logs the given message into the SearchContext logger only\\n    43\u2192// if TRACE_VERBOSE is enabled in the receiver's SearchContext.\\n    44\u2192func (s *SearchContext) PolicyTraceVerbose(format string, a ...interface{}) {\\n    45\u2192\\tswitch s.Trace {\\n    46\u2192\\tcase TRACE_VERBOSE:\\n    47\u2192\\t\\tlog.Debugf(format, a...)\\n    48\u2192\\t\\tif s.Logging != nil {\\n    49\u2192\\t\\t\\ts.Logging.Printf(format, a...)\\n    50\u2192\\t\\t}\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code (\",\"is_error\":false}]},\"uuid\":\"d7a8339d-eced-4608-8cd3-c1521c1bb7ba\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:38.648Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"fe1c4a99-d32f-4f56-a7ec-3bf95d5e1fe2\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"d7a8339d-eced-4608-8cd3-c1521c1bb7ba\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01ArkiU82mTEniavxnzHq8v3\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"tool_use\",\"id\":\"toolu_01RUnTr3Cz7GtxZ58iswNFLD\",\"name\":\"Read\",\"input\":{\"file_path\":\"/workspace/pkg/policy/resolve.go\",\"limit\":60},\"caller\":{\"type\":\"direct\"}}],\"stop_reason\":\"tool_use\",\"stop_sequence\":null,\"usage\":{\"input_tokens\":4,\"cache_creation_input_tokens\":167,\"cache_read_input_tokens\":48564,\"output_tokens\":79,\"server_tool_use\":{\"web_search_requests\":0,\"web_fetch_requests\":0},\"service_tier\":\"standard\",\"cache_creation\":{\"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens\":167,\"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens\":0},\"inference_geo\":\"\",\"iterations\":[],\"speed\":\"standard\"}},\"requestId\":\"req_011CYf38GjhYk4kxZuvsuw62\",\"type\":\"assistant\",\"uuid\":\"0d3a82c8-5ee1-4ddd-a2c3-722f61ab6c5f\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:40.567Z\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"0d3a82c8-5ee1-4ddd-a2c3-722f61ab6c5f\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"type\":\"user\",\"message\":{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":[{\"tool_use_id\":\"toolu_01RUnTr3Cz7GtxZ58iswNFLD\",\"type\":\"tool_result\",\"content\":\"     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192package policy\\n     5\u2192\\n     6\u2192import (\\n     7\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\\\"\\n     8\u2192)\\n     9\u2192\\n    10\u2192// selectorPolicy is a structure which contains the resolved policy for a\\n    11\u2192// particular Identity across all layers (L3, L4, and L7), with the policy\\n    12\u2192// still determined in terms of EndpointSelectors.\\n    13\u2192type selectorPolicy struct {\\n    14\u2192\\t// Revision is the revision of the policy repository used to generate\\n    15\u2192\\t// this selectorPolicy.\\n    16\u2192\\tRevision uint64\\n    17\u2192\\n    18\u2192\\t// SelectorCache managing selectors in L4Policy\\n    19\u2192\\tSelectorCache *SelectorCache\\n    20\u2192\\n    21\u2192\\t// L4Policy contains the computed L4 and L7 policy.\\n    22\u2192\\tL4Policy L4Policy\\n    23\u2192\\n    24\u2192\\t// IngressPolicyEnabled specifies whether this policy contains any policy\\n    25\u2192\\t// at ingress.\\n    26\u2192\\tIngressPolicyEnabled bool\\n    27\u2192\\n    28\u2192\\t// EgressPolicyEnabled specifies whether this policy contains any policy\\n    29\u2192\\t// at egress.\\n    30\u2192\\tEgressPolicyEnabled bool\\n    31\u2192}\\n    32\u2192\\n    33\u2192func (p *selectorPolicy) Attach(ctx PolicyContext) {\\n    34\u2192\\tp.L4Policy.Attach(ctx)\\n    35\u2192}\\n    36\u2192\\n    37\u2192// EndpointPolicy is a structure which contains the resolved policy across all\\n    38\u2192// layers (L3, L4, and L7), distilled against a set of identities.\\n    39\u2192type EndpointPolicy struct {\\n    40\u2192\\t// Note that all Endpoints sharing the same identity will be\\n    41\u2192\\t// referring to a shared selectorPolicy!\\n    42\u2192\\t*selectorPolicy\\n    43\u2192\\n    44\u2192\\t// policyMapState contains the state of this policy as it relates to the\\n    45\u2192\\t// datapath. In the future, this will be factored out of this object to\\n    46\u2192\\t// decouple the policy as it relates to the datapath vs. its userspace\\n    47\u2192\\t// representation.\\n    48\u2192\\t// It maps each Key to the proxy port if proxy redirection is needed.\\n    49\u2192\\t// Proxy port 0 indicates no proxy redirection.\\n    50\u2192\\t// All fields within the Key and the proxy port must be in host byte-order.\\n    51\u2192\\t// Must only be accessed with PolicyOwner (aka Endpoint) lock taken.\\n    52\u2192\\tpolicyMapState MapState\\n    53\u2192\\n    54\u2192\\t// policyMapChanges collects pending changes to the PolicyMapState\\n    55\u2192\\tpolicyMapChanges MapChanges\\n    56\u2192\\n    57\u2192\\t// PolicyOwner describes any type which consumes this EndpointPolicy object.\\n    58\u2192\\tPolicyOwner PolicyOwner\\n    59\u2192}\\n    60\u2192\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\\n</system-reminder>\\n\"}]},\"uuid\":\"8c766f01-9b02-4cf8-b269-0636baa80d03\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:40.596Z\",\"sourceToolAssistantUUID\":\"0d3a82c8-5ee1-4ddd-a2c3-722f61ab6c5f\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"8c766f01-9b02-4cf8-b269-0636baa80d03\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"type\":\"progress\",\"data\":{\"type\":\"hook_progress\",\"hookEvent\":\"PostToolUse\",\"hookName\":\"PostToolUse:Read\",\"command\":\"callback\"},\"parentToolUseID\":\"toolu_01RUnTr3Cz7GtxZ58iswNFLD\",\"toolUseID\":\"toolu_01RUnTr3Cz7GtxZ58iswNFLD\",\"timestamp\":\"2026-03-02T22:24:40.596Z\",\"uuid\":\"43893a13-adfc-428c-880d-ede26455a6f9\"}\n{\"parentUuid\":\"43893a13-adfc-428c-880d-ede26455a6f9\",\"isSidechain\":true,\"userType\":\"external\",\"cwd\":\"/workspace\",\"sessionId\":\"2b267b83-8a0d-48dc-9b6d-ff294d81697b\",\"version\":\"2.1.63\",\"gitBranch\":\"main\",\"agentId\":\"ae78e49c80e908185\",\"slug\":\"crispy-launching-wigderson\",\"message\":{\"model\":\"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001\",\"id\":\"msg_01U3U1B9sRR3ntir85igf5g9\",\"type\":\"message\",\"role\":\"assistant\",\"content\":[{\"type\":\"text\",\"text\":\"Let     1\u2192// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\\n     2\u2192// Copyright Authors of Cilium\\n     3\u2192\\n     4\u2192package policy\\n     5\u2192\\n     6\u2192import (\\n     7\u2192\\t\\\"fmt\\\"\\n     8\u2192\\t\\\"net\\\"\\n     9\u2192\\t\\\"slices\\\"\\n    10\u2192\\t\\\"strconv\\\"\\n    11\u2192\\n    12\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/hashicorp/go-hclog\\\"\\n    13\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\\\"\\n    14\u2192\\t\\\"golang.org/x/exp/maps\\\"\\n    15\u2192\\n    16\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/container/bitlpm\\\"\\n    17\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\\\"\\n    18\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/ip\\\"\\n    19\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/labels\\\"\\n    20\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock\\\"\\n    21\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\\\"\\n    22\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/option\\\"\\n    23\u2192\\t\\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/policy/trafficdirection\\\"\\n    24\u2192\\tpolicyTypes \\\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/policy/types\\\"\\n    25\u2192)\\n    26\u2192\\n    27\u2192// Key and Keys are types used both internally and externally.\\n    28\u2192// The types have been lifted out, but an alias is being used\\n    29\u2192// so we don't have to change all the code everywhere.\\n    30\u2192//\\n    31\u2192// Do not use these types outside of pkg/policy or pkg/endpoint,\\n    32\u2192// lest ye find yourself with hundreds of unnecessary imports.\\n    33\u2192type Key = policyTypes.Key\\n    34\u2192type Keys = policyTypes.Keys\\n    35\u2192\\n    36\u2192var (\\n    37\u2192\\t// localHostKey represents an ingress L3 allow from the local host.\\n    38\u2192\\tlocalHostKey = Key{\\n    39\u2192\\t\\tIdentity:         identity.ReservedIdentityHost.Uint32(),\\n    40\u2192\\t\\tInvertedPortMask: 0xffff, // This is a wildcard\\n\\n<system-reminder>\\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code.
