  "originalFile": "// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n\npackage policy\n\nimport (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\"\n)\n\n// setupAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with a buffer for testing.\nfunc setupAuditLogger(t *testing.T, level logrus.Level) (*bytes.Buffer, *PolicyAuditLogger) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(level, buf)\n\treturn buf, logger\n}\n\n// parseAuditLog parses a JSON audit log entry from the buffer output.\nfunc parseAuditLog(t *testing.T, logOutput string) map[string]interface{} {\n\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\n\terr := json.Unmarshal(bytes.TrimSpace([]byte(logOutput)), &auditEntry)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\treturn auditEntry\n}\n\nfunc TestNewPolicyAuditLogger(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test without audit output\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.DebugLevel, nil)\n\trequire.NotNil(t, logger)\n\n\t// Test with audit output\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\trequire.NotNil(t, logger)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n\n\t// Verify output buffer is empty at start\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"\", buf.String())\n}\n\nfunc TestLogDecision(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(1001)\n\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"test-policy\", \"allow\", \"matches selector\")\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\tauditEntry := parseAuditLog(t, logOutput)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-decision\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, float64(1001), auditEntry[\"identity\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"test-policy\", auditEntry[\"policy\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"allow\", auditEntry[\"verdict\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"matches selector\", auditEntry[\"reason\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogDecisionDeny(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(2002)\n\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"restrictive-policy\", \"deny\", \"denied by rule\")\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\tauditEntry := parseAuditLog(t, logOutput)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-decision\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, float64(2002), auditEntry[\"identity\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"restrictive-policy\", auditEntry[\"policy\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"deny\", auditEntry[\"verdict\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"denied by rule\", auditEntry[\"reason\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogEvaluation(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tpolicyKey := \"test-policy-key\"\n\n\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, nil, true)\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\tauditEntry := parseAuditLog(t, logOutput)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-evaluation\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, policyKey, auditEntry[logfields.PolicyKey])\n\trequire.Equal(t, true, auditEntry[\"result\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogEvaluationFailure(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tpolicyKey := \"failing-policy\"\n\n\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, nil, false)\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\tauditEntry := parseAuditLog(t, logOutput)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-evaluation\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, policyKey, auditEntry[logfields.PolicyKey])\n\trequire.Equal(t, false, auditEntry[\"result\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogLevelManagement(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n\n\tlogger.SetLogLevel(logrus.DebugLevel)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.DebugLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n\n\tlogger.SetLogLevel(logrus.WarnLevel)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.WarnLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n}\n\nfunc TestGetLogLevelWithoutAuditLogger(t *testing.T) {\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.ErrorLevel, nil)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n}\n\nfunc TestConcurrentLogging(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\n\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\n\t\twg.Add(1)\n\t\tgo func(index int) {\n\t\t\tdefer wg.Done()\n\t\t\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(3000 + uint32(index))\n\t\t\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"concurrent-policy\", \"allow\", \"test\")\n\t\t}(i)\n\t}\n\twg.Wait()\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\tlines := bytes.Split(bytes.TrimSpace([]byte(logOutput)), []byte(\"\\n\"))\n\trequire.Equal(t, 10, len(lines))\n\n\t// Verify all entries are policy-decision messages\n\tfor _, line := range lines {\n\t\tentry := parseAuditLog(t, string(line))\n\t\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-decision\", entry[\"msg\"])\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestConcurrentEvaluation(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\n\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\n\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\n\t\twg.Add(1)\n\t\tgo func(index int) {\n\t\t\tdefer wg.Done()\n\t\t\tpolicyKey := \"policy-\" + strconv.Itoa(index)\n\t\t\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, nil, index%2 == 0)\n\t\t}(i)\n\t}\n\twg.Wait()\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\tlines := bytes.Split(bytes.TrimSpace([]byte(logOutput)), []byte(\"\\n\"))\n\trequire.Equal(t, 10, len(lines))\n\n\t// Verify all entries are policy-evaluation messages\n\tfor i, line := range lines {\n\t\tentry := parseAuditLog(t, string(line))\n\t\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-evaluation\", entry[\"msg\"])\n\t\trequire.Equal(t, i%2 == 0, entry[\"result\"])\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestMultipleDecisionsWithDifferentVerdicts(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)\n\n\tverdicts := []string{\"allow\", \"deny\", \"allow\", (\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"io\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging\"\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields\"\n)\n\nvar log = logging.DefaultLogger.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, \"policy\")\n\nconst (\n\tauditLoggerSubsys = \"policy-audit\"\n\tlogMsgDecision    = \"policy-decision\"\n\tlogMsgEvaluation  = \"policy-evaluation\"\n\tfieldPolicy       = \"policy\"\n\tfieldVerdict      = \"verdict\"\n\tfieldReason       = \"reason\"\n\tfieldResult       = \"result\"\n)\n\n// PolicyAuditLogger provides structured logging of policy evaluation decisions\n// for audit and debugging purposes.\ntype PolicyAuditLogger struct {\n\tmutex      lock.Mutex\n\tlogger     *logrus.Entry\n\tauditLog   *logrus.Logger\n\tauditEntry *logrus.Entry\n}\n\n// NewPolicyAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with the specified configuration.\n// If auditOutput is provided, audit logs will be written to it in addition to the standard logger.\nfunc NewPolicyAuditLogger(logLevel logrus.Level, auditOutput io.Writer) *PolicyAuditLogger {\n\tpal := &PolicyAuditLogger{\n\t\tlogger: log.WithField(\"component\", \"audit\"),\n\t}\n\n\t// If auditOutput is provided, create a separate audit logger\n\tif auditOutput != nil {\n\t\tauditLog := logrus.New()\n\t\tauditLog.SetFormatter(&logrus.JSONFormatter{\n\t\t\tDisableTimestamp: false,\n\t\t\tTimestampFormat:  time.RFC3339Nano,\n\t\t})\n\t\tauditLog.SetOutput(auditOutput)\n\t\tauditLog.SetLevel(logLevel)\n\t\tpal.auditLog = auditLog\n\t\tpal.auditEntry = auditLog.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, auditLoggerSubsys)\n\t}\n\n\treturn pal\n}\n\n// LogDecision logs a policy decision with the given parameters.\n// This is used for audit trail of policy verdicts.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogDecision(\n\tidentity identity.NumericIdentity,\n\tpolicy string,\n\tverdict string,\n\treason string,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.Identity: identity,\n\t\tfieldPolicy:        policy,\n\t\tfieldVerdict:       verdict,\n\t\tfieldReason:        reason,\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger\n\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\tswitch verdict {\n\tcase \"allow\":\n\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision: allow\")\n\tcase \"deny\":\n\t\tentry.Warning(\"Policy decision: deny\")\n\tdefault:\n\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy decision\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to audit logger if configured\n\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(logMsgDecision)\n\t}\n}\n\n// LogEvaluation logs a policy evaluation with full context.\n// This provides detailed information about how a policy was evaluated against identities.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogEvaluation(\n\tctx context.Context,\n\tpolicyKey string,\n\tselectorCache *SelectorCache,\n\tresult bool,\n) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tfields := logrus.Fields{\n\t\tlogfields.PolicyKey: policyKey,\n\t\tfieldResult:         result,\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to standard logger\n\tentry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)\n\tif result {\n\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy evaluation completed: pass\")\n\t} else {\n\t\tentry.Debug(\"Policy evaluation completed: fail\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Log to audit logger if configured\n\tif pal.auditEntry != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(logMsgEvaluation)\n\t}\n}\n\n// SetLogLevel updates the log level for the audit logger if configured.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) SetLogLevel(level logrus.Level) {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tif pal.auditLog != nil {\n\t\tpal.auditLog.SetLevel(level)\n\t}\n}\n\n// GetLogLevel returns the current log level of the audit logger, or InfoLevel if not configured.\nfunc (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) GetLogLevel() logrus.Level {\n\tpal.mutex.Lock()\n\tdefer pal.mutex.Unlock()\n\n\tif pal.auditLog != nil {\n\t\treturn pal.auditLog.Level\n\t}\n\treturn logrus.InfoLevel\n}\n",

  "file": {
    "content": "// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0\n// Copyright Authors of Cilium\n\npackage policy\n\nimport (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"strconv\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n\t\"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n\n\t\"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity\"\n)\n\n// setupAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with a buffer for testing.\nfunc setupAuditLogger(t *testing.T, level logrus.Level) (*bytes.Buffer, *PolicyAuditLogger) {\n\tbuf := &bytes.Buffer{}\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(level, buf)\n\treturn buf, logger\n}\n\n// parseAuditLog parses a JSON audit log entry from the buffer output.\nfunc parseAuditLog(t *testing.T, logOutput string) map[string]interface{} {\n\tvar auditEntry map[string]interface{}\n\terr := json.Unmarshal(bytes.TrimSpace([]byte(logOutput)), &auditEntry)\n\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n\treturn auditEntry\n}\n\nfunc TestNewPolicyAuditLogger(t *testing.T) {\n\t// Test without audit output\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.DebugLevel, nil)\n\trequire.NotNil(t, logger)\n\n\t// Test with audit output\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\trequire.NotNil(t, logger)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n\n\t// Verify output buffer is empty at start\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"\", buf.String())\n}\n\nfunc TestLogDecision(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(1001)\n\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"test-policy\", \"allow\", \"matches selector\")\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\tauditEntry := parseAuditLog(t, logOutput)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-decision\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, float64(1001), auditEntry[\"identity\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"test-policy\", auditEntry[\"policy\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"allow\", auditEntry[\"verdict\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"matches selector\", auditEntry[\"reason\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogDecisionDeny(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(2002)\n\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"restrictive-policy\", \"deny\", \"denied by rule\")\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\tauditEntry := parseAuditLog(t, logOutput)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-decision\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, float64(2002), auditEntry[\"identity\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"restrictive-policy\", auditEntry[\"policy\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"deny\", auditEntry[\"verdict\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"denied by rule\", auditEntry[\"reason\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogEvaluation(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tpolicyKey := \"test-policy-key\"\n\n\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, nil, true)\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\tauditEntry := parseAuditLog(t, logOutput)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-evaluation\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, policyKey, auditEntry[logfields.PolicyKey])\n\trequire.Equal(t, true, auditEntry[\"result\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogEvaluationFailure(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\tpolicyKey := \"failing-policy\"\n\n\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, nil, false)\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\tauditEntry := parseAuditLog(t, logOutput)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-evaluation\", auditEntry[\"msg\"])\n\trequire.Equal(t, policyKey, auditEntry[logfields.PolicyKey])\n\trequire.Equal(t, false, auditEntry[\"result\"])\n}\n\nfunc TestLogLevelManagement(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n\n\tlogger.SetLogLevel(logrus.DebugLevel)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.DebugLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n\n\tlogger.SetLogLevel(logrus.WarnLevel)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.WarnLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n}\n\nfunc TestGetLogLevelWithoutAuditLogger(t *testing.T) {\n\tlogger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.ErrorLevel, nil)\n\trequire.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())\n}\n\nfunc TestConcurrentLogging(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\n\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\n\t\twg.Add(1)\n\t\tgo func(index int) {\n\t\t\tdefer wg.Done()\n\t\t\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(3000 + uint32(index))\n\t\t\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"concurrent-policy\", \"allow\", \"test\")\n\t\t}(i)\n\t}\n\twg.Wait()\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\tlines := bytes.Split(bytes.TrimSpace([]byte(logOutput)), []byte(\"\\n\"))\n\trequire.Equal(t, 10, len(lines))\n\n\t// Verify all entries are policy-decision messages\n\tfor _, line := range lines {\n\t\tentry := parseAuditLog(t, string(line))\n\t\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-decision\", entry[\"msg\"])\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestConcurrentEvaluation(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\tctx := context.Background()\n\n\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\n\tfor i := 0; i < 10; i++ {\n\t\twg.Add(1)\n\t\tgo func(index int) {\n\t\t\tdefer wg.Done()\n\t\t\tpolicyKey := \"policy-\" + strconv.Itoa(index)\n\t\t\tlogger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, nil, index%2 == 0)\n\t\t}(i)\n\t}\n\twg.Wait()\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\tlines := bytes.Split(bytes.TrimSpace([]byte(logOutput)), []byte(\"\\n\"))\n\trequire.Equal(t, 10, len(lines))\n\n\t// Verify all entries are policy-evaluation messages\n\tfor i, line := range lines {\n\t\tentry := parseAuditLog(t, string(line))\n\t\trequire.Equal(t, \"policy-evaluation\", entry[\"msg\"])\n\t\trequire.Equal(t, i%2 == 0, entry[\"result\"])\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc TestMultipleDecisionsWithDifferentVerdicts(t *testing.T) {\n\tbuf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)\n\n\tverdicts := []string{\"allow\", \"deny\", \"allow\", \"deny\"}\n\tfor i, verdict := range verdicts {\n\t\ttestID := identity.NumericIdentity(5000 + uint32(i))\n\t\tlogger.LogDecision(testID, \"test-policy\", verdict, \"test reason\")\n\t}\n\n\tlogOutput := buf.String()\n\tlines := bytes.Split(bytes.TrimSpace([]byte(logOutput)), []byte(\"\\n\"))\n\trequire.Equal(t, len(verdicts), len(lines))\n\n\tfor i, verdict := range verdicts {\n\t\tauditEntry := parseAuditLog(t, string(lines[i]))\n\t\trequire.Equal(t, verdict, auditEntry[\"verdict\"])\n\t\trequire.Equal(t, \"test-policy\", auditEntry[\"policy\"])\n\t}\n}\n",

1. WRITE /workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger.go
// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
// Copyright Authors of Cilium

package policy

import (
	"context"
	"io"

	"github.com/sirupsen/logrus"

	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity"
	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock"
	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields"
)

// PolicyAuditLogger provides structured logging of policy evaluation decisions
// for audit and debugging purposes.
type PolicyAuditLogger struct {
	mutex      lock.Mutex
	logger     *logrus.Entry
	logLevel   logrus.Level
	auditLog   *logrus.Logger
	auditEntry *logrus.Entry
}

// NewPolicyAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with the specified configuration.
// If auditOutput is provided, audit logs will be written to it in addition to the standard logger.
func NewPolicyAuditLogger(logLevel logrus.Level, auditOutput io.Writer) *PolicyAuditLogger {
	logger := log.WithField("component", "audit")

	pal := &PolicyAuditLogger{
		logger:   logger,
		logLevel: logLevel,
	}

	// If auditOutput is provided, create a separate audit logger
	if auditOutput != nil {
		auditLog := logrus.New()
		auditLog.SetFormatter(&logrus.JSONFormatter{})
		auditLog.SetOutput(auditOutput)
		auditLog.SetLevel(logLevel)
		pal.auditLog = auditLog
		pal.auditEntry = auditLog.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, "policy-audit")
	}

	return pal
}

// LogDecision logs a policy decision with the given parameters.
// This is used for audit trail of policy verdicts.
func (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogDecision(
	identity identity.NumericIdentity,
	policy string,
	verdict string,
	reason string,
) {
	pal.mutex.Lock()
	defer pal.mutex.Unlock()

	fields := logrus.Fields{
		logfields.Identity: identity,
		"policy":           policy,
		"verdict":          verdict,
		"reason":           reason,
	}

	// Log to standard logger if enabled
	if pal
... (truncated)
2. WRITE /workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger_test.go
// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
// Copyright Authors of Cilium

package policy

import (
	"bytes"
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"testing"

	"github.com/sirupsen/logrus"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"

	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity"
	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/labels"
)

func TestNewPolicyAuditLogger(t *testing.T) {
	// Test without audit output
	logger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.DebugLevel, nil)
	require.NotNil(t, logger)
	require.Equal(t, logrus.DebugLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())
	require.Nil(t, logger.auditLog)

	// Test with audit output
	buf := &bytes.Buffer{}
	logger = NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)
	require.NotNil(t, logger)
	require.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())
	require.NotNil(t, logger.auditLog)
}

func TestLogDecision(t *testing.T) {
	buf := &bytes.Buffer{}
	logger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)

	testID := identity.NumericIdentity(1001)
	logger.LogDecision(testID, "test-policy", "allow", "matches selector")

	// Verify audit log contains the decision
	logOutput := buf.String()
	require.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)

	// Parse JSON to verify structure
	var auditEntry map[string]interface{}
	err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	require.Equal(t, "policy-decision", auditEntry["msg"])
	require.Equal(t, float64(1001), auditEntry["identity"])
	require.Equal(t, "test-policy", auditEntry["policy"])
	require.Equal(t, "allow", auditEntry["verdict"])
	require.Equal(t, "matches selector", auditEntry["reason"])
}

func TestLogDecisionDeny(t *testing.T) {
	buf := &bytes.Buffer{}
	logger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)

	testID := identity.NumericIdentity(2002)
	logger.LogDecision(testID, "restrictive-policy", "deny", "denied by
... (truncated)

// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
// Copyright Authors of Cilium

package policy

import (
	"context"
	"io"

	"github.com/sirupsen/logrus"

	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity"
	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock"
	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields"
)
After
// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
// Copyright Authors of Cilium

package policy

import (
	"context"
	"io"
	"time"

	"github.com/sirupsen/logrus"

	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity"
	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/lock"
	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging"
	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/logging/logfields"
)

var log = logging.DefaultLogger.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, "policy")

const (
	auditLoggerSubsys = "policy-audit"
	logMsgDecision    = "policy-decision"
	logMsgEvaluation  = "policy-evaluation"
	fieldPolicy       = "policy"
	fieldVerdict      = "verdict"
	fieldReason       = "reason"
	fieldResult       = "result"
)
4. EDIT /workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger.go
Before
// PolicyAuditLogger provides structured logging of policy evaluation decisions
// for audit and debugging purposes.
type PolicyAuditLogger struct {
	mutex      lock.Mutex
	logger     *logrus.Entry
	logLevel   logrus.Level
	auditLog   *logrus.Logger
	auditEntry *logrus.Entry
}
After
// PolicyAuditLogger provides structured logging of policy evaluation decisions
// for audit and debugging purposes.
type PolicyAuditLogger struct {
	mutex      lock.Mutex
	logger     *logrus.Entry
	auditLog   *logrus.Logger
	auditEntry *logrus.Entry
}

// NewPolicyAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with the specified configuration.
// If auditOutput is provided, audit logs will be written to it in addition to the standard logger.
func NewPolicyAuditLogger(logLevel logrus.Level, auditOutput io.Writer) *PolicyAuditLogger {
	logger := log.WithField("component", "audit")

	pal := &PolicyAuditLogger{
		logger:   logger,
		logLevel: logLevel,
	}

	// If auditOutput is provided, create a separate audit logger
	if auditOutput != nil {
		auditLog := logrus.New()
		auditLog.SetFormatter(&logrus.JSONFormatter{})
		auditLog.SetOutput(auditOutput)
		auditLog.SetLevel(logLevel)
		pal.auditLog = auditLog
		pal.auditEntry = auditLog.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, "policy-audit")
	}

	return pal
}
After
// NewPolicyAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with the specified configuration.
// If auditOutput is provided, audit logs will be written to it in addition to the standard logger.
func NewPolicyAuditLogger(logLevel logrus.Level, auditOutput io.Writer) *PolicyAuditLogger {
	pal := &PolicyAuditLogger{
		logger: log.WithField("component", "audit"),
	}

	// If auditOutput is provided, create a separate audit logger
	if auditOutput != nil {
		auditLog := logrus.New()
		auditLog.SetFormatter(&logrus.JSONFormatter{
			DisableTimestamp: false,
			TimestampFormat:  time.RFC3339Nano,
		})
		auditLog.SetOutput(auditOutput)
		auditLog.SetLevel(logLevel)
		pal.auditLog = auditLog
		pal.auditEntry = auditLog.WithField(logfields.LogSubsys, auditLoggerSubsys)
	}

	return pal
}
6. EDIT /workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger.go
Before
// LogDecision logs a policy decision with the given parameters.
// This is used for audit trail of policy verdicts.
func (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogDecision(
	identity identity.NumericIdentity,
	policy string,
	verdict string,
	reason string,
) {
	pal.mutex.Lock()
	defer pal.mutex.Unlock()

	fields := logrus.Fields{
		logfields.Identity: identity,
		"policy":           policy,
		"verdict":          verdict,
		"reason":           reason,
	}

	// Log to standard logger if enabled
	if pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {
		entry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)
		switch verdict {
		case "allow":
			entry.Debug("Policy decision: allow")
		case "deny":
			entry.Warning("Policy decision: deny")
		default:
			entry.Debug("Policy decision")
		}
	}

	// Log to audit logger if configured
	if pal.auditEntry != nil {
		pal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info("policy-decision")
	}
}
After
// LogDecision logs a policy decision with the given parameters.
// This is used for audit trail of policy verdicts.
func (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogDecision(
	identity identity.NumericIdentity,
	policy string,
	verdict string,
	reason string,
) {
	pal.mutex.Lock()
	defer pal.mutex.Unlock()

	fields := logrus.Fields{
		logfields.Identity: identity,
		fieldPolicy:        policy,
		fieldVerdict:       verdict,
		fieldReason:        reason,
	}

	// Log to standard logger
	entry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)
	switch verdict {
	case "allow":
		entry.Debug("Policy decision: allow")
	case "deny":
		entry.Warning("Policy decision: deny")
	default:
		entry.Debug("Policy decision")
	}

	// Log to audit logger if configured
	if pal.auditEntry != nil {
		pal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(logMsgDecision)
	}
}
7. EDIT /workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger.go
Before
// LogEvaluation logs a policy evaluation with full context.
// This provides detailed information about how a policy was evaluated against identities.
func (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogEvaluation(
	ctx context.Context,
	policyKey string,
	selectorCache *SelectorCache,
	result bool,
) {
	pal.mutex.Lock()
	defer pal.mutex.Unlock()

	fields := logrus.Fields{
		logfields.PolicyKey: policyKey,
		"result":            result,
	}

	// Add selector cache info if available
	if selectorCache != nil {
		// Get the model representation for audit purposes
		model := selectorCache.GetModel()
		fields["selectors_count"] = len(model)
	}

	// Log to standard logger if enabled
	if pal.logger.Logger.Level >= pal.logLevel {
		entry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)
		if result {
			entry.Debug("Policy evaluation completed: pass")
		} else {
			entry.Debug("Policy evaluation completed: fail")
		}
	}

	// Log to audit logger if configured
	if pal.auditEntry != nil {
		pal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info("policy-evaluation")
	}
}
After
// LogEvaluation logs a policy evaluation with full context.
// This provides detailed information about how a policy was evaluated against identities.
func (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) LogEvaluation(
	ctx context.Context,
	policyKey string,
	selectorCache *SelectorCache,
	result bool,
) {
	pal.mutex.Lock()
	defer pal.mutex.Unlock()

	fields := logrus.Fields{
		logfields.PolicyKey: policyKey,
		fieldResult:         result,
	}

	// Log to standard logger
	entry := pal.logger.WithFields(fields)
	if result {
		entry.Debug("Policy evaluation completed: pass")
	} else {
		entry.Debug("Policy evaluation completed: fail")
	}

	// Log to audit logger if configured
	if pal.auditEntry != nil {
		pal.auditEntry.WithFields(fields).Info(logMsgEvaluation)
	}
}
8. EDIT /workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger.go
Before
// SetLogLevel updates the log level for both standard and audit loggers.
func (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) SetLogLevel(level logrus.Level) {
	pal.mutex.Lock()
	defer pal.mutex.Unlock()

	pal.logLevel = level
	if pal.auditLog != nil {
		pal.auditLog.SetLevel(level)
	}
}

// GetLogLevel returns the current log level.
func (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) GetLogLevel() logrus.Level {
	pal.mutex.Lock()
	defer pal.mutex.Unlock()
	return pal.logLevel
}
After
// SetLogLevel updates the log level for the audit logger if configured.
func (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) SetLogLevel(level logrus.Level) {
	pal.mutex.Lock()
	defer pal.mutex.Unlock()

	if pal.auditLog != nil {
		pal.auditLog.SetLevel(level)
	}
}

// GetLogLevel returns the current log level of the audit logger, or InfoLevel if not configured.
func (pal *PolicyAuditLogger) GetLogLevel() logrus.Level {
	pal.mutex.Lock()
	defer pal.mutex.Unlock()

	if pal.auditLog != nil {
		return pal.auditLog.Level
	}
	return logrus.InfoLevel
}

Before
// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
// Copyright Authors of Cilium

package policy

import (
	"bytes"
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"testing"

	"github.com/sirupsen/logrus"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"

	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity"
	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/labels"
)
After
// SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
// Copyright Authors of Cilium

package policy

import (
	"bytes"
	"context"
	"encoding/json"
	"strconv"
	"sync"
	"testing"

	"github.com/sirupsen/logrus"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"

	"github.com/cilium/cilium/pkg/identity"
)

// setupAuditLogger creates a new PolicyAuditLogger with a buffer for testing.
func setupAuditLogger(t *testing.T, level logrus.Level) (*bytes.Buffer, *PolicyAuditLogger) {
	buf := &bytes.Buffer{}
	logger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(level, buf)
	return buf, logger
}

// parseAuditLog parses a JSON audit log entry from the buffer output.
func parseAuditLog(t *testing.T, logOutput string) map[string]interface{} {
	var auditEntry map[string]interface{}
	err := json.Unmarshal(bytes.TrimSpace([]byte(logOutput)), &auditEntry)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	return auditEntry
}
10. EDIT /workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger_test.go
Before
func TestNewPolicyAuditLogger(t *testing.T) {
	// Test without audit output
	logger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.DebugLevel, nil)
	require.NotNil(t, logger)
	require.Equal(t, logrus.DebugLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())
	require.Nil(t, logger.auditLog)

	// Test with audit output
	buf := &bytes.Buffer{}
	logger = NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)
	require.NotNil(t, logger)
	require.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())
	require.NotNil(t, logger.auditLog)
}
After
func TestNewPolicyAuditLogger(t *testing.T) {
	// Test without audit output
	logger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.DebugLevel, nil)
	require.NotNil(t, logger)

	// Test with audit output
	buf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)
	require.NotNil(t, logger)
	require.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())

	// Verify output buffer is empty at start
	require.Equal(t, "", buf.String())
}
11. EDIT /workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger_test.go
Before
func TestLogDecision(t *testing.T) {
	buf := &bytes.Buffer{}
	logger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)

	testID := identity.NumericIdentity(1001)
	logger.LogDecision(testID, "test-policy", "allow", "matches selector")

	// Verify audit log contains the decision
	logOutput := buf.String()
	require.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)

	// Parse JSON to verify structure
	var auditEntry map[string]interface{}
	err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	require.Equal(t, "policy-decision", auditEntry["msg"])
	require.Equal(t, float64(1001), auditEntry["identity"])
	require.Equal(t, "test-policy", auditEntry["policy"])
	require.Equal(t, "allow", auditEntry["verdict"])
	require.Equal(t, "matches selector", auditEntry["reason"])
}
After
func TestLogDecision(t *testing.T) {
	buf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)

	testID := identity.NumericIdentity(1001)
	logger.LogDecision(testID, "test-policy", "allow", "matches selector")

	logOutput := buf.String()
	auditEntry := parseAuditLog(t, logOutput)

	require.Equal(t, "policy-decision", auditEntry["msg"])
	require.Equal(t, float64(1001), auditEntry["identity"])
	require.Equal(t, "test-policy", auditEntry["policy"])
	require.Equal(t, "allow", auditEntry["verdict"])
	require.Equal(t, "matches selector", auditEntry["reason"])
}

	var auditEntry map[string]interface{}

	require.Equal(t, "policy-decision", auditEntry["msg"])
	require.Equal(t, "deny", auditEntry["verdict"])
	require.Equal(t, "denied by rule", auditEntry["reason"])

After
func TestLogDecisionDeny(t *testing.T) {
	buf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)

	testID := identity.NumericIdentity(2002)
	logger.LogDecision(testID, "restrictive-policy", "deny", "denied by rule")

	logOutput := buf.String()
	auditEntry := parseAuditLog(t, logOutput)

	require.Equal(t, "policy-decision", auditEntry["msg"])
	require.Equal(t, float64(2002), auditEntry["identity"])
	require.Equal(t, "restrictive-policy", auditEntry["policy"])
	require.Equal(t, "deny", auditEntry["verdict"])
	require.Equal(t, "denied by rule", auditEntry["reason"])
}

func TestLogEvaluation(t *testing.T) {
	buf := &bytes.Buffer{}
	logger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)

	ctx := context.Background()
	policyKey := "test-policy-key"

	// Create a minimal SelectorCache for testing
	repo := NewPolicyRepository(nil, nil, nil)
	selectorCache := repo.GetSelectorCache()

	logger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, selectorCache, true)

	logOutput := buf.String()
	require.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)

	var auditEntry map[string]interface{}
	err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(logOutput[:len(logOutput)-1]), &auditEntry)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	require.Equal(t, "policy-evaluation", auditEntry["msg"])
	require.Equal(t, policyKey, auditEntry[logfields.PolicyKey])
	require.Equal(t, true, auditEntry["result"])
}
After
func TestLogEvaluation(t *testing.T) {
	buf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)

	ctx := context.Background()
	policyKey := "test-policy-key"

	logger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, nil, true)

	logOutput := buf.String()
	auditEntry := parseAuditLog(t, logOutput)

	require.Equal(t, "policy-evaluation", auditEntry["msg"])
	require.Equal(t, policyKey, auditEntry[logfields.PolicyKey])
	require.Equal(t, true, auditEntry["result"])
}

func TestLogLevelManagement(t *testing.T) {
	buf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)

	require.Equal(t, logrus.InfoLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())

	logger.SetLogLevel(logrus.DebugLevel)
	require.Equal(t, logrus.DebugLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())

	logger.SetLogLevel(logrus.WarnLevel)
	require.Equal(t, logrus.WarnLevel, logger.GetLogLevel())
}

17. EDIT /workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger_test.go
Before
func TestConcurrentLogging(t *testing.T) {
	buf := &bytes.Buffer{}
	logger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)

	// Test concurrent access to LogDecision
	done := make(chan bool)
	for i := 0; i < 10; i++ {
		go func(index int) {
			testID := identity.NumericIdentity(3000 + uint32(index))
			logger.LogDecision(testID, "concurrent-policy", "allow", "test")
			done <- true
		}(i)
	}

	for i := 0; i < 10; i++ {
		<-done
	}

	logOutput := buf.String()
	require.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)

	// Count the number of log entries
	lineCount := bytes.Count([]byte(logOutput), []byte("\n"))
	require.Equal(t, 10, lineCount)
}
After
func TestConcurrentLogging(t *testing.T) {
	buf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)

	var wg sync.WaitGroup
	for i := 0; i < 10; i++ {
		wg.Add(1)
		go func(index int) {
			defer wg.Done()
			testID := identity.NumericIdentity(3000 + uint32(index))
			logger.LogDecision(testID, "concurrent-policy", "allow", "test")
		}(i)
	}
	wg.Wait()

	logOutput := buf.String()
	lines := bytes.Split(bytes.TrimSpace([]byte(logOutput)), []byte("\n"))
	require.Equal(t, 10, len(lines))

	// Verify all entries are policy-decision messages
	for _, line := range lines {
		entry := parseAuditLog(t, string(line))
		require.Equal(t, "policy-decision", entry["msg"])
	}
}
18. EDIT /workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger_test.go
Before
func TestConcurrentEvaluation(t *testing.T) {
	buf := &bytes.Buffer{}
	logger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)

	repo := NewPolicyRepository(nil, nil, nil)
	selectorCache := repo.GetSelectorCache()
	ctx := context.Background()

	done := make(chan bool)
	for i := 0; i < 10; i++ {
		go func(index int) {
			policyKey := "policy-" + string(rune(index))
			logger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, selectorCache, index%2 == 0)
			done <- true
		}(i)
	}

	for i := 0; i < 10; i++ {
		<-done
	}

	logOutput := buf.String()
	require.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)

	// Count the number of log entries
	lineCount := bytes.Count([]byte(logOutput), []byte("\n"))
	require.Equal(t, 10, lineCount)
}
After
func TestConcurrentEvaluation(t *testing.T) {
	buf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)

	ctx := context.Background()

	var wg sync.WaitGroup
	for i := 0; i < 10; i++ {
		wg.Add(1)
		go func(index int) {
			defer wg.Done()
			policyKey := "policy-" + strconv.Itoa(index)
			logger.LogEvaluation(ctx, policyKey, nil, index%2 == 0)
		}(i)
	}
	wg.Wait()

	logOutput := buf.String()
	lines := bytes.Split(bytes.TrimSpace([]byte(logOutput)), []byte("\n"))
	require.Equal(t, 10, len(lines))

	// Verify all entries are policy-evaluation messages
	for i, line := range lines {
		entry := parseAuditLog(t, string(line))
		require.Equal(t, "policy-evaluation", entry["msg"])
		require.Equal(t, i%2 == 0, entry["result"])
	}
}
19. EDIT /workspace/pkg/policy/audit_logger_test.go
Before
func TestMultipleDecisionsWithDifferentVerdicts(t *testing.T) {
	buf := &bytes.Buffer{}
	logger := NewPolicyAuditLogger(logrus.InfoLevel, buf)

	verdicts := []string{"allow", "deny", "allow", "deny"}
	for i, verdict := range verdicts {
		testID := identity.NumericIdentity(5000 + uint32(i))
		logger.LogDecision(testID, "test-policy", verdict, "test reason")
	}

	logOutput := buf.String()
	require.NotEmpty(t, logOutput)

	lines := bytes.Split(bytes.TrimSpace([]byte(logOutput)), []byte("\n"))
	require.Equal(t, 4, len(lines))

	for i, verdict := range verdicts {
		var auditEntry map[string]interface{}
		err := json.Unmarshal(lines[i], &auditEntry)
		require.NoError(t, err)
		require.Equal(t, verdict, auditEntry["verdict"])
	}
}
After
func TestMultipleDecisionsWithDifferentVerdicts(t *testing.T) {
	buf, logger := setupAuditLogger(t, logrus.InfoLevel)

	verdicts := []string{"allow", "deny", "allow", "deny"}
	for i, verdict := range verdicts {
		testID := identity.NumericIdentity(5000 + uint32(i))
		logger.LogDecision(testID, "test-policy", verdict, "test reason")
	}

	logOutput := buf.String()
	lines := bytes.Split(bytes.TrimSpace([]byte(logOutput)), []byte("\n"))
	require.Equal(t, len(verdicts), len(lines))

	for i, verdict := range verdicts {
		auditEntry := parseAuditLog(t, string(lines[i]))
		require.Equal(t, verdict, auditEntry["verdict"])
		require.Equal(t, "test-policy", auditEntry["policy"])
	}
}
